Summary: Simulink.Timeseries objects are converted to doubles when using parfor
Description: When using Simulink.Timeseries objects in a parfor loop, they are converted to doubles. A typical situation in which this may occur is when Simulink.Timeseries objects and the models they are intended to be inputs for are inside a parfor loop. In this situation, the Simulink.Timeseries objects are converted to doubles.
Workaround: Starting in R1010b, you can use MATLAB timeseries objects instead of Simulink.Timeseries objects. Please see the MATLAB timeseries documenation for more information.Simulink.Timeseries objects are used when logging signals in ModelDataLogs format. The ModelDataLogs format is being phased out and replaced with Dataset format which uses MATLAB timeseries objects. Please refer the the section Migrate from ModelDataLogs to Dataset format in the documentation for more information.

Summary: datcomimport function has problems reading certain DATCOM files
Description: When the datcomimport  function reads a DATCOM file that has a message at the end of a run, and that message contains the string vehicle weight , the function will generate the following warning: Warning: DATCOM file reader state inconsistent
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file: /toolbox/aero/aero/private/usafdatcom.pTo see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB><.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: C++ functions generated as encapsulated class member functions might cause compiler errors
Description: The generated code might cause compilation errors, if You are generating code using C++ (Encapsulated)  as the code generation target language. Your model needs to generate utility functions from signal processing blocks, Stateflow charts, or MATLAB Function blocks.  Compilation errors occur when functions are incorrectly generated as encapsulated class member functions rather than as non-member functions with external linkage. One compiler error indicates that some utility functions are not C++ class member functions. In the generated code, the class definitions do not include the functions, but the function implementations are generated as if they are member functions. Another compiler error indicates that overloaded member functions with the same signature are present. In this case, the generated code includes overloaded functions with the same signature. The following example shows the code for the Random Source block in the DSP Toolbox: extern void RndTestModelClass::RandSrc_U_D(real_T y[], const real_T minVec[], int32_T minLen, const real_T maxVec[], int32_T maxLen, real_T state[], int32_T nChans, int32_T nSamps){ ...
Workaround: If the error occurs for functions generated for a Stateflow chart or a MATLAB function block:Right-click the chart or block and select Subsystem Parameters.On the Code Generation tab, set Function packaging to Reusable function.If the error occurs for functions generated for a signal processing block:Place the block inside a subsystem.Set the subsystem parameter Function packaging to Reusable function.?
Products: Embedded Coder, Embedded IDE Link, Target Support Package

Summary: The Model Advisor returns the error message Abnormal exit: Index exceeds matrix dimensions when running checks on Relational Operator blocks
Description: The Model Advisor MAAB check Check for proper position of constants used in Relational Operator blocks  (Modeling Guidelines jc_0131), DO-178B check Check for proper usage of Relational Operator blocks,  and IEC 61508 check Check usage of Simulink constructs  return the error message Abnormal exit: Index exceeds matrix dimensions  when any relational operator block in the model has only one input.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file, corresponding to your release, associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Code Reuse Subsystem between two nested For Each Subsystems might cause segment violation when generating code for a Simulink model?
Description: A segment violation might occur when generating code for a Simulink model under the following conditions: 1. One instance of a Code Reuse Subsystem contains an inner For Each Subsystem, and is inside another For Each Subsystem. 2. The type of the Code Reuse Subsystem is not For Each Subsystem. 3. The code generation option of the inner For Each Subsystem is "Reusable function" or "Auto". 4. There is another instance of the same Code Reuse Subsystem that is not inside any For Each Subsystem. ?
Workaround: Manually insert a Signal Conversion Block in front of the inner For Each Subsystem contained by the Code Reuse Subsystem.

Summary: Type mismatch error might occur if Embedded MATLAB Function block outputs have inherited sizes.
Description: If some outputs of an Embedded MATLAB Function block or Truth Table block have inherited size (-1), you might get a type mismatch error when preparing for model simulation (update diagram).
Workaround: Set output sizes explicitly in the Ports and Data manager.

Summary: Drag select and Drag resize can get stuck on in Windows 7 and Vista.
Description: When running Simulink on Windows 7 or Vista with the Aero desktop composition turned on, it's possible to get stuck in drag resize or drag select mode. When this happens, the resize or select rectangle continues to draw on the screen even after the mouse button is released.
Workaround: Turn of Windows Desktop Composition.1. Click on the Windows button on the task bar.2. Right-click 'Computer' (in the right-hand section of the menu) and select 'Properties'.3. On the left, select 'Advanced System Settings'.4. In the resulting dialog, select the 'Advanced' tab.5. Under 'Performance', click the 'Settings' button.6. Uncheck the box labeled 'Enable Desktop Composition'.

Summary: "Linux-ARM Code Generation for Texas Instruments OMAP35xx Processors" contains a link that works intermittently
Description: In the "Linux-ARM Code Generation for Texas Instruments OMAP35xx Processor" product help topic, the link entitled "The Angstrom Distribution" is malformed. Clicking this link displays "Warning - Documentation File Not Found" in the instead of displaying http://www.angstrom-distribution.org. After clicking the back arrow and returning to the "Linux-ARM Code Generation for Texas Instruments OMAP35xx Processor" topic, if you click the preceding "Getting Started" link, the product help can display another warning page.
Workaround: Do not click the "The Angstrom Distribution" link. Instead, open a browser and visit http://www.angstrom-distribution.org.If the "Getting Started" link does not work after clicking the "The Angstrom Distribution" link, close and reopen the product help before clicking the "Getting Started" link. Alternatively, open the "Getting Started" topic directly by entering the following command at the MATLAB command prompt: web([docroot '/toolbox/idelink/eclipse/ug/bsarany.html'])

Summary: sym causes a segmentation violation error when called with a cell array of empty strings.
Description: Calling the sym  function with a cell array containing an empty string causes a segmentation violation error with the following top two function calls on the stack trace:   [0] libmx.dll:_mxGetN(0, 1, 0x00c3a704, 0x0b47a538) + 8 bytes   [1] mupadmex.mexw32:unsigned int __cdecl compute_total_length(struct mxArray_tag const *,unsigned int)(100, 1, 0x00c3ada0, 1) + 48 bytes

Summary: Assertion when generating code from Stateflow chart under rare circumstances
Description: Under the following conditions, Stateflow fails to generate code and produces an assertion stack: A supertransition exits a state with parallel decomposition. Some (but not all) of the sibling states of the state from which the transition originates have an exit action. A certain internal inlining threshold matches up. This last factor is hard to predict accurately.  When those conditions are satisfied, Stateflow asserts with the following signature: ### Starting Real-Time Workshop build procedure for model: ###Model Name###failed assertion in .\cdr\cdr_analysis_dataflow.cpp at line 315:edgesRemaining>=1   The attached model, mg669448.mdl, satisfies all three conditions and exhibits this problem.
Workaround: A workaround is to set the Function Inline Option to Function for the state with parallel decomposition that is being exited as part of the supertransition. This workaround prevents certain internal inlining actions from happening and prevents the assertion.

Summary: FDATool errors when displaying the impulse response for filters with normalized frequency specifications
Description: Attempting to view the impulse response from the figure toolbar in FDATool results in the error "Matrix dimensions must agree". This error occurs when the Frequency Specifications Units  are set to Normalized (0 to 1) . ?
Workaround: You can view the impulse response from FDATool by selecting View -> Filter Visualization Tool.

Summary: On MacOS 10.6.4, MuPAD crashes when the Print dialog box is open.
Description: When the Print dialog box is open, MuPAD crashes on all actions except Cancel . For example, crash occurs when you try to Print Use the system's preview Use the system's Print to File  function  The crash does not occur on MacOS versions before 10.6.4.
Workaround: Export a MuPAD notebook to HTML, and then print the HTML file. Alternatively, use Print Preview to export notebooks and help pages to PDF. There is no workaround for MuPAD program (.mu) or graphic (.xvc and .xvz) documents.

Summary: On a Linux system, the license activation mechanism is called when you use the Automatic Orange Tester
Description: When you open the Automatic Orange Tester user interface, the license activation mechanism is called even when you have a valid license. This occurs only on Linux systems.
Workaround: To workaround this issue on a Linux system:Close all Polyspace user interfaces, including the Automatic Orange Tester, and and Polyspace Viewer.Create a backup copy of the file $POLYSPACE_COMMON/AutomaticOrangeTester/lib/x86-linux/libpst.soCopy the file: $POLYSPACE_COMMON/Viewer/lib/x86-linux/libpst.so to the folder:$POLYSPACE_COMMON/AutomaticOrangeTester/lib/x86-linux?

Summary: Invalid data port value at control port of Multiport Switch block causes MATLAB to close without errors when Block reduction and Inline parameters are both 'On'
Description: A model with a Multiport Switch block causes MATLAB to close without error messages when both of the following conditions are satisfied: During simulation, the control port receives an invalid value that does not correspond to any data port. Block reduction  and Inline parameters  are selected on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.
Workaround: Clear the Block reduction check box in the Configuration Parameters dialog box and make sure that the simulation is running without errors.  Then reselect the Block reduction optimization.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: incorrect initial output value in generated code for Merge or Concatenate block inside a For Each Subsystem
Description: If a Merge or Concatenate block is inside a For Each subsystem, Real Time Workshop generates code with an incorrect initial value for the output of the block when the following conditions are true, The Merge block parameter Initial output  is set to empty. The Concatenate block satisfies one of the following condition: Parameter Mode  is set to Vector  Parameter Mode  is set to Multidimensional array  and parameter Contenate dimension  is set to the highest dimension of the input signals.   A conditionally executed subsystem output connects directly to such a Merge or Concatenate block The corresponding Outport block of the conditionally executed subsystem has an initial condition value other than zero or empty.  The initial value for the output of a Merge or Concatenate block is set to zero which overwrites the non-zero initial value set by the conditionally executed subsystem.
Workaround: For a Merge block, set the initial condition for the output of the Merge block to the correct value.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: For some constant floating point index expressions in Stateflow charts
Description: In a Stateflow chart, if the index expression is a constant floating point type and the named or literal constant has an integer value, the generated code is incorrect. For example, the following expression in Stateflow y = u[2.0];  is translated into y = u[0];  If an index expression in Stateflow is an integer type, or floating point type with a non-integer value, for example,   y = u[2];  ory = u[2.1];  the generated code is correct  y = u[2];  For an example of this issue, see the attached model.
Workaround: Download the attached zip file and extract the P-code MATLAB script. Execute the MATLAB script with argument 0 to disable the optimization that is causing this issue. For example,>> setExtendedScalarizationOptimization(0)ans = 0disables the optimization and generates correct code for the attached model.

Summary: Segmentation fault for simulation of model with Switch block
Description: Simulating a model in Rapid Accelerator mode might cause a segmentation fault if the model contains a Switch block and all of the following conditions are true: The Switch block parameter Enable zero-crossing detection  is selected. The second input of the Switch block is a scalar. The first or third input of the Switch block is a matrix.
Products: Simulink Coder, Stateflow Coder, Embedded IDE Link, Target Support Package

Summary: Generated code for model with implicit iterator subsystem might not compile
Description: The generated C/C++ code for a model might not compile under the following conditions: Model contains an implicit iterator subsystem, such as the For Each Subsystem block. The model configuration parameter Maximum stack size (bytes)  is set to a small enough value such that it no longer creates a stack variable for the implicit iterator subsystem index.
Workaround: Increase the Maximum stack size (bytes) value on the Optimization > Signals and Parameters pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: A Concatenate or Merge block with unconnected input or input that is driven directly by a Ground block may generate code with an uninitialized local variable.
Description: This problem is related to three optimizations in Simulink and Real-Time Workshop, Simulink assumes there is no work to do when an input port of a block is unconnected or driven by a Ground block. Real-Time Workshop minimizes global memory usage by making block outputs local variables. The Concatenate or Merge block attempts to perform in-place computation where the output signals of the source blocks are written directly to the Concatenate or Merge block output.  The combination of the above optimizations leads to generated code where the Concatenate or Merge block output is a local variable and the elements corresponding to the grounded input are never assigned an intial value. If the uninitialized elements do not have a zero value at run-time, the generated code may produce a wrong answer. A Merge block always perform in-place computation. A Concatentate block may perform in-place computation when all of the following conditions are true, The output signal dimensions do not change during model execution. All input signals are either real or complex.  and one of the following conditions is true, The parameter Number of inputs  is set to 1 . The parameter Mode  is set to Vector  or the parameter Concatenate dimension  is set to the highest dimension of all input signals when the parameter Mode  is set to Multidimensional array .
Workaround: To avoid this problem, do not leave an input port of a Concatenate or Merge block unconnected or grounded. Use a Constant block with a zero value instead. This may produce a global variable for the Concatenate or Merge block output. To facilitate the local variable optimization, set the sample time of the Constant block to an explicit value other than inf or -1.

Summary: Models that contain National Instruments 6601 and 6602 pulse width measurement and pulse generation blocks do not work appropriately when built with Open Watcom compilers
Description: Models that contain National Instruments 6601 and 6602 pulse width measurement and pulse generation blocks do not work appropriately when built with Open Watcom compilers. These models build correctly when you compile them with Microsoft compilers.
Workaround: As a workaround, for xPC Target V4.3 (R2010a):Log into your computer using an account with Administrator privileges.Download the workaround files associated with this Bug Report.Locate the download - this directory is referred to as <DOWNLOAD> in subsequent steps.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt - this directory is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Open the downloaded file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Restart MATLAB.Rebuild your model.July 20, 2010: 2010-07-19-attachment-657992-10a.zip: Fixes the linking error with Watcom. This only affects R2010a.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Fixed-point division operation with exact power of two denominator constant might produce incorrect results in generated code.
Description: In some models with fixed-point division operations, the Simulink Fixed Point software might generate code that produces the wrong numerical result. The following conditions are all necessary: The hardware embedded target implements signed division as round to zero. The division is fixed point. The division operation rounds to 'Zero' or 'Simplest'. The division has a signed numerator with an unsigned denominator. The denominator is constant. The constant value of the denominator is an exact power of two.  While necessary, these conditions are not sufficient to cause incorrect generated code. Incorrect code is generated in rare cases depending on model contents and configuration. Detection and Verification  The recommended way to detect if this bug affects your model is to compare numerical simulation results from your model against the numerical code generation results with input that known to exercise the model. To do this: Simulate your model and store the output results. Generate code for your model and run the generated code. Compare the two results.  If the results are the same, your model is not affected by this bug. Detecting differences between the results should not be difficult, as the code generation results will be different than the simulation results by a power of 2 each time a new calculation is executed, except in the trivial case where the numerator is 0. If there are differences between the numerical results of simulation and code generation, the attached MATLAB script 'detectPossibleBadDivisions' may be able to detect the Simulink Divide blocks that generate the incorrect code. NOTE: This script cannot detect divisions that come from Embedded MATLAB Function blocks or Stateflow charts, and is only meant as a diagnostic tool. This script requires a Simulink Fixed Point license.  To use this script: Download files 'detectPossibleBadDivisions.m' and 'detectPossibleBadDivisions.p' into your current working directory, or a directory on the MATLAB path. At the MATLAB Command Prompt, enter the following command with a valid Simulink model name:  [blocksWithTypesAndPow2Const blocksWithTypesAndUndeterminedConst blocksWithTypes] =  detectPossibleBadDivisions(model)  This script will look through the input model and return three variables: 'blocksWithTypesAndPow2Const' contains blocks that meet all six of the above necessary criteria and are most prone to trigger this bug. 'blocksWithTypesAndUndeterminedConst' contains blocks that meet necessary criteria 1-5, but for these blocks the script cannot determine the constant value input to the denominator. You may wish to verify this value is not a power of two by using, for example, a Simulink Display block while simulating your model. 'blocksWithTypes' contains blocks that meet criteria 1-4, but no other useful information could be determined about the denominator. These blocks are not likely to trigger this bug, but under rare circumstances could have a constant denominator value. For example, if a Constant block with value 4 feeds a Gain block with value 4, these values might be expression folded into a constant value of 16 within the generated code.
Workaround: In order to workaround this issue, change the type of the denominator input to a signed type by either changing the output type of the upstream block or inserting a Data Type Conversion block.

Summary: Missing mask prompts and values when strings end with backslashes or some Japanese characters
Description: If a mask value or mask prompt ends with a backslash character ("\"), or a Japanese character whose Shift-JIS encoding uses a backslash character as its second byte, Simulink cannot store the mask correctly. When you load the model, the mask values and prompts will be missing from the block and you see a warning: ??? Invalid setting in masked block 'mymodel/My Masked Block' for parameter 'MaskValueString'.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, make sure you include an ASCII character other than a backslash at the end of each prompt and value string. For example, you can add a space character at the end of each prompt or value.

Summary: On a 64-bit Linux machine, pressing any arrow key (up, down, left, right) during the execution of the pause command causes MATLAB to crash with a MATLAB system error.
Description: If any arrow key is pressed during the execution of the pause command will cause MATLAB to crash.  For example, if you have a simple code segment as follows: for t=1:2,pause,end and then press any arrow key while it is run, MATLAB will crash with an internal error.
Workaround: There is no work around for this issue when using the pause command.If you are using pause to wait for a button or key press while using Handle Graphics based programs you may try waitforbuttonpress.  When an arrow key is pressed in this case MATLAB will not crash.

Summary: ServerComSpec definition missing in XML description files generated from server operation model
Description: AUTOSAR RTE generators require the ServerComSpec definition for a server operation runnable. However, the software does not generate this information from a server operation model.
Workaround: Either load the XML description files into an AUTOSAR authoring tool before running the RTE generator, or export the description from the authoring tool. The authoring tool will automatically configure the correct ServerComSpec.

Summary: Dead Zone block fails to report signal overflows even if the Configuration Parameter Detect overflow is set to warning.
Description: When the Dead Zone block parameter Saturate on integer overflow  is not selected, and the Configuraion Parameter Detect overflow  on the Data Validity pane is set to warning , the block does not report a warning even when the input signal overflows.

Summary: The Automatice Orange Tester incorrectly reports a red NIV check for structure fields when the depth of the structure is greater than 1.
Description: On the following code, the Automatice Orange Tester will not intialize the 2nd depth level of the structure Mystruct.  This leads to a red NIV error. typedef struct {  int bin1;  int bin2;} dep1;  typedef struct {  dep1 array[2]; } Mystruct;  extern void callstub(Mystruct *);  int main(void){  int a, b;  Mystruct toto;    callstub(&toto);  a = toto.array[0].bin1; // AOT red error on bin1  b = toto.array[0].bin2;  return (a + b);}   ? ?
Workaround: A patch is available to address this issue for the R2009a, R2009b and R2010a releases.You must install this patch in the location where the AOT is executed.  This is usually on the client side. Instructions can be found in the README.TXT.

Summary: Function maptrims gives incorrect results when combining the cellDensity option with an input having unequal sampling in latitude and longitude
Description: Beginning with R2009b, the maptrims  function accepts a referencing matrix to relate the columns and rows of a regularly sampled grid to latitude and longitude. Unlike the older referencing vectors, a referencing matrix can support a raster grid that is sampled at different densities in latitude versus longitude. (Away from the Equator, one might compensate for the convergence of the meridians by sampling less densely in longitude.) When such a data set is input and, in addition to being trimmed, a different cell density is requested for the output, then both the data grid and the referencing matrix returned by maptrims  are incorrect.
Workaround: To work around this problem, apply the workaround provided with Bug Report 610010. If you apply that workaround now (or you've applied it since July 16, 2010), you will fix this problem as well and no further action will be needed.

Summary: PolySpace Queue Manager hangs when using "Follow Progress" on a running analysis.
Description: When you select "Follow Progress" in the PolySpace Queue Manager to monitor a running verification, the Polyspace Queue Manager (Spooler) and the "PolySpace follow remote code verificatio progress" window display the message "IO error in connection ." After the error message is displayed, the Queue Manager does not respond, even if it appears to be working properly.
Workaround: This worakround can only be applied on Polyspace Server side for QM and CPUs. It includes a workaround of the issue described in bug report 625512.To workaround this issue on a Windows system:Stop the Queue Manager service on the CPU using the following command: %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\RemoteLauncher\PSTRLManager.exeNavigate to the following folder: %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\RemoteLauncher\bin\x86-mingw32Create a backup copy of the following file: polyspaced-RL-R2010a-V12.cx86-mingw32.exeDownload the attached file: 656283_Windows.zip.Extract the file to the folder specified in step 2,Restart the Queue Manager service using the command specified in step 1.Repeat all steps for each CPU, including the Queue Manager server. Note: You must update the Queue Manager server last.To workaround this issue on a Linux system:Stop the Queue Manager service on the CPU using the following command: /etc/init.d/polsypaced stopNavigate to the followiong folder: $POLYSPACE_COMMON/RemoteLauncher/bin/x86-linuxCreate a backup copy of the following file:polyspaced-RL-R2010a-V12.cx86-linuxDownload the attached file: 656283_Linux.zip.Extract the file to the folder specified in step 2.Restart the Queue Manager service using the following command: /etc/init.d/polyspaced startRepeat all steps for each CPU, including the Queue Manager server. Note: You must update the Queue Manager server last.

Summary: Incorrect answer from generated code of S-function block output type
Description: You might produce an incorrect numerical answer from the generated code of an S-function block output with multiple outputs, if you use one of the following: A Probe block on the S-function block output type An S-function that takes the data type and uses it as an integer value.

Summary: Failure to match block parameters
Description: For some blocks, the XML comparison algorithm reports all parameters as unmatched, even though the blocks are unmodified. These results can be confusing. For example, this problem occurs with blocks from the third party blockset "Xilinx System Generator for DSP". The algorithm cannot match the block parameters and displays them as unmatched in the resulting comparison report.This problem occurs because the XML files exported from Simulink models for the Xilinx blocks were missing certain tags. This causes the pattern matching algorithm to treat them as different and report all the parameters as unmatched.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog into your computer using an account with Administrator privilegesDownload the workaround files associated with this Bug ReportLocate the download - this directory is referred to as <DOWNLOAD> in subsequent stepsLocate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt - this directory is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent stepsExit MATLABFollow additional steps, depending on your operating systemOn Windows:Open the downloaded fileExtract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> directoryOn Linux and Macintosh:Type the following in a shell (on Linux) or a Terminal (on Macintosh) window:cd <MATLAB>unzip <DOWNLOAD>/<downloaded file>

Summary: Polyspace verification stops with the following message: 'Limitation: Beginning then end of critical section must be in same block : myfunc'
Description: When launching a verification, Polyspace stops with a message 'Limitation: Beginning then end of critical section must be in same block : myfunc '. This is an incorrect message that occurs when functions given as begin/end of critical sections have some parameters by reference. In the following example, using the options -critical-section-begin csenter:cs, -critical-section-end csexit:cs and -entry-points t1,t2 , Polyspace stops with this message on the following code function myfunc(): void csenter(void *x) {}void csexit(void *x) {}void myfunc(void){  int var = 1;  csenter(&var);  csexit(&var);}void main(void) {}void t1(void) { myfunc(); }void t2(void) { myfunc(); }  Note:  Polyspace accepts only 'void <anyname>(void)' as prototype function for critical sections. ?

Summary: Adding an integrator to the compensator in SISO Design Tool generates an error
Description: In the SISO Design Tool, adding an integrator to the compensator might generate an error. The error occurs when your plant is a FRD (Frequency Response Data) model that has data at zero frequency. The error includes the following: ??? Error using ==> timesMatrix dimensions must agree.  Error in ==> sisogui.bodeditorOL.update at 73  this.Magnitude = TFMagnitude.*FRDMagnitude;  Error in ==> sisogui.grapheditor.addlisteners>LocalDataChanged at 86  Editor.update;   This error occurs when you add the integrator in the Bode or Nichols editor using the right-click menu. It also occurs when you add the integrator in the Compensator Editor tab of the SISO Design Task.
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can remove the zero-frequency value in the FRD model before using the model in the SISO Design Tool.For example, use the fselect command to remove the zero-frequency data:sys = frd(tf(1,[1,1]),0:10); % frd model with a zero-frequency data pointnewsys = fselect(sys,2:length(sys.Frequency))This command removes the first data point in the FRD model, creating a new model without the zero-frequency value. Launch the SISO Design Tool using the new model:sisotool({'bode'},newsys)You can now add an integrator to the compensator.?

Summary: Missing red overflow on a fixed point variable for the unary minus operator
Description: Verification results miss a red OVFL check on a fixed point variable for the unary minus operator. procedure Reproduction is  type D is delta 3.0517578125E-5 range -1.0 .. 1.0; for D'small use 3.0517578125E-5; for D'size use 16; V, W : D; begin V := -1.0;  W := (-V); -- overflow on assignment to W - but no Red end Reproduction;
Workaround: A patch is available for this issue. However, since the patch is large, it cannot be placed on the MathWorks website. If you are interested in receiving this patch, please contact MathWorks technical support.  We will send you instructions to download the patch from our FTP site.?

Summary: S-function macro ssGetDestFCSSEnabled within an S-function can cause a segmentation violation during code generation
Description: Using the S-function macro ssGetDestFCSSEnabled  within an S-function can cause a segmentation violation during code generation with Real-Time Workshop. If an S-function initiates a function-call to check the enabled status of the corresponding function-call subsystem, a segmentation violation always occurs when the function-call subsystem is invoked exclusively by the initiator.
Workaround: There is no known workaround for this issue. Avoid using the macro ssGetDestFCSSEnabled with the circumstances describe above.

Summary: wavread returns audio data from 32-bit integer WAVE (.wav) files with nonnormalized amplitude
Description: wavread  returns audio data that is not normalized between -1 and 1 for some 32-bit integer WAVE files.  For example, running the following code:y = wavread('my32bitfile.wav');plot(y); sometimes returns values in y  that are larger than 1 or smaller than -1.
Workaround: Follow these steps to replace the file wavread.m.     Find the matlabroot directory, where matlabroot is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, by typing the following command at the MATLAB prompt:       matlabroot        Quit MATLAB.    Find the file       matlabroot\toolbox\matlab\audiovideo\wavread.m    and rename this file to wavread.m.old    Download the file attached to this report. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 3.    Restart MATLAB.    Issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt:    rehash toolboxcache

Summary: stepwisefit errors when there is just one row of data.
Description: If there is just one row of data (the X  argument, predictors), or if there are multiple rows but just one that is complete (no NaN values), the stepwisefit  function errors.
Workaround: It does not make sense to perform stepwise regression with just a single row of data. But if you are running stepwisefit in a program and need to guard against this error, edit the stepwisefit function, and change the line   sx = std(allx);to   sx = std(allx,0,1);

Summary: Integrating MCR merge module into product using Visual Studio and WIX 3.5 for Visual Studio causes ICE18 Error
Description: Attempting to integrate the MATLAB Compiler Runtime (MCR) merge module (MATLAB_Compiler_Runtime32.msm ) into a product using Visual Studio and WIX 3.5 for Visual Studio causes an ICE18 Error: light.exe(0,0): error LGHT0204: ICE18: KeyPath for Component: 'SetCTFXFileAssoc_32.A7E30F4D_4EB0_44AB_A0C3_6A8E547047A2' is Directory: 'INSTALLLOCATION.A7E30F4D_4EB0_44AB_A0C3_6A8E547047A2'. The Directory/Component pair must be listed in the CreateFolders table. The merge module is missing an empty folder for the .ctfx  file association.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, edit the MCR merge module, adding an empty folder for the .ctfx file association. These instructions tell how to edit the merge module using the Orca database editor.Open the MATLAB Compiler Runtime merge module, MATLAB_Compiler_Runtime32.msm, in Orca.Orca displays a list of all of the tables in the merge module.Add a new CreateFolder table to the merge module. Click the Tables menu and select Add Table.In the Add Tables dialog, select the CreateFolder checkbox and click OK. Orca creates the CreateFolder table in the merge module and highlights the new table in the list.With the CreateFolder table highlighted, click the Tables menu and select Add Row.In the Add Row dialog box, enter the component and directory information requested. You can copy this information from this EBR, or you can copy it from the Components table in the merge module. See below for more information.With the _Validation table highlighted, click the Tables menu and select Add Row.In the add row dialog box, enter values for the Component_ and Directory_ entries. See below for the values.Save the changes and use with Visual studio to build the installer.Values to use for the CreateFolder row:Directory_: INSTALLLOCATION.A7E30F4D_4EB0_44AB_A0C3_6A8E547047A2 Component_: SetCTFXFileAssoc_32.A7E30F4D_4EB0_44AB_A0C3_6A8E547047A2You can also find these values in the Components table of the merge module. Select the Components table and find the row containing the ctfx file association. Copy the information in the Component and Directory columns into a temporary Notepad file so that you can paste them when requested by the Add Row dialog box.Values to use for the _Validation table rows:Table: CreateFolderColumn: Component_Nullable: NMinValue:MaxValue:KeyTable: ComponentKeyColumn: 1Category: IdentifierSet:Description: Foreign key into the Component table.Table: CreateFolderColumn: Directory_Nullable: NMinValue:MaxValue:KeyTable: DirectoryKeyColumn: 1Category: IdentifierSet:Description: Primary key, could be foreign key into the Directory table.?

Summary: fsolve prints message "Unrecognized exitflag input to createExitMsg."
Description: When the initial point given to fsolve  is stationary, meaning the gradient of the sum of squared residuals is near zero, but the point is not a root of the equations, fsolve  prints the message: "Unrecognized exitflag input to createExitMsg." The message is a reporting issue with fsolve , and does not indicate that fsolve  is in an error condition. The exit flag correctly indicates that fsolve  did not converge to a root of the equations.
Workaround: Ignore the "Unrecognized exitflag" message, and use the returned exit flag to determine the quality of the solution.

Summary: classregtree/test can error or produce incorrect results for some classification labels.
Description: The order of classes in the response vector Y passed to classregtree/test  can differ from the order of classes in the response vector Y used for training the tree. If the order differs, test  either errors or returns an incorrect result. If the order differs and there are only two classes in the trained tree, test  always errors.
Workaround: Replace the test.m file in the MATLAB folder toolbox/stats/stats/@classregtree with the attached test.m file.

Summary: lillietest and jbtest can return p-values that are inconsistent with their other outputs
Description: When you call the lillietest  or jbtest  functions with a desired significance level that is very close to the observed p-value, it is possible for the first output from those functions to be inconsistent with the second. For example, >> x = [0.431 0.911 0.182 0.264 0.145 0.136 0.869 0.580 0.550 0.145];>> [h,p,stat,cv] = lillietest(x,0.30)h = 0p = 0.2977stat = 0.2013cv = 0.2022 Note that the test statistic (stat ) is slightly less than the critical value used for the test (cv ), and therefore h  is 0 (fail to reject the null hypothesis). However, the p-value (p ) for these data is just slightly smaller than the specified significance level (0.30), which should lead to rejecting the null hypothesis.
Workaround: For consistency, you can compute h based on comparing the p-value and the significance level. For example,[~,p] = lillietest(x,alpha);h = (p < alpha);lillietest and jbtest both compute h internally as (cv < stat). However, because there is no closed form for the distribution of the test statistic in either case, both functions use approximate critical values for the test, and both compute approximate p-values. So either method of computing h is only approximate. Therefore, when p and alpha (or equivalently, cv and stat) are very close, be careful when making a hard decision to reject (or fail to reject) the null hypothesis.

Summary: PolySpace verification fails during compilation phase with the error message: "An unexpected error (148) occured in stubsuif.x86-mingw32.exe"
Description: When verifiying C code, the verification stops during compilation with the following message: "An unexpected error (148) occured in stubsuif.x86-mingw32.exe" .  This generally happens when you use the option -data-range-specifications , and your DRS file contains some void *  arguments to a function. ?

Description: When you try to simulate a model that contains Simulink.Parameter as parameters on Simscape blocks, it issues the following error:  Conversion to double from Simulink.Parameter is not possible.

Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Model fails to compile for charts with more than 256 states at a single level of the hierarchy
Description: If a Stateflow chart contains more than 256 sibling states at a single level of the hierarchy, the generated code for the simulation target fails to compile with the following error message: Error :   duplicate case label '1'  Several such error messages might appear during compilation of the generated code.
Workaround: This issue arises only when the number of child states in a given state is greater than 256.To work around this issue, surround these substates with dummy superstates such that the size of a single group is always less than 256. Empty parent superstates do not change chart semantics.

Summary: Computer reboots unexpectedly or stops responding when a Real-Time Windows Target model accesses the Vector CAN device driver
Description: When using a Real-Time Windows Target model that access a Vector CAN device driver with multiple CAN messages, the computer might do one of the following: Randomly and unexpectedly reboot Stop responding and display a blue screen
Workaround: As a workaround:Log into your computer using an account with Administrator privileges.Look at the list of available workaround files. These files are named according to the release they support.Select and download the appropriate workaround file for your release.Locate the downloaded file - this directory is referred to as <DOWNLOAD> in subsequent steps.Type matlabroot to locate your MATLAB installation - this directory is referred to as <MATLABROOT> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Navigate to <DOWNLOAD> and open the downloaded file.Extract the contents of the file to <MATLABROOT>.Restart MATLAB.September 15, 2010: attachment_652152_9a_win32_2010-09-15.zip: initial version, works on R2009a onlySeptember 15, 2010: attachment_652152_9b_win32_2010-09-15.zip: initial version, works on R2009b onlySeptember 15, 2010: attachment_652152_10a_win32_2010-09-15.zip: initial version, works on R2010a only?

Summary: Segmentation fault for Embedded MATLAB expressions
Description: If you use function calls as array indices in Embedded MATLAB programs or Embedded MATLAB blocks, a segmentation fault might occur in Real-Time Workshop, Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder, or when calling emlmex .

Summary: Linearization, operating point searches, and frequency response estimation produce errors for models with referenced configuration set
Description: Linearizing, computing an operating point, or frequency response estimation can result in an error for a model having a referenced configuration set.
Workaround: For the R2010a release of MATLAB, download and install the two attached files. To do this:Find the $MATLABROOT directory by entering the command matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.Exit MATLAB.Rename the file $MATLABROOT\toolbox\shared\slcontrollib\@slcontrol\@ModelParameterMgr\restoreModel.p to restoreModel.pbck and replace with the attached file restoreModel.p.Rename the file $MATLABROOT \toolbox\shared\slcontrollib\@slcontrol\@Utilities\setNonSimWarningOff.m to setNonSimWarningOff.mbck and replace with the attached file setNonSimWarningOff.m.Restart MATLAB.Enter the following command at the MATLAB command prompt:rehash toolboxcacheFor earlier releases of MATLAB make sure your model's configuration set objects are Simulink.ConfigSet objects, and not Simulink.ConfigSetRef objects.?

Summary: Code generation failure when tunable, 1-dimensional parameter is referenced with multiple indices
Description: If a block references a tunable, 1-dimensional parameter (e.g. whose value has dimensions 1xN  or Nx1 ) and you select an element of the parameter with multiple indices, then code generation fails. For example, if P = [4 7 9]  and you reference the second element as P(1, 2) , then code generation fails. The code generation failure might be one of the following: An assertion similar to the following:  CGIR assertion in rtwcg_utils/rtwcg_emlapi.cpp(62): 'Index expression out of bounds. Attempted to access element 2. The valid range is 1-1.' A TLC failure similar to the following:  Error: File: /home/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/lib/paramlib.tlc Line: 244 Column: 91Undefined identifier sigIdxIndexError: File: /home/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/lib/paramlib.tlc Line: 280 Column: 92Undefined identifier sigIdxError: File: /home/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/lib/paramlib.tlc Line: 364 Column: 70Undefined identifier sigIdxError: File: /home/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/lib/paramlib.tlc Line: 535 Column: 39Undefined identifier sigIdx
Workaround: If a block references a tunable, 1-dimensional parameter, then select an element of the parameter using one index. For example, if P = [4 7 9], then access the second element as P(2).

Summary: Error loading models that call function "c2d" from their PreLoadFcn when using "load_system" or "rtwbuild"
Description: When using "load_system" or "rtwbuild" to load a model which calls "c2d" from its PreLoadFcn, Simulink calls the wrong function, causing an error like the following. >> load_system('mymodel') Warning: mymodel.mdl, line 19: Error evaluating 'PreLoadFcn' callback of block_diagram 'mymodel'.Invalid syntax. The first input argument of the "tf" command cannot be a string. This error occurs because there are two version of function "c2d". One is part of the Control System Toolbox. The other is a private function in Simulink, which should not be available to callbacks in a model. No error occurs when loading the model by calling its name directly: >> mymodel
Workaround: To avoid this problem with "load_system", load the model by calling its name directly instead:>> mymodelWhen you use this method instead of using "load_system", Simulink calls the correct version of function "c2d".To avoid this problem with "rtwbuild", load the model before calling "rtwbuild". When the model is already loaded, "rtwbuild" does not attempt to load the model again, so no errors occur.>> mymodel>> rtwbuild('mymodel')

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Calling Simulink function in Stateflow chart with inputs or return values of bus type might yield incorrect simulation results
Description: If a Simulink function in a Stateflow chart accepts or returns a bus signal, calling this function might yield incorrect simulation results.
Workaround: Avoid passing bus signals in or out of a Simulink function in a Stateflow chart.

Summary: wavread and wavfinfo fail to read wave (.wav) files created on some hardware field recorders
Description: Using wavread  or wavfinfo  to read files created from some field recorders causes the following error:>> [y,fs] = wavread('myfile.wav'); ??? Error using ==> wavread at 166 Invalid field name: ''.
Workaround: Follow these steps to replace the file wavread.m.     Find the matlabroot directory, where matlabroot is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, by typing the following command at the MATLAB prompt:       matlabroot        Quit MATLAB. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 3.    Restart MATLAB.    Issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt:    rehash toolboxcache

Summary: When running models with external interrupts, floating point calculations might show glitches
Description: When running models with external interrupts, floating point calculations might show glitches. These glitches might appear on target scopes.  Models running with timer interrupts (default) work correctly.
Workaround: If you are running models with external interrupts, contact MathWorks Technical Support for further instructions.

Summary: Publishing: Unexpected content in published document
Description: Publishing: If you publish MATLAB? code that loops over cell breaks more than 99999 times, the published document might include unexpected content.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when typing an accented character or arrow key during a pause on MAC OS X.
Description: MATLAB crashes when typing an accented character or arrow key during a pause on MAC OS X. For example, typing an accented character or arrow key while the following commands execute can crash your MATLAB process:   for k=1:10     pause;   end

Summary: The Steady-State Voltages and Currents and Impedance vs Frequency Measurement tools of Powergui do not work when opened from a new Powergui block added to a model
Description: If you create a new SimPowerSystems model, where you add a new Powergui block, then open the Powergui, and select Steady-State Voltages and Currents , you get an error similar to the following: Warning: Error: Expression or statement is incomplete or incorrect. > In C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2010a\toolbox\physmod\powersys\powersys\powergui.p>powergui at 87 If you select Impedance vs Frequency Measurement , you get an error similar to the following: Error evaluating the frequency range expression. Undefined function or variable :.
Workaround: To fix this issue, at the MATLAB command prompt, type the following commands:open_system('powerlib') set_param('powerlib','lock','off') set_param('powerlib/powergui','RmsSteady','1') set_param('powerlib/powergui','Frange','logspace(0,3,50)')save_system('powerlib') close_system('powerlib')

Summary: Polyspace for C++ main generator does not call private virtual methods
Description: When using the Polyspace main generator for C++ code, private virtual methods are systematically stubbed when you set the options -unit-by-unit  or -class-analyzer all , even if you have defined methods. For example, in the following code, when using the option -class-analyzer all , the method funcg()  of class MyClass  is stubbed and appears as gray code in the verification results: class MyClass {private:  virtual int funcg(){return 1;}public:  void funcf() { int x = funcg();}};  ?
Workaround: Setting the option -class-analyzer MyClass, works around the problem.

Summary: Using global data stores in a referenced model may cause an inappropriate error during Real-Time Workshop code generation
Description: During Real-Time Workshop code generation, you may get the following error even if you have defined the correct Simulink.Signal  object in the base workspace: Data store memory with name 'DSM' was not found.  You may get this error when generating Real-Time Workshop code for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains at least three levels of model hierarchy. The lowest level model in the hierarchy is referenced in Accelerator mode. The lowest level model uses a global data store. All of the models above the lowest level are referenced in Normal mode.
Workaround: Set the simulation mode to Normal or Accelerator for all of the referenced models in the branch of the hierarchy that uses the global data store.

Summary: Import Wizard fails to report an error when unable to import files larger than 1 GB
Description: When you use the Import Wizard to import a file larger than 1 GB, and the import fails, the Import Wizard does not report an error.  The Import Wizard disables all the buttons except Cancel .

Summary: Errors when using the 'clabels' and 'cpatches' options with the handlem function
Description: The documentation for the handlem  function states that the input string 'clabels'  can be used to obtain handles for contour labels on the current map axes, but when used it results in an error: Unrecognized object name: clabels . Use of the input string cpatches , which is supposed to find filled contour patches on the current map axes, results in a similar error.
Workaround: Instead of 'clabels', you can use the string 'clabel'. (As intended, this will find labels created with the Mapping Toolbox clabelm function but not the MATLAB clabel function.) Instead of 'cpatches', you can use the string 'Cpatches', working around an unintended case sensitivity.

Summary: For very large models, simulation process can stall or crash MATLAB
Description: For very large models, the simulation process can stall or crash MATLAB.

Summary: Simulink stops executing unexpectedly when the diagnostic Detect multiple driving blocks executing at the same time step for Merge block is enabled.
Description: Simulink may stop executing unexpectedly during simulation if your model meets the following conditions: The run-time diagnostic Detect multiple driving blocks executing at the same time step  under Merge Block in the Data Validity pane of the Configurations Parameters dialog is set to warning  or error . The model contains a Merge block that is driven directly by an Inport block of a nonvirtual subsystem.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, do one of the following:Turn off the diagnostic by setting Detect multiple driving blocks executing at the same time step to none.Modify your model so that the Merge block in question is not driven directly by an Inport block. For example, move the Merge block out of the subsystem and merge the signals before sending the result into the subsystem.

Summary: MATLAB terminates unexpectely when using the functions GETGENBANK, GENBANKREAD, GETGENPEPT, and GENPEPTREAD.
Description: MATLAB terminates unexpectely when using the functions GETGENBANK , GENBANKREAD , GETGENPEPT , and GENPEPTREAD . This is due to a bug in the built-in function EXIST , which does not accept large char arrays.
Workaround: Download the attached files.Quit MATLAB.Rename the files $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\genbankread.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\genbankread.m.old and $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\genpeptread.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\genpeptread.m.old, where $MATLAB$ is the directory where MATLAB is installed on your machine.Copy the downloaded files to the directory $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfoRestart MATLAB.?

Summary: Incorrect warning for Model Advisor MAAB check Check for exclusive states, default states and substate validity (db_0137)
Description: The Model Advisor MAAB check Check for exclusive states, default states and substate validity  (db_0137) incorrectly reports an error when the Stateflow chart is a flow graph. The MAAB rule (db_0137) only applies to finite state machines, for example, Stateflow charts that contain multiple states. The rule should not apply to flow graphs.
Workaround: If you are running this check as part of a formal process, violations of this class should reference the bug report number BR-646298.

Summary: Wrong output and warning when executing latex command on symbolic expressions containing the erf function.
Description: When the expression contains the erf function, the latex  command gives a warning and return a wrong result: >> syms x;>> y = int(exp(-x^2))y =(pi^(1/2)*erf(x))/2>> latex(y) Warning: Invalid escape sequence appears in format string. See help sprintf for valid escape sequences. > In sym.latex at 34 ans = \frac{\sqrt{\pi}\, \mathop{\mathrm{erf}}
Workaround: Replace the file toolbox/symbolic/@sym/latex.m (or toolbox/symbolic/symbolic/@sym/latex.m, depending on the release version) with the attached file.

Summary: Software displays an "assertion failed" message when you modify some integral values under the Peripherals tab of the Target Preferences block.
Description: Software displays an "assertion failed" message when you modify some integral values under the Peripherals tab of the Target Preferences block. The message states: "Error: Error invoking object method". The Target Preferences block displays a Peripherals tab for C2000 processors. The Peripherals tab contains several panes that display integral values. For example, the eCAN_A and eCAN_B pane displays a "Baud rate prescaler". If you try to modify the value shown in these widgets, the software displays the "assertion failed" message and the value remains unchaged.
Workaround: The attached patch is for the R2010a release.To install the patch:Download the attached files, patch_installer.m and patch_645792.zip.Start MATLAB and enter the following command at the prompt: patch_installer('patch_645792.zip', 'install')Follow the instructions that are displayed on the MATLAB command line.Reboot MATLAB.This patch adds or modifies the following files:toolbox/target/extensions/processor/tic2000/+tic2000/+targetprefext/@Peripheral/Peripheral.m toolbox/target/extensions/processor/tic2000/+tic2000/+targetprefext/@Peripheral/SPI_ABaudRateFactor_UICallback.m

Summary: Incorrect Saturation block coverage when using fixed-point or mixed (different input/output) signal types and one of the limits is eliminated by optimizations.
Description: The Saturation block coverage might show incorrect results after one of the limits has been eliminated. For example, if the lower limit is set to 0 and the signal is an unsigned fixed-point data type (ufix), the Saturation block does not execute the lower limit check, but the coverage report might show that the branch was always false.
Workaround: There is no workaround for this problem. You can reduce its occurrence by disabling the Simulink Inline parameters optimization.

Summary: There is an error in the EVM calculation in the "Passband Modulation" demo
Description: The Error Vector Magnitude should be computed as sqrt(Perror/Preference) * 100%. However, the "Calculate EVM" subsystem in the demo calculates the mean after the square-root operation.
Workaround: Step 1: Open the Passband Modulation demo by entering commpassbandmod at the MATLAB command line.Step 2: Double-click the "commpassbandmod/Calculate EVM" subsystem.Step 3: Copy the "commpassbandmod/Calculate EVM/Mean" block and insert copies between the two |u|^2 and sqrt operations in the subsystem.Step 4: Delete the original "commpassbandmod/Calculate EVM/Mean" block and connect the "commpassbandmod/Calculate EVM/Gain" block to the "commpassbandmod/Calculate EVM/EVM" block.Step 5: Save the model.

Summary: Data structures generated for a model containing Variant Model blocks may be empty
Description: When a model containing a Variant Model block is configured to generate preprocessor conditionals, the compiled data structures in the code generated from the model may be empty under certain preprocessor conditions. For example, if a model contained a Variant Model block with three variants, and only the first two submodels had dworks , then the dwork  data structure for that top model is similar to the following:  typedef struct {#if S1  rtMdlrefDWork_bot1 Model_1_DWORK1;#endif#if S2  rtMdlrefDWork_bot2 Model_2_DWORK1;#endif} D_Work_top; If the third variant is the one to be compiled into the executable, so that S1  and S2  were false when compiling the code, the structure D_work_top  has no elements.
Workaround: If one of the submodels does not introduce an element into a data structure, add a block to the submodels that results in the creation of an element for that submodel. For example, add an unreferenced Data Store Memory block to a submodel to introduce an element corresponding to the submodel into the top model's dwork data structure.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink generates incorrect initialization code for a Model block inside a For Each subsystem
Description: Simulink generates incorrect initialization code for a Model block inside a For Each subsystem if any one of the following  conditions is true: The reference model initializes any DWork or Simulink global IO to non-zero values. The reference model has blocks that require zero-crossing detection.  This is because the code generated by Simulink calls the initialization function for the Model block only for the last iteration of the For Each subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Uninitialized memory read when minimizing artificial algebraic loop in the generated code
Description: The code generator might incorrectly turn a global block I/O structure into a local variable, which results in an uninitialized memory read, if all of the following conditions are met: Your model contains a nonvirtual subsystem, which is part of an artificial algebraic loop You selected the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences  parameter on the Subsystem block parameter dialog box The artificial algebraic loop contains a block with a continuous state The block I/O connects to the continuous state block through direct feedthrough The block I/O is not defined as global, for example, it is not defined with custom storage class  The resulting generated code might trigger a compiler warning about an uninitialized memory read in some compilers with a high warning setting. For example, if you use '-Wuninitialized' compiler setting on gcc.
Workaround: Define the block I/O as global, for example, using a custom storage class.

Summary: Incorrect attribute or timer value on entity after combining, replicating, modifying, and then splitting
Description: If your model performs the following operations in sequence, the attribute value after splitting has the original value prior to combining: Combine the entity, using the Entity Combiner block with the Make attributes accessible in departing entity  option selected. Replicate the composite entity, using the Replicate block. Modify an attribute value from the original component entity. Split the composite entity into its components, using the Entity Splitter block.  Alternatively, if your model performs the following operations in sequence, the timer after splitting does not reflect the restarting in step 3: Combine the entity, using the Entity Combiner block with the Make timers accessible in departing entity  option selected. Replicate the composite entity, using the Replicate block. Restart a timer from the original component entity, using the Start Timer block. Split the composite entity into its components, using the Entity Splitter block.
Workaround: Modify your model to perform steps 2 and 3 from the Description section in the opposite sequence.

Summary: Setting the data type of a Simulink block to a variable whose name starts with fixdt causes an error
Description: Setting the data type of a Simulink block to a variable whose name starts with fixdt  (for example, fixdt_foo ) results in the following error: ??? Invalid data type specification in 'vdp/Mu'. Caused by: 'fixdt_foo' specifies a fixed-point data type for parameter 'OutDataTypeStr', but the syntax of the expression is incorrect.
Workaround: Avoid using variables whose names start with fixdt.If you have to prefix a variable name with "fixdt" is required, first give such a variable a unique name (that does not start with fixdt), and then change the model file directly by replacing that name with the desired name. (Warning: MathWorks does not recommend directly modifying a model file; modify a model file directly only as the last resort and do so carefully.)

Summary: MATLAB can crash when loading an object that contains another object that was created with a classdef-based class, but is being loaded into a version of MATLAB that has an old-style definition of the same class.
Description: If you save an object that has a property containing an instance of another MATLAB classdef-style class, loading the .mat  file can cause MATLAB to crash if the contained object's class that is now on the MATLAB path is an old-style (non-classdef) definition. This problem can occur when all of the following are true:- The contained class was originally implemented using a non-classdef-style class definition- The contained class was updated to use a classdef-style class definition- You save an object that has an instance of the contained class that was created with the classdef-style definition- You load the object into a version of MATLAB that has the older, non-classdef-style definition of the contained object

Summary: Segmentation violation when using method handles inside anonymous functions.
Description: The following function is a simple example of how segmentation violations of this kind can arise:   function t = crash      t = @(bar) @bar.x;   end
Workaround: A method handle can be written as an anonymous function.  For example, the method handle @bar.x can be written @(varargin)bar.x(varargin{:}).  The crash may be avoided by using this expansion inside the outer anonymous function:   function t = crash      t = @(bar) @(varargin)bar.x(varargin{:});   end

Summary: Building a model that contains an asynchronous S-Function block or VxWorks Task Sync block might generate a compiler error
Description: Building a model that contains an asynchronous S-Function block or a VxWorks Task Sync block might generate code that causes compiler errors, such as undeclared identifier errors or parse errors. The compiler errors occur if braces are not generated around the inlined asynchronous code for the block but are needed to correctly define code scope when local variables exist. Here is a sample instance of the compiler error messages: C:\Tornado2.2/host/x86-win32/bin/ccppc -c -DCPU=PPC604 -DVXWORKS -W -nostdinc -fno-builtin -Bc:\Tornado2.2/host/x86-win32/lib/gcc-lib/ -DUSE_RTMODEL -DMODEL=rtwdemo_vxworks -DRT -DNUMST=2 -DTID01EQ=0 -DNCSTATES=0 -DRT_STATIC -DMULTITASKING=1 -I. -I.. -IC:\PROGRA~1\MATLAB\R2010a/simulink/include -IC:\PROGRA~1\MATLAB\R2010a/extern/include -IC:\PROGRA~1\MATLAB\R2010a/rtw/c/src -IC:\PROGRA~1\MATLAB\R2010a/rtw/c/src/ext_mode/common -IC:\PROGRA~1\MATLAB\R2010a/rtw/c/tornado/devices -IC:\DOCUME~1\RUSIEC~1\MYDOCU~1\MATLAB\rtwdemo_vxworks_tornado_rtw -IC:\DOCUME~1\RUSIEC~1\MYDOCU~1\MATLAB -IC:\PROGRA~1\MATLAB\R2010a\toolbox\rtw\rtwdemos -Ic:\Tornado2.2/target/h -Ic:\Tornado2.2/host/include/share -Ic:\Tornado2.2/host/x86-win32/lib/gcc-lib/powerpc-wrs-vxworks/gcc-2.96/include -Ic:\Tornado2.2/host/x86-win32/lib/gcc-lib/m68k-wrs-vxworks/gcc-2.96/include -O rtwdemo_vxworks.crtwdemo_vxworks.c: In function 'Task0':rtwdemo_vxworks.c:64: parse error before 'i'rtwdemo_vxworks.c:65: 'i' undeclared (first use in this function)rtwdemo_vxworks.c:65: (Each undeclared identifier is reported only oncertwdemo_vxworks.c:65: for each function it appears in.)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Real-Time Workshop might eliminate required integer downcasts in index expression required to match Simulink wrapping behavior
Description: Real-Time Workshop software might eliminate required integer downcasts in an index expression under the following circumstance: The model contains a Selector or Assignment block with an external index signal that is either an integer, or a fixed point data type, which is implemented using an integer. The external index signal data type implementation is an integer of a smaller type then the targeted platform native size (for example, uint8  or unint16  on a 32-bit target).  The Simulink simulation wraps the index value if there is an overflow in the value computation. Real-Time Workshop software might not replicate this wrapping behavior, due to the missing integer downcast.

Summary: Multiple problems with the contourcmap function
Description: Multiple problems occur with the function contourcmap:  When filled contour data is displayed on the axes, you need to know the contour interval in order to accurately use the function. Otherwise, if the contour interval does not match the specified 'CDELTA ' value, additional colors will exist in the colorbar that are not in the contour data or fewer colors will be displayed in the colorbar compared to the filled contour data. When contour data is displayed on the axes, the function should fix the 'ColorAlignment'  property to 'center'  when line-only contours are present and 'ends'  when filled contours are present. Otherwise, the colorbar provides misleading information. When the output handle from contourcmap  is deleted, all the graphics in the figure window are also deleted. When a single input argument is provided, input error checking provides misleading information. If the colorbar's UserData  is modified, then errors can occur when the handle is deleted.

Summary: mnrnd returns the error "Edge vector must be monotonically non-decreasing"
Description: When the probability vector passed into the mnrnd  function sums to just slightly more than 1 (1+eps , for example), mnrnd  accepts it, but adjusts its last element to make the sum exactly 1. However, this adjustment fails to correct cases where the last two or more elements are zero, and mnrnd  returns an error. For example, p = [ones(1,9)/9 0 0];mnrnd(5,p)??? Error using ==> histcEdge vector must be monotonically non-decreasing.Error in ==> mnrnd at 85r = histc(rand(m,n,outClass),edges,2);   This can also happen when p is a matrix whose rows contain multiple probability vectors.
Workaround: Normalize the probability vector to have a sum of 1, usinge = min([0 cumsum(p)],1);e(end) = 1;p = diff(e);When p is a matrix, usee = min([zeros(size(p,1),1) cumsum(p,2)],1);e(:,end) = 1;p = diff(e,[],2);This may alter some of the probabilities p. However, since p is supposed to have row sums equal to 1, the alteration is negligible.

Summary: Consecutive "on <event>:" statements in a Stateflow state might crash MATLAB
Description: If a Stateflow chart contains a state with two consecutive "on <event>:" statements, and the first "on <event>:" statement has no actions, opening the chart or simulating the model crashes MATLAB. The following is an example: on E1: // No actions specified. on E2: y++; ?
Workaround: Edit the model file in a text editor.Find and comment out the first "on <event>:" statement. This step will not change model behavior as it has no actions.

Summary: gammainc(x,a) with negative input x may hang for certain values of a.
Description: gammainc(x,a)  may hang for negative x  if abs(x)  is much bigger than a , for example, gammainc(-800, 1) . ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation and simulation: Using function calls in index expressions for arrays of structures might cause incorrect results
Description: Simulating or generating code for Embedded MATLAB Function blocks ˇŞ or for MEX functions created using emlmex  ˇŞ might produce incorrect results under the following conditions: Source code contains matrix indexing Matrix being indexed is an array of structures Index expression contains one or more function calls  For example, the following Embedded MATLAB function generates incorrect code: function out1=useArray(in1) out1 = in1; out1(foo()).a(1:3) = 2; end  You can use the attached example model and follow these steps to reproduce this problem: >> in1 = repmat(struct('a', ones(1, 3)), 2) >> emlmex useArray.m -eg {in1}   The result by running the generated MEX file is different from MATLAB.
Workaround: Don't use function call expressions in array indices that indexes array of structures.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: zero initialization code for rtM data might be removed even when Remove internal data zero initialization option cleared
Description: In some cases, code generation for a model might remove internal data zero initialization code for rtM  data even when the Optimization  pane option Remove internal data zero initialization  is cleared. This might affect code execution, for example, if the generated code uses an absolute time variable counter that is assumed to have been initialized to zero but has not been initialized to zero.
Workaround: To work around this bug, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the matlabroot command.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following file, for example, by adding .old to the end of the file name:matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/rtmdllib.tlcDownload the attached file for which the .version suffix matches your MATLAB version and place it at the same location as the file you renamed. Rename the downloaded file to remove the suffix. For example, for R2009a, download the file rtmdllib.tlc.9a and rename it to rtmdllib.tlc.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Truth tables created using Stateflow API might crash MATLAB
Description: When a model is being saved or simulated, a Stateflow truth table might crash MATLAB if the following conditions are true: The truth table was created using the Stateflow API. The truth table has empty cell values in the condition table or action table.
Workaround: When setting the condition table or action table with the Stateflow API, make sure the assigned cell array does not contain any empty cell values, such as "[]". Use an empty string "''" instead.

Summary: Incorrect detection of multirate access of global data stores from submodels
Description: Simulink provides a diagnostic to detect when global data stores are accessed within a model hierarchy at different rates. This feature relies upon Data Store Read and Write blocks. In some cases, Simulink may report that a data store is accessed at multiple rates, when in fact it is not. In other cases, Simulink may not report that a data store is accessed at multiple rates, when in fact it is.
Workaround: 1. Change the diagnostic 'Multitask data store' to 'warning' or 'none'.2. In the Simulink.Signal object that defines the global data store, set the SampleTime property to -1 (inherited).

Summary: Problems with polygon edge lines
Description: If you display a polygon using either mapshow  or geoshow  and then re-parent it to a different axes, the polygon fill will move as expected, but the edge line will be left behind in the original axes. Depending on your renderer setting and hardware, the edge line of a single polygon created by passing a scalar mapstruct or geostruct to mapshow  or geoshow  may appear to be missing, fragmentary, or dashed. The edge line for the first element  of a non-scalar mapstruct or geostruct passed to mapshow  or geoshow  may display improperly as described above.
Workaround: If you are using R2010a, you can work around this problem as follows:Make a back-up copy of the file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/map/private/constructEdgeLine.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.Add the following line:addlistener(h,'Parent','PostSet',@setEdgeLineProps);to the set of addlistener calls in the constructEdgeLine.m file.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.If you are using R2009a or R2009b, make the same change, but in the constructEdgeLine subfunction of the file matlabroot/toolbox/map/map/private/mappolygon.m.

Summary: Real-Time Windows Target models that contain SimMechanics blocks might not build correctly
Description: Real-Time Windows Target models that contain SimMechanics blocks might not build correctly. In the event of an incorrect build, the error reports the missing precompiled library, mech_rtwin.lib .
Workaround: As a workaround, Open the model.In the MATLAB Command Window, type rtwinconfigset(gcs).Save and build the model.

Summary: Link errors for exported external identifiers and global data stores caused by modeling error
Description: When generating code for a model containing variants, external identifiers and global data stores are not exported from inactive submodels. The problem occurs when compiling the generated code. The submodels that are inactive during code generation might be active when compiling the generated code. As a consequence, you might see the following link errors: Multiple definitions of an exported symbol in the generated code (rather than seeing the error when generating code)  Missing definitions of variables corresponding to global data stores  ?
Workaround: Correct this modeling error by modifying the model. If you have link errors due to multiple definitions of an exported symbol in the generated code, change the model to avoid reuse of an exported symbol. If you have link errors due to missing definitions of variables corresponding to global data stores, in the top model, connect a Data Store Read block of the affected data store to a Terminator block.

Summary: Keyboard shortcuts do not work for Simulink Web view opened in Firefox 3.6.3 or later
Description: The keyboard navigation shortcuts (for example, the r  key for zooming) do not work for Simulink Report Generator Web views in Firefox 3.6.3 or later.
Workaround: Install the attached file, to support keyboard shortcuts on Firefox 3.6.3 and later.To install the attached file:1. Stop MATLAB.2. Back up matlabroot/toolbox/rptgenext\resources\slwebview_utils.js.3. Download and copy the attached file to its respective location (see the previous step).4. Restart MATLABThis patch works with releases R2007a-R2010a. There is no patch available for earlier releases.

Summary: MWException is not thrown from compiled MATLAB function on error when one or more of the inputs to the function is of type MWJavaObjectRef.
Description: For example: function alwaysfail(x)  error('failure');  end  Java code: Compiled c = new Compiled();  // this line will NOT throw  c.alwaysfail( new MWJavaObjectRef( new Object() ) );  // this line will  c.alwaysfail( "not a java object ref" );

Summary: Probe block might cause segmentation fault in some cases when the Signals > Detect overflow diagnostic is set to warning
Description: Suppose that Signals > Detect overflow  is set to warning  in the Diagnostics > Data Validity  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. If the output value of the Probe block is not representable with the output data type, simulation of the model causes a segmentation fault.
