Workaround: There are multiple workaround methods:Change the output data type of the Probe block to a bigger data type, for example, double.Change the Signals > Detect overflow diagnostic to none.  This bug does not change any numeric values. It is related only to diagnostics.

Summary: The vpa function does not look for multiples of pi or rational numbers.
Description: When you call vpa  with double precision inputs for pi  or rational numbers like 1/10, the function  uses only the double precision digits. It does not use the full exact precision. For example: >>vpa(1/10,50)ans =0.1000000000000000055511151231257827021182
Workaround: Convert the expression that contains pi or rational numbers to a symbolic object. Then, call vpa for that symbolic object. For example:>>vpa(sym(1/10),40)ans =0.1

Summary: Assignments of the form A(i)= B might cause errors in Embedded MATLAB if A is of integer type and B is a fixed-point value.
Description: Assignments of the form A(i) = B  and A(i,j) = B  might cause "assertion failed" errors in Embedded MATLAB if A  is a matrix or vector of integer values and B  is a fixed-point value. The bug occurs in Embedded MATLAB Function blocks in Simulink, and in code compiled with emlmex  and emlc  functions.
Workaround: Cast the right-hand side of the assignment to the type of the left-hand side. For instance, if the type of A is int16, replace A(i)= B with A(i)= int16(B) .

Summary: Polyspace Viewer graphical user interface seems to freeze when you save review comments
Description: When saving review comments using the Polyspace Viewer GUI, the Viewer can "freeze" (become unresponsive) for a long time. The software may remain unresponsive for more than 30 minutes.  This occurs most often when your results are located in a remote folder.
Workaround: The following workaround is for R2010a only:Navigate to the following folder:%POLYSPACE_COMMON%\Viewer\bin ($POLYSPACE_COMMON/Viewer/bin on a UNIX system)Create backup copies of the following files: - rte-displayer.jar- rte-ihme-if.jar- rte-writer.jar- rte-ihme-verifier.jar- rte-reader.jarDownload the attached file:641573_AllOS_R2010a.zipExtract all files to the folder specified in step 1.

Summary: Certain polygons displayed by function geoshow may appear "inside-out"
Description: A round-off problem during trimming in function geoshow  may cause the exteriors of certain polygons to be filled, instead of the interiors.
Workaround: To work around this problem:Log into your computer using an account with Administrator privileges.Download the attached file, workaround_641390-10a_2010-07-22.zipOn Windows double-click the file to view its contents, then click the Extract button. On Macintosh, double-click the file to extract its contents. On Linux use the unzip command.Complete the instructions in the enclosed text file, README.txtThe updates provided in this workaround also address several issues that are older but much more subtle.

Summary: The Model Advisor check Identify blocks that generate expensive saturation and rounding code errors out when a Direct Lookup Table (n-D) block's output port is not of floating-point type
Description: Running the Model Advisor check Identify blocks that generate expensive saturation and rounding code  causes an error when the following conditions are true: The model has a Direct Lookup Table (n-D) block.  The block output is not of double  or single  type.

Summary: Updating the Time for a Simulink.Timeseries data log whose Data is of type embedded.fi produces an error
Description: If you update the Time  property for a Simulink.Timeseries  data log whose Data  property is of type embedded.fi , Simulink displays an error similar to this: my_ts.Time = new_time_vector; ??? No appropriate method, property, or field getSize for class Simulink.TimeseriesDataConstructor.
Workaround: This workaround is only for R2010a. (This issue is fixed in later releases.)Add the attached file (getSize.p) to the following folder:toolbox/simulink/simulink/@Simulink/@TimeseriesDataConstructor/

Summary: When you load a DFE or a linear equalizer from a MATLAB data file, the adaptive algorithm of the equalizer reverts to LMS
Description: When creating a decision feedback equalizer or linear equalizer using the DFE function or LINEAREQ function, you can specify an adaptive algorithm, such as RLS, CMA, Signed LMS, etc.When you load an equalizer object, it does not retain the saved adaptive algorithm settings and reverts to the default selection, which is LMS with a StepSize of 0.01 and a LeakageFactor of 1.

Summary: mmreader does not work with QuickTime movie (.mov) files on Macintosh OS X 10.5.8 systems that use QuickTime 7.6.6
Description: After you install QuickTime 7.6.6, mmreader  may give incorrect results when reading QuickTime movies (.mov).For example, the following code:    >> vidObj = mmreader('myfile.mov');    >> img = read( vidObj, 100 ); returns an error in Release R2009a:??? The frame index requested is beyond the end of the file.Error in ==> mmreader.read at 74        videoFrames = read(getImpl(obj), index);  and returns distorted images in Release R2009b or R2010a:    >> image(img) %shows distorted image This issue affects OS X 10.5.8 with QuickTime 7.6.6 only.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog into your computer using an account with Administrator privilegesDownload the workaround files associated with this Bug ReportLocate the download - this directory is referred to as <DOWNLOAD> in subsequent stepsLocate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt - this directory is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent stepsExit MATLABType the following in a Terminal window:cd <MATLAB>tar ?xvf <DOWNLOAD>/<downloaded file>

Summary: dependencies.toolboxDependencyAnalysis closes the model and errors if there are unsaved changes
Description: Running dependencies.toolboxDependencyAnalysis  on a Simulink model closes that model. If the model has unsaved changes, then you see an error about not being able to save that model.
Workaround: To workaround this, save all models first and then use the following code.files_to_analyze = {'vdp','sf_car'}; % Which files are loaded? files_to_reopen = files_to_analyze(bdIsLoaded(files_to_analyze)); % Run analysis: toolboxes = dependencies.toolboxDependencyAnalysis(files_to_analyze); % Re-open models: open_system(files_to_reopen)

Summary: Unable to deploy when using the fmincon 'sqp' algorithm.
Description: Deployed MATLAB applications that use the fmincon  'sqp' algorithm do not run on standalone machines. The deployed application fails because it does not include a required library for running the algorithm.
Workaround: After deploying your application, manually copy the *activeset* files from MATLABROOT2010a/bin/<ARCH>/ to MCRROOT/v713/bin/<ARCH>/. MATLABROOT2010a is the root location where MATLAB 2010a is installed, and MCRROOT is the location where you are deploying the application.Also, *activeset* is used to denote files specific to the platform that contain the names 'activeset'. On Microsoft Windows, copy the files activeset.dll and activeset.dll.csf. On Linux, copy the files libmwactiveset.so and libmwactiveset.so.csf. On Apple Macintosh, copy the directory libmwactiveset.dylib.dSYM.

Summary: Potential incorrect simulation results for right-click generated SIL block placed in enabled subsystem
Description: Simulating a model containing an enabled subsystem generates an uninitialized value on the enabled subsystem output port(s) if all of the following conditions exist: You generate a Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder SIL block using a right-click build of a subsystem. At least one output port of the subsystem that you right-clicked is driven by a local signal. You place the generated SIL block into an enabled subsystem. (Note that placing a generated SIL block into an enabled subsystem for simulation is not a recommended practice.) The SIL block directly drives one or more output ports of the enabled subsystem. The enabled subsystem output ports driven by the SIL block do not specify an Initial output  value. The enabled subsystem does not run at t=0 .  Under these conditions, blocks downstream from the enabled subsystem read an uninitialized value until the subsystem is enabled. This can potentially cause incorrect simulation results. Note:  This bug applies only to code run in simulation.
Workaround: To work around this bug, you should avoid placing a generated SIL block into an enabled subsystem for simulation.If using a SIL block in an enabled subsystem is unavoidable, and the enabled subsystem does not run at t=0, you must specify an explicit Initial output value on the enabled subsystem output port(s).

Summary: Cryptic error message when the start time specified in FFT Analysis tool of Powergui is prior to the start time of the signal structure
Description: In the FFT Analysis tool of Powergui, if the start time you specify in the FFT window is prior to the start time of the signal structure, the following error message is displayed: Index exceeds matrix dimensions Ideally, the error message should clearly explain that the start time you specified is lower than the first data point available in the selected signal.
Workaround: If the structure signal was generated from a Scope block, by using Save data to workspace, make sure the Limit data points to last check box in the Scope parameters dialog box is not selected. Restart the simulation and reopen the FFT Analysis tool, select the given signal and perform the FFT analysis with the desired start time.Alternatively, you can check the start time of the structure signal by typing: MyStructData.time(1) at the command line. The returned value corresponds to the lowest start time that can be specified in the FFT Analysis tool for this signal.

Summary: The UEI PD2-DIO-128i (isolated) digital I/O block falsely detects a channel conflict
Description: The UEI PD2-DIO-128i (isolated) digital I/O block falsely detects a channel conflict. If your model contains digital input and digital output blocks for the PD2-DIO-128i board, and these blocks use the same channel numbers, the software falsely reports an error.
Workaround: As a workaround,Close MATLAB.Download the attached file, mdiouei.m, to the directory matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocksRestart MATLAB.Rebuild your model.

Summary: Calling bsxfun with a sparse matrix and a full N-D array crashes MATLAB?.
Description: The following usage of bsxfun  causes MATLAB? to crash: A = rand(5,5,5); % 3-D full arrayB = sparse(5,5); % 2-D sparse matrixC = bsxfun(@plus, A, B);    This crash occurs for any combination of a full array with 3 or more dimensions and a sparse matrix with more than one column, and for any function handle argument that returns a full output.

Summary: Error taking address of field of structured parameter with custom storage class
Description: If both of the following conditions exist, calls to the TLC parameter function LibBlockParameterAddr  fail: Block accesses an element of a tunable parameter structure, such as P.a  Storage class of the parameter is Custom   When the error occurs, the following message appears: Error: Real-Time Workshop Error: Parameter 'P' is not addressable because of its custom storage class
Workaround: If you encounter this bug due to expression folding, locate the relevant section of the block diagram and insert a Signal Conversion block.Otherwise, change the parameter expression or the storage class.

Summary: Incorrect ASAP2 COMPU_METHOD section generated for model parameter resolved with custom storage class
Description: An incorrect ASAP2 description might be generated for a parameter in the model if both of the following conditions exist: The parameter has a fixed-point data type. A custom storage class is associated with the parameter.  Under these conditions, the COMPU_METHOD generated for the parameter in the ASAP2 file might use incorrect coefficients. For example, if the parameter data type was set using the fixed-point expression "fixdt(0,8,2) ", the generated COMPU_METHOD might be the following: /begin COMPU_METHOD.../* Long identifier */ "Q = V"/* Conversion Type */ RAT_FUNC/* Coefficients */ COEFFS 0 1 0 0 0 1/end COMPU_METHOD The correct representation of the fixed-point scaling would be the following: /begin COMPU_METHOD.../* Long identifier */ "Q = V * 4"/* Conversion Type */ RAT_FUNC/* Coefficients */ COEFFS 0 4 0 0 0 1/end COMPU_METHOD Note:  This error does not occur with integer or floating-point data types.

Summary: DIR command returns too many matches.
Description: When the following command is executed:    dir r1.* MATLAB displays all files that start with r1, including names such as r11.001. This is caused by mistakenly treating the input string as r1*.

Summary: The blackman function and generate method for sigwin.blackman return negative values.
Description: The values of a Blackman window should always be in the range [0 1]. Due to roundoff errors, the first and the last elements of a Blackman window returned by the blackman  function or generate  method of sigwin.blackman  are negative numbers.

Summary: Polyspace stops during compilation phase with the message: "Error: A segmentation fault occured in "edgcpfe.c.x86-mingw32.exe"
Description: Launching a verification on a C source code, Polyspace stops after some compilation issues with the following message: C:\mypath\string.h, line xx: error: expected a declarationC:\mypath\string.h, line xx: error: a declaration here must declare a parameterC:\mypath\string.h, line xxx: error: expected a ";"...Error: A segmentation fault occured in "edgcpfe.c.x86-mingw32.exe".Warning: Failed compilation of myfile.c  It can happen around MACROS like '__DATA_PTR_MEMORY_LIST1__() '.
Workaround: If a MACRO like '__DATA_PTR_MEMORY_LIST1__()' exists in your header files, adding a ';' after the macro can work around the compilation issue.If no MACRO exists, the reason for this error is specific to the code. If you see this message, please contact technical support, and send your code if possible, as we may provide a workaround.?

Summary: Error running models with Three-Phase V-I Measurement blocks in Rapid Accelerator mode
Description: If you try to run a model containing Three-Phase V-I Measurement blocks and using the Rapid Accelerator, you get an error:  Initialization commands cannot be evaluated.

Summary: Sample time independent model generates AUTOSAR Software Component with TimingEvent that has period of zero seconds
Description: If the Periodic sample time constant  parameter for your AUTOSAR model is set to Ensure sample time independent , then building your model generates an AUTOSAR Software Component with a TimingEvent that has a period of zero seconds: <TIMING-EVENT> <SHORT-NAME>Event_Step</SHORT-NAME> <START-ON-EVENT-REF DEST="RUNNABLE-ENTITY">/pkg/ib/SWC/Runnable_Step</START-ON-EVENT-REF> <PERIOD>0.000000000000000</PERIOD> </TIMING-EVENT>
Workaround: To work around this issue, do one of the following:Edit the XML file and change the period to a positive valueLoad the generated XML files into an AUTOSAR authoring tool, and change the period.

Summary: Compiling code on Macintosh computers with MATLAB Compiler, using Control Toolbox functions, may result in:Invalid MEX-filewhen the compiled code is executed against the MATLAB Compiler Runtime (MCR).
Description: Compiled code using Control Toolbox, attempting to load a Control Toolbox MEX-file with a dependency on libmwslicot.dylib , will not be able to locate libmwslicot.dylib  when executed against the MCR. This is due to MEX-files being linked with incorrect dependencies.
Workaround: For code compiled using R2010a, for execution in a 64-bit environment, use the attached fixmex.sh shell script.Back up the /toolbox/control/ctrlutil folder. For example, issue the command: cd /toolbox/control;cp -r ctrlutil ctrlutil.bak.Download and execute the attached shell script against your install_root (your MATLAB installation root). For example, issue the command: ./fixme.me install_rootRebuild any compiled applications, shared library components, or MATLAB Builder JA components that were exhibiting this failure.

Summary: Invalid error when exporting functions from a subsystem that uses data stores
Description: When all of the following conditions exist, an invalid error might be reported during code generation if you are exporting functions from a subsystem that reads from or writes to data stores: Data store memory block is placed outside the subsystem from which functions are being exported Data store memory block specifies <tt>auto</tt> for its data type or complexity SIL verification block is being generated  An example of the error that appears follows: Error reported by S-function 'ASWC_sf' in 'DSM_per_instance_2/ASWC/ASWC_sfcn': Data-Store Memory 'MYMEM' is registered with a 'real_T' data type.
Workaround: Explicitly set the data type and complexity parameters for the Data Store Memory block to a value other than <tt>auto</tt>.

Summary: When loading root inport data that uses structure format, empty signals.values fields may cause a crash
Description: When you load data that uses structure or structure with time format to root input ports, all signals.values  fields must be non-empty. If any element of the signals vector contains an empty values field, this may result in a crash.
Workaround: Before loading data using structure format to root input ports, verify that all values fields are non-empty.To determine whether there are any empty value fields in the structure, you can use code similar to this (where var is the input variable that is in structure format):for idx = 1:length(var.signals)   % Check for empty value fields   if isempty(var.signals(idx).values)   ...endAssign values to any empty var.signals fields.

Summary: MATLAB COM Control Container does not clear the result argument after successful event callback invocation
Description: MATLAB COM Control Container implementation of the Event Advise Sink IDispatch::Invoke method does not clear VARIANT FAR* pVarResult argument. This is related to COM server components which fire events and then rely on this variable type being set to VT_EMPTY by the Container Application upon successful event handler invocation. For example, COM Controls with the following Connection Point implementation are affected:         VARIANT varResult;        VariantInit (&varResult);        V_VT(&varResult) = VT_ERROR;        HRESULT hr = pDispatch->Invoke(dispId,                                       IID_NULL,                                        LOCALE_USER_DEFAULT,                                        DISPATCH_METHOD,                                        &dispParams,                                        &varResult,                                        pExcepInfo,                                        puArgErr);        if (SUCCEEDED(hr))        {            if (V_VT(&varResult) == VT_EMPTY)            {                DoSomething();            }            else            {                DoSomethingElse();            }        }  ?
Workaround: Download and save the attached patch corresponding to the installed MATLAB release version. The patch consists of 32 and 64-bit versions of comcli.dll. Prerequisite:Before installing the MATLAB comcli.dll patch, the Microsoft Visual C++ 2005 Service Pack 1 Redistributable Package ATL Security Update must be installed on your system.?1. Shutdown MATLAB 2. Backup the original comcli.dll located in the MATLAB bin directory 32-bit:  matlabroot\bin\win3264-bit:  matlabroot\bin\win643. Copy the patched DLL version to the above directory 4. Restart MATLAB

Summary: MATLAB can hang when opening the Model Browser or CAGE using the MATLAB Start menu
Description: When you open the Model Browser or CAGE for the first time after installing, the User Information dialog box appears. MATLAB can hang when you close the User Information dialog box. This only occurs if you used the MATLAB Start button to open the Model Browser or CAGE.
Workaround: You can avoid this problem by opening the Model-Based Calibration browsers from the command-line, by entering one of the commands mbcmodel or cage. After you fill in or dismiss the User Information dialog box, you can then open the GUIs from the MATLAB Start menu.

Summary: The neural network function roc returns incorrect values
Description: The threshold values returned by roc  are in reverse order of what they should be.
Workaround: To work around this error, flip the thresholds returned by roc to make their sequence consistent with the other returned values. If targets t and outputs y are row vectors, then the thresholds th can be flipped directly:[tpr,fpr,th] = roc(t,y) th = fliplr(th)If the targets and outputs have multiple rows, then each element of the cell array of thresholds must be flipped:[tpr,for,th] = roc(t,y)for i=1:numel(th), th{i} = fliplr(th{i}); end

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using emlc with emulation hardware might generate incorrect code if integer sizes do not match MATLAB host computer
Description: Using emlc  when you specify integer sizes in emulation hardware that differ from integer sizes in your MATLAB host computer might cause unexpected behavior, including: Compiler crashes when you turn on variable sizing frexp  produces wrong answers Rounding and overflow behavior of primary arithmetic operations on the emulation hardware might differ from behavior on the MATLAB host computer
Workaround: To prevent emlc from crashing, turn off variable sizing. There is no known workaround for numeric answer mismatches.

Summary: MATLAB version R2009b or earlier can crash if you try to load a MAT file containing a certain type of function handle that was saved in version R2010a or later.
Description: If you save a function handle that was created inside a version R2010a or later class definition, MATLAB can crash when you try to open the MAT file containing the function handle in version R2009b or earlier. The problem occurs only if the function handle is created inside a class definition. For example, the following class creates a function handle and assigns it to a property, fh . classdef cls    properties        fh;        p;    end    methods        function obj = cls            obj.fh = @meth;            obj.p = 101;        end        function a = meth(obj)            a = obj.p;        end    endend If you try to save the function handle in a file foo.mat  in version R2010a: % R2010a>> obj = cls;>> f = obj.fh;>> save foo.mat f;  and load it in R2009b, MATLAB can crash: % R2009b>> load foo.mat;>> obj = cls; >> f(obj); Also, the following steps can crash MATLAB: % First save an instance of cls in R2010a>> obj = cls;>> save foo obj;% Then load it in R2009b and MATLAB can crash.>> load foo
Workaround: There are two workarounds.1. Create the function handle outside of the class definition. Use this workaround if the function handle does not point to a local function or a method in the class definition file.2. Use an anonymous function handle in the class definition. For example, modify the cls class definition to use an anonymous function:classdef cls    properties        fh;        p;    end    methods        function obj = cls            obj.fh = @(a)meth(a); % Define an anonymous function handle            obj.p = 101;        end        function a = meth(obj)            a = obj.p;        end    endend?

Summary: Maryland polygon corrupted in usastatelo shapefile
Description: The usastatelo shapefile (file usastatelo.shp  and associated index file usastatelo.shx ) was modified in R2010a in response to Bug 532944 . When these changes were made, the polygon for the state of Maryland was inadvertently corrupted when a "bow tie" defect was detected but was not properly repaired.
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the data files usastatelo.shp and usastatelo.shx in your current installation with the updated versions in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make backup copies of the following files:matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapdemos/usastatelo.shpmatlabroot/toolbox/map/mapdemos/usastatelo.shxwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing version of usastatelo.shp, adding "old" to the file extension: usastatelo.shp.oldCopy the versions of usastatelo.shp and usastatelo.shx from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Saving a model with a Three-Phase V-I Measurement block produces a warning about block diagram containing one or more parameterized library links
Description: When you first simulate and then save a SimPowerSystems model containing Three-Phase V-I Measurement blocks, an error similar to the following is displayed:  Warning: Block diagram 'power_PSS' contains one or more parameterized library links. To find the parameterized links use the Model Advisor. The diagram has been saved but may not behave as you intended.
Workaround: To fix this issue, at the MATLAB command prompt, type the following commands: open_system('powerlib') set_param('powerlib','lock','off') set_param('powerlib/Measurements/Three-Phase V-I Measurement','PreSaveFcn','set_param(gcb,''LinkStatus'',''restore'')') save_system('powerlib') close_system('powerlib')

Summary: Simulink model containing Signal Builder block created in previous releases does not work properly in a later release (R2010a)
Description: Simulink model containing Signal Builder block created in a previous Simulink release does not work properly in a later release (R2010a). The model works properly in Simulink 7.4 (R2090b).
Workaround: As a workaround, if you are using Simulink 7.5 (R2010a),Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file: /toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/restore_from_saveStruct.p/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/signalbuilder.mTo see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: The interpn function gives incorrect interpolation values if one of the coordinate vectors of the sample data is monotonically decreasing and nonequally spaced.
Description: If the interpn  function is called with the following syntax: VI = interpn(X1, X2, X3, . . ., V, Y1, Y2, Y3, . . .) where Xi  specifies the coordinate vectors of the points where the values V  are located and Yi  specifies the points at which the interpolant is to be evaluated.If the coordinate vector Xi  is monotonically decreasing and nonequally spaced, the interpolated values in VI  will be incorrectly oriented; they will be flipped in the i 'th dimension. For example, we can determine whether the following vector X3  is monotonically decreasing and nonequally spaced using the diff  function. X3 = [10 8 7 6.3 4 2.7 2.1 1.8]diff(X3)ans = -2.0000 -1.0000 -0.7000 -2.3000 -1.3000 -0.6000 -0.3000 The consistency in the negative signs indicates the vector is monotonically decreasing. Since the magnitudes differ, the spacing is not equal.
Workaround: Installation instructions1. Log into your computer using an account with Administrator privileges2. Download the workaround files associated with this Bug Report3. Locate the download - this directory is referred to as <DOWNLOAD> in subsequent steps4. Locate your MATLAB?? installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt - this directory is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps5. Exit MATLAB6. Follow additional steps, depending on your operating systemOn Windows:- Open the downloaded file- Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> directoryOn Linux and Macintosh:- Type the following in a shell (on Linux) or a Terminal (on Macintosh) window:cd <MATLAB>tar -xvf <DOWNLOAD>/<downloaded file>

Summary: Generated code for model with Model Variants blocks does not compile
Description: The generated code for a model with Model blocks might not compile if both of the following conditions are true: The generated code uses preprocessor directives to conditionally compile subsets of that code The Simulink.Variant  objects reference Simulink.Parameter  objects which utilize the custom storage class ImportedDefine
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds:Change the storage class of the referenced Simulink.Parameter objects to the custom storage class CompilerFlag.Replace the referenced Simulink.Parameter objects with Variants.Macro objects, which are defined via the attached, zipped data package. Unzip the package into the directory containing the model, and then set the storage class of the Variants.Macro objects to either ImportedDefine or CompilerFlag.

Summary: When the Signal Builder block Run All button is clicked, an error occurs with no error source message
Description: When the Signal Builder block Run All  button is clicked, an error occurs, but there is no error message that describes where the error occurred.
Workaround: As a workaround, if you have Simulink 7.4 (R2009b),Close MATLAB.Rename matlabroot/matlab/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/playAll.p to matlabroot/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/playAll_old.p.Download attached file playAll_r2009bd.p and rename it to playAll.p.Save playAll.p to matlabroot/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private.Restart MATLAB.From the MATLAB Command Window, type rehash toolboxcache.As a workaround, if you have Simulink 7.5 (R2010a),Close MATLAB.Rename matlabroot/matlab/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/playAll.p to matlabroot/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/playAll_old.p.Download attached file playAll_r2010ad.p and rename it to playAll.p.Save playAll.p to matlabroot/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private.Restart MATLAB.From the MATLAB Command Window, type rehash toolboxcache.

Summary: Selecting multiple variables in the Workspace Browser can make MATLAB unresponsive in debug state if Signal Processing Toolbox is installed
Description: MATLAB can stop responding when you select certain combinations of variables in the Workspace Browser. The issue occurs under the following conditions: dbstop if error  or dbstop if all error  is on Signal Processing Toolbox is installed You select two variables are selected in the Workspace Browser, the first of which is a scalar (or any other non-vector or non-matrix)  ? ?
Workaround: To work around this issue:Download the file signalplotfunc.m from the attached zip arhive.Replace your installed version of signalplotfunc.m which exists in matlabroot/toolbox/shared/siglib/ with the file you just downloaded.

Summary: Spectrum Scope may compute and display incorrect Power Spectral Density when buffering with overlap and inheriting sample time
Description: The Signal Processing Blockset? Spectrum Scope block incorrectly computes and displays the Power Spectral Density (PSD) when all of the following conditions are met: The Spectrum units  parameter is set to one of the following options: Watts/Hertz  dBW/Hertz  dBm/Hertz    The Buffer input  check box is selected. The Buffer overlap  parameter is set to a non-zero value. The Inherit sample time from input  check box on the Axis Properties  tab is selected.
Workaround: You can workaround this issue by changing the following parameter settings on the Spectrum Scope block:Uncheck the Inherit sample time from input checkbox on the Axis Properties tab.Specify the sample time of the input signal using the Sample time of original time series parameter.?

Summary: ClusterSize property value does not match setting in hpcserver configuration
Description: The first time you call findResource  with an hpcserver configuration, the ClusterSize  property value might not be the same as the value defined in the configuration. This problem occurs only when using Microsoft HPC Server 2008 or CCS schedulers.
Workaround: Ensure that the CCP_SCHEDULER environment variable is defined on the client computer, and set its value to be the hostname of the scheduler to which you are trying to connect.

Summary: checkEnvSetup generates warnings with c6747evm
Description: Executing checkEnvSetup  on the MATLAB command line produces the following warning messages >> checkEnvSetup('ccs', 'C6747', 'check' )1. CCS (Code Composer Studio)Your version : 3.3.82.13Required version: 3.3.82.13Required by : Embedded IDE Link 4.1Required for : Automation and Code GenerationWarning: Illegal right hand side in assignment. Too many elements.  > In C:\rw_apps\matlab\R2010a\toolbox\idelink\extensions\ticcs\envChecker\checkEnvSetup_private.p>genReport at 373In C:\rw_apps\matlab\R2010a\toolbox\idelink\extensions\ticcs\envChecker\checkEnvSetup_private.p>checkEnvSetup_private at 61In checkEnvSetup at 131   2. CGT (Code Generation Tools)Your version : Required version: 6.1.10Required by : Embedded IDE Link 4.1Required for : Code generation Warning: Illegal right hand side in assignment. Too many elements. > In C:\rw_apps\matlab\R2010a\toolbox\idelink\extensions\ticcs\envChecker\checkEnvSetup_private.p>genReport at 373In C:\rw_apps\matlab\R2010a\toolbox\idelink\extensions\ticcs\envChecker\checkEnvSetup_private.p>checkEnvSetup_private at 61In checkEnvSetup at 131   3. DSP/BIOS (Real Time Operating System)Your version : Required version: 5.33.05Required by : Embedded IDE Link 4.1Required for : Code generation  ...
Workaround: Download the attached patch installer, patch_installer.m, and the ZIP archive, patch_635740.zip.To install the patch, start MATLAB and enter the following command at the prompt:patch_installer('patch_635740.zip', 'install')Follow the instructions on the MATLAB command line to complete the installation.The attached patch modifies the following file in your MATLAB installation:<matlabroot>\toolbox\idelink\extensions\ticcs\envChecker\checkEnvSetup_private.p

Summary: Write-protected readme.txt halts MATLAB Compiler.
Description: With a write-protected readme.txt  file in the output  directory, compile a standalone application using mcc. MATLAB Compiler stops and the application is not generated.
Workaround: Ensure the existing readme.txt file in the output directory is not write-protected.

Summary: PID Controller or PID Controller (2DOF) block inside Stateflow? Simulink FCN block produces error
Description: When used inside a Stateflow? Simulink FCN block, the PID Controller  and PID Controller (2DOF)  blocks can generate errors, including the following error: Reference to non-existent field 'Controller'  When an error occurs, the blocks do not behave as specified in their block dialogs, producing incorrect simulation results.
Workaround: Unzip the attached file, and apply the attached patch as follows:Exit MATLAB?.Rename $matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\blocks\+pidpack\@PIDConfig\checkApplicationData.m to $matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\blocks\+pidpack\@PIDConfig\checkApplicationData.m.old. $matlabroot is the MATLAB root directory on your machine. To locate $matlabroot, type matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.Save the attached file as $matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\blocks\+pidpack\@PIDConfig\checkApplicationData.m.Restart MATLAB.At the MATLAB command prompt, type the following command to complete the installation:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Assignments that read and write to the same complex structure field and perform a complex multiplication or division may generate incorrect code.
Description: This bug affects code generation for any model that generates the following pattern: Assignments that read and write the same complex field of the same structure Right-hand side of the assignment contains a complex multiply or divide, as in this example:a.f = a.f * 3i;   Here, a  is a structure or bus with a complex (non-real) field f . In this case, the generated code incorrectly reads intermediate computations from the complex multiplication, leading to incorrect answers. This bug can occur when you use Simulink Accelerator, Model Reference in non-normal mode, Real-Time Workshop, emlc , emlmex , and blocks that use code generation for simulation such as Stateflow charts, Truth Table blocks, and Embedded MATLAB Function blocks. To determine if the problem is present, check the numerical correctness of any complex multiplications or divisions with a single, non-trivial input.

Summary: The Simulink Design Verifier report is not generated at the end of analysis.
Description: At the end of an analysis, Simulink Design Verifier starts generating results, such as the data file and the harness model if applicable, but the report of analysis is not generated. No error is given when the tool is run from the Tools  menu. If the analysis is run using the command sldvrun , the following error is displayed: ??? Incorrect number of right hand side elements in dot name assignment. Missing [] around left hand side is a likely cause.  Error in ==> ReportUtils.prepareProperties at 17 properties.expectedOutputDesc = ['These output values are expected assuming '...  Error in ==> /mathworks/devel/jobarchive/R2009bd/.snapshot/2010_03_04_h07m53s56_job703a_pass/current/matlab/toolbox/sldv/sldv/+Sldv/@ReportUtils/generate.p>generate at 146 sldvRep.properties = Sldv.ReportUtils.prepareProperties(data, testComp);  Error in ==> sldvRunAnalysis>generateReport at 326 rptCreated = Sldv.ReportUtils.generate(sldvData, testcomp, {'usesldvoptions'}, showUI);  Error in ==> sldvRunAnalysis at 169 generateReport(sldvData, testcomp);  Error in ==> sldvrun at 75 [ status, fileNames, newModelH, msg ] = sldvRunAnalysis(modelH, blockH, opts, showUI, startCov);
Workaround: Installation instructions:Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Change logOctober 14, 2010: attachment_635493_9b_10a_2010-10-14.zip: initial version

Summary: Segmentation violation occurs when using the frestimate command with certain values of the Simulink model parameter Mux blocks used to create bus signals (GUI) or 'StrictBusMsg' (API)
Description: When estimating frequency response of a Simulink model using the frestimate  command, a segmentation violation occurs if: Mux blocks used to create bus signals  in the Configuration Parameters window is set to error . The corresponding command-line parameter 'StrictBusMsg ' is set to 'ErrorLevel1 ', 'WarnOnBusTreatedAsVector ' or 'ErrorOnBusTreatedAsVector'.
Workaround: To work around this issue, change the Simulink model parameter to 'None' or 'warning'.To do this programatically, use the one of following commands:set_param(mdl,'StrictBusMsg','None');set_param(mdl,'StrictBusMsg','warning');To set the parameter using the GUI, use the following steps:In the model window, select Simulation-> Configuration Parameters.In the Configuration Parameters window, select the Connectivity node under the Diagnostics node.In the Buses area, specify the Mux blocks used to create bus signals option to be none or warning.

Summary: A parfor loop can yield incorrect results when a reduction variable has multiple assignments per iteration and the reduction function is non-commutative.
Description: An example that illustrates this bug is: v = [] parfor i=1:2     v = [v, 2*i-1];     v = [v, 2*i]; end  The same failure occurs if there is a single reduction inside a loop nested within the body of the parfor, when the loop has at least two iterations.
Workaround: Rewrite the code so that there is not more than one reduction assignment per iteration:v = [] parfor i=1:2     w = [];     w = [w, 2*i-1];     w = [w, 2*i];     v = [v, w]; end

Summary: Manifest analysis cannot discover all dependenices if masked blocks have multiple dialog callbacks
Description: Simulink Manifest analysis reports only files associated with the first "dialog callback" function of any masked block that has dialog parameters.If a masked block has multiple dialog parameters, and more than one of those dialog parameters has a "dialog callback" function, then the Simulink manifest cannot discover any files associated with the subsequent dialog callback functions.
Workaround: You can manually add any files that your design requires but manifest analysis cannot discover automatically. Add files to the manifest, select Tools > Model Dependencies > View/Edit Manifest.

Summary: Calling ezplot can result in an infinite recursion.
Description: If axis limits are sym  objects, calling ezplot  can lead to an infinite recursion. As a result, MATLAB errors: syms xa = sqrt(sym(2));ezplot(x^2,[-a a])??? Maximum recursion limit of 500 reached. Use set(0,'RecursionLimit',N)to change the limit.  Be aware that exceeding your availablestack space can crash MATLAB and/or your computer. If the recursion limit is too large, an infinite recursion causes MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: Convert the axis limits to double precision:>>ezplot(x^2,double([-a a]))

Summary: Documentation for dataset/stack incorrectly refers to the 'Newdatavarsname' parameter
Description: The reference page for the dataset/stack method , and the example on that page, refer to the input parameter name 'Newdatavarsname'. Using that name causes the stack method to error.
Workaround: The correct input parameter name is 'NewDataVarName'.

Summary: Specifying a linearization for a block or subsystem using a configuration function can give incorrect results
Description: When specifying a linearization for a block or subsystem, you may get an incorrect linearization result when the following are true: You specify the linearization as a configuration function instead of a MATLAB? expression. The block or subsystem has a vector or bus input signal. The block or subsystem has multiple input ports. The specified linearization depends on the input signal level.  When the above are true, the blockdata  input argument to the configuration function?can register an incorrect input signal level. The resulting block linearization can be incorrect.
Workaround: Follow these steps to apply the attached patch:Quit MATLAB?.Rename the file MATLABROOT\toolbox\slcontrol\slcontrol\@LinearizationObjects\@linearizationutil\utEvaluateSpecification.mtoutEvaluateSpecification.bat. To locate the MATLABROOT folder, enter matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.Download the attached file to the MATLABROOT\toolbox\slcontrol\slcontrol\@LinearizationObjects\@linearizationutil\folder.Restart MATLAB.Enter the following command at the MATLAB prompt:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: MWCharArray constructor appends an additional NULL character for each extended character in a java.lang.String
Description: String s = new String("Fecha de comercializaci¨®n");MWCharArray c = new MWCharArray( "Fecha de comercializaci¨®n");int[] dim = c.getDimensions();for(int d:dim)   System.out.println(d + " ");
Workaround: As a workaround use the MWCharArray factory functions that allow the caller to specify the exact dimensions of the MATLAB array to be created, rather than letting the native conversion layer infer the dimensions. For example:String s = new String("Fecha de comercializaci¨®n");MWCharArray c2 = MWCharArray.newInstance(new int[]{1,s.length()}, s);

Summary: The subs function errors when no substitutions can be made.
Description: Calling subs  when no substitutions can be made errors. For example: >>subs(sym('x'))??? Subscript indices must either be real positive integers orlogicals.
Workaround: Always specify at least one substitution. For example:>>subs(sym('x'), 'x', 10)

Summary: The tooltip in the Current Folder Browser hides the file name or icon from view.
Description: When using the MATLAB? desktop, if you hover the mouse pointer over certain files in the Current Folder Browser a tooltip appears. This tooltip displays either the full file location or a message indicating why the file is not accessible to MATLAB. When the Current Folder Browser is positioned on the right side of the Desktop, this tooltip might hide the file name or icon, making it difficult to click on the file.
Workaround: Use this workaround with MATLAB? Version 7.10 (R2010a) only. Do not use it with any other version of MATLAB.Download the attached zip file to your matlabroot/java folder, where matlabroot is the location on your file system returned by running the matlabroot function in MATLAB.Unzip the file using the MATLAB unzip function.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: In Polyspace, importing comments from results generated with older versions of the software can cause @FFT errors.
Description: When using Polyspace release 2010a to import comments from results generated with older versions of the software, the following errors can occur: "@FFT: color of comment is different when importing comments" "@FFT: Try to access line <line> " "@FFT: Unable to find the source file <source.h>" These errors will be displayed in a dialog window. ?
Workaround: The patch attached to this EBR addresses the 3 possible problems. You should install this patch on your client system.Download either the Windows or Linux version of the patch. Installation instructions are provided  in the archive.

Summary: Sorting a cell array with uninitialized contents may crash MATLAB?
Description: Using the sort  command on a cell array of strings where some cells are uninitialized may crash MATLAB?. The following code demonstrates the problem: c = cell(2,1);c(1) = {'a'};sort(c);

Summary: Harness creation fails for models that have identically named variables in their model workspace and in a referenced model's workspace.
Description: Simulink Design Verifier analysis fails to generate a harness model if the analyzed model or any referenced models have model workspace variables with the same name as model workspace variables from any other referenced model. A warning dialog box appears during harness generation and asks the user to save the unsaved changes in the referenced models. ?
Workaround: Back up matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv\+Sldv\+xform\@BlkReplacer\fixModelWorkspace.m to a folder outside MATLAB path.Download the appropriate attached file for your MATLAB installation.Rename the attached file to fixModelWorkspace.m.Replace fixModelWorkspace.m with matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv\+Sldv\+xform\@BlkReplacer\fixModelWorkspace.m.Restart MATLAB.?

Summary: Simulink may crash for models with blocks with structure parameters and virtual bus inputs on Windows
Description: Simulink may crash when compiling a model on Microsoft Windows when all  these conditions occur: 1. The model contains a block such as Unit Delay, Memory, Rate Transition, or Merge, and that block specifies a MATLAB structure parameter for its Initial conditions  or Initial output  parameter. 2. The input to the block is a virtual bus signal. 3. The Model Browser window is visible and has the Show masked subsystems  option enabled.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, with the model open, perform one of the following actions:In the Model Browser, disable the Browse masked subsystems option (using the middle toolbar at the top of the Model Browser tree view).In the Model Editor, select View > Model Browser Options and clear the Show Masked Subsystems option.In the MATLAB Command Window, enter:set_param('model','BrowserLookUnderMasks','off')

Summary: HDL code generation for an Extract Bits block fails if bit range is defined by variables (instead of constants)
Description: If you use a variable to specify the Bit indices  of an Extract Bits block, and then initiate HDL code generation for the model, Simulink HDL Coder terminates code generation and displays the following error message: "Error: Evaluation of elaborate function on class hdldefaults.ExtractBits failed with the error message: Attempted to access range(1); index out of bounds because numel(range)=0." If you use constants, instead of variables, to specify the Bit indices , this error does not occur. ?
Workaround: Instead of using variables to specify the Bit indices, resolve the variables manually and use the resultant values as constants to specify the Bit indices.

Summary: Saving user-defined subclasses of event.EventData in MAT-files and loading them can crash MATLAB.
Description: A defect in the loading of user-defined subclasses of event.EventData  can cause MATLAB to crash when loading these saved subclasses. The problem occurs because MATLAB checks the ConstructOnLoad  attribute only at the subclass, but not for superclasses. Therefore superclass constructors are not being called even if the superclass defines the ConstructOnLoad  attribute but a subclass does not.

Summary: Calling the subs function with a string containing a symbolic variable f gives incorrect results
Description: If you call the subs  function with a string which contains the free variable f , you get an incorrect result. For example, syms x; f = x * x; subs('2*f') ans =     4*f
Workaround: To fix this issue, replace the subs.m file in the directory toolbox/symbolic/symbolic with the file attached to this report.Alternatively, use a sym object instead of a string. For example,syms x; f = x * x; subs(sym('2*f')) ans = 2*x^2

Summary: Polyspace Launcher fails to automatically detect the Polyspace Queue Manager Server with a message: 'No QueueManager server found'
Description: When launching a verification on the Queue Manager server, Polyspace stops with a message 'No QueueManager server found' .  This happens when the Queue Manager has not been configured, even if Polyspace Client and Polyspace Server are installed on the same machine.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, provide the hostname of the Queue Manager server in the Polyspace Launcher GUI by selecting Edit > Preferences > Remote Launcher.You can also manually add the field  remoteConfiguration=<hostname>\:<port #>in the file Polyspace-Launcher.prf. where <hostname> is the IP address or hostname of the Queue Manager server machine and <port #> port number. Polyspace-Launcher.prf is located in $HOME/.rte sub-folder on UNIX or in %USERPROFILE%\.rte on Windows.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB library blocks using non-tunable structure parameters cannot be instantiated more than once.
Description: If an Embedded MATLAB library block uses non-tunable structure parameters, you cannot add more than one instance of the block in the same main model, even when all instances inherit the same parameter value. The error message looks like this: "Data 'XXX' must have the same initial value in all instances of library."
Workaround: Disable links for affected Embedded MATLAB library block instances.

Summary: PDF generation for Chart Loop component may fail
Description: PDF generation for reports with a Chart Loop component may fail for a model that includes multiple linked Stateflow charts that reference the same library chart.
Workaround: Close MATLAB.Make a backup copy of MATLABROOT/toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/@rptgen_sf/@csf_chart_loop/loop_getLoopObjects.p, where MATLABROOT is the root of the MATLAB installation directory on your system.Download a copy of the version of loop_getLoopObjects.p attached to this report.Replace MATLABROOT/toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/@rptgen_sf/@csf_chart_loop/loop_getLoopObjects.p with the downloaded copy of loop_getLoopObjects.p.

Summary: MATLAB may crash or give incorrect results when using recursive functions.
Description: When using recursion, it may be possible to make MATLAB crash or give the wrong answer. This can happen if the call to the recursive function is part of a complex expression that includes other operations and function calls. For example, the last line of the ELSE block shown here makes recursive calls to helpsort  twice during a concatenation operation:    function res = bug      res = helpsort([2 3 4 1]);   end   function sorted = helpsort(vec)      if length(vec) <= 1         sorted = vec;      else         a = vec(vec < vec(1));         b = vec(vec > vec(1));         sorted = [helpsort(a), helpsort(b)];   % The bug occurs here.      end   end
Workaround: Rewrite helpsort to assign the results of the recursive calls into separate statements, as shown here:   function sorted = helpsort(vec)      if length(vec) <= 1         sorted = vec;      else         a = helpsort(vec(vec < vec(1)));         b = helpsort(vec(vec > vec(1)));         sorted = [a, b];      end   end

Summary: tscollection object with a nonuniform time vector loads with no time vector
Description: A tscollection  object with a nonuniform time vector saved using a version of MATLAB? before Version 7.10 (R2010a), loads into MATLAB Version 7.10 with an empty time vector.

Summary: Publish configuration settings are not always restored after exiting MATLAB?
Description: If you specify any of the following publish configuration settings in the Editor (using the Edit Configurations dialog box), the settings are not restored after exiting and restarting MATLAB: Output file format: doc  or ppt  Image Format: jpeg  Image Format: default , when in the Japanese locale
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Confusing error message Reference to non-existent field 'buildHooksCheckSum' on attempting to run a SIL or PIL simulation
Description: With R2010a, if the build folder slprj  already exists and was created when you generated code for the model using a previous release, the software produces a confusing error message when you try to run a SIL or PIL simulation. The error message is: Reference to non-existent field 'buildHooksCheckSum'
Workaround: To work around this issue, delete the build folder slprj and rerun the SIL or PIL simulation.

Summary: Calling inv or mldivide (\) with complex single precision inputs may return incorrect results on machines with Intel Core i7 processors.
Description: On machines with Intel Core i7 processors, inv  and mldivide  (\ ) may return incorrect results when the inputs are complex and single precision data.

Summary: Models that contain CAN object mode Send blocks configured with two or more identifiers cannot compile
Description: Models that contain CAN object mode Send blocks configured with two or more identifiers cannot compile. The affected blocks are the Softing CAN AC2-104 and  AC2-PCI object mode Send blocks.
Workaround: As a workaround for xPC Target versions 4.2 (R2009b) and 4.3 (R2010a),Close MATLAB.Download the attached file, canac2objsend.tlc, to the directory matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\target\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks\tlc_cRestart MATLAB.Rebuild your model.

Summary: Mask parameter value ignored when used as the initial condition for another block.
Description: This bug surfaces when a block's initial condition is set by a mask parameter and this parameter is defined in the Simulink Design Verifier parameter configuration file, for example, when a Data Store Memory block's initial condition is set to a mask parameter that is included in the parameter configuration file. Under this scenario, the block initial condition gets set to 0 instead of to the parameter value(s) specified in the configuration file. This leads to incorrect results.

Summary: Documentation contains incorrect tested version numbers for GCC and GDB in Linux for use with Eclipse IDE.
Description: The R2010a documentation lists the incorrect tested version numbers for GCC and GDB in Linux for use with Eclipse IDE as follows: GCC 3.4.5 GDB 6.3.2  The tested software version numbers in R2010a are: GCC 4.2.x GDB 6.8.x

Summary: getdatasources fails returning list of data sources
Description: When you execute the getdatasources  command in the MATLAB Command Window, the data source most recently added in the ODBC Data Source Administrator may not show up. (You may not see this data source in the Visual Query Builder either.) In addition, you may receive one of the following errors: Unable to open ODBC.INI Or:Registry has been disabled by your Administrator Or: ??? Cell contents assignment to a non-cell array object.Error in ==> getdatasources at 76d{end+1} = tmp(1:j-1); %#ok Store source name Despite these error messages, you can still connect to the data source.
Workaround: To retrieve information about which ODBC data sources are available on your system, use the getdatasources command to query the registry and read the odbc.ini file in your SystemRoot directory. Due to various security restrictions, these operations may fail on your system and lead to error messages or the absence of data sources from querybuilder. To resolve this issue, update getdatasources with the attached file, which uses an alternative method of accessing the registry.Find the $MATLABROOT directory where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.Quit MATLAB.Rename the following MATLAB file: $MATLABROOT\toolbox\database\vqb\getdatasources.mto getdatasources.m.oldDownload the attached file. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 3.Restart MATLAB.After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB command prompt: rehash toolboxcache

Summary: The GETPDB and PDBREAD functions return a PDB structure with an invalid sequence of residues.
Description: In few PDB-formatted files, the sequence of residues can be misinterpreted by the GETPDB  and PDBREAD  functions as a nucleotide sequence instead of a three-letter amino acid abbreviation. A symptom of this problem is the NumOfResidues  field in tthe Sequence  field of the output structure differing from the actual length of the Sequence  field.
Workaround: Download the attached file.Quit MATLAB.Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\pdbread.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\pdbread.m.old, where $MATLAB$ is the folder where MATLAB is installed on your machine.Copy the downloaded file to the folder $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfoRestart MATLAB.Note: This workaround is only functional in versions 2007b (3.0) and later.

Summary: Polyspace cannot initialize to 'full-range' or take into account data range specifications on some global variables when using the main generator.
Description: When launching a verification using the options '-main-generator ' and '-main-generator-writes-variables ' with the argument 'public ' or 'all ', Polyspace can not initialize some of the global variables. This happens when the verification contains more than one C file, and when the global variable is to be used in one file and not in others. Note : Range information about global variables contained in the data range specification (DRS) file is also ignored when the program is structured this way.
Workaround: To workaround this issue on a Windows system:Browse to the folder: %POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\binCreate a backup copy of the file: .\x86-mingw32\rte-kernel.cx86-mingw32.exeDownload the attached file: 630707_Windows.zip.Extract the .exe files from the zip archive and copy it to the following sub-folder: .\x86-mingw32To workaround this issue on a Linux system:Browse to the folder: $POLYSPACE_C/Verifier/binCreate backup copy of the following file ./x86-linux/rte-kernel.cx86-linuxDownload the attached file: 630707_Linux.zipExtract the -linux file from the zip archive and copy it to the following sub-folder: ./x86-linux

Summary: Invalid topology in Great Lakes polygon (gtlakelat, gtlakelon) in conus.mat causes display problems with geoshow
Description: Some examples that use the file conus.mat  display incorrectly when using geoshow  in R2010a. Part of the polygon defined by the vertex arrays gtlakelat  and gtlakelon  (a single closed curve including Lakes Superior, Huron, and Michigan) has "spike" defects in its topology. The invalid polygon topology predates R2010a, but earlier versions of geoshow  were insensitive to it due to other problems.
Workaround: To work around this problem, replace the version of conus.mat in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapdemos/conus.matwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: conus.mat.oldCopy the version of conus.mat.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Code generation error or incorrect ASAP2 file when index signal into Interpolation using Prelookup block is configured without storage class
Description: Generating an ASAP2 file for a model can fail or generate an incorrect ASAP2 file if both of the following conditions exist: The model interpolates an n  -dimensional table using a Prelookup block that feeds an index value into an Interpolation Using Prelookup block. The ASAP2 file generated for the model describes the n  -dimensional table either as a CURVE (n  == 1 ) or as a MAP (n  == 2 ). The index signal, represented in the ASAP2 file by an AXIS_PTS_REF entry, is configured without a storage class (Storage class  is set to Auto ).  Under these conditions, code generation might generate an incorrect AXIS_PTS_REF description in the ASAP2 file or, in some cases, might fail with an error message similar to the following: Error: File: C:\MATLAB\R2010a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\asap2lib.tlc Line: 102 Column: 62Unable to find Parameter within the Block scope
Workaround: To work around this bug, perform one of the following actions:Configure the index signal with a storage class. For example, set Storage class to ExportedGlobal.Test-point the index signal.

Summary: Coverage details for Multiport Switch blocks do not describe user-specified indices.
Description: When you collect and report coverage for models that have Multiport Switch blocks with the Data port order  parameter set to Specify indices , the detailed coverage outcomes in the generated report do not accurately describe the conditions for selecting a particular input. For example, if you parameterize a Multiport Switch block with three data inputs, specify the indices as {9, 5, 0} , and then run a simulation where the control input has values of 9 and 5, the coverage report correctly identifies the number of occurences the output came from input ports 2 and 3. However, the report labels the coverage details as follows: truncated input value  = 1 (output is from input port 2)  = 2 (output is from input port 3)  Instead, the actual conditions should be in==9  and in==5 . ? ? ? ?

Summary: A = containers.Map('hello',[1 2]) errors.
Description: The error occurs when, in the constructor,  a single element is used as a key, and a non-scalar value is supplied.

Summary: Incorrect list of System objects code generation support
Description: These System objects were omitted from the list of objects that support code generation. video.BoundaryTracer  (incorrectly listed as  video.BoundaryTracer)video.ContrastAdjustervideo.DeployableVideoPlayervideo.FFT2Dvideo.GeometricTransformEstimatorvideo.GeometricTransformervideo.HoughLinesvideo.ImageFiltervideo.ImagePaddervideo.TemplateMatchervideo.TextInserter  ?

Summary: Incorrect list of System objects code generation support
Description: These System objects are included in the list of objects that support code generation. They were not included in this release. signalblks.CholeskyFactorizationsignalblks.LUFactorization signalblks.QRFactorizationsignalblks.QRSolver  These System objects were omitted from the list of objects that support code generation. signalblks.Autocorrelatorsignalblks.LDLFactorsignalblks.LPCToLSPsignalblks.LSPToLPCsignalblks.LUFactorsignalblks.RCToAutocorrelation ?

Summary: Incorrect input factor symbols for local multiple models built from templates
Description: When you use a model template to build local multiple models, the models have incorrect symbol names for the input factors.  The models have symbols from the model template instead of the test plan symbols.This causes problems later for operations that depend on symbol names, such as creating point-by-point and composite models.
Workaround: Replace the files gui_localmodopts.m and set.m in the following locations of your MATLAB installation folder with the attached files:/toolbox/mbc/mbcmodels/@localmulti/gui_localmodopts.m/toolbox/mbc/mbcmodels/@xregmulti/set.mRestart MATLAB.

Summary: Attempt to generate HDL code for vector division operation in Division block emits uninformative error message
Description: Simulink HDL Coder does not support vector division for HDL code generation. When HDL code is generated for a  Division block that is configured to perform vector division, the coder emits the error message shown below, and does not suggest any workaround. ??? Error: lowerpir: lowering pir native comps to eml failed with the error message:No appropriate method, property, or field Signed for class hdlcoder.tp_array.
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds:The first possible workaround is to use a suitably sized array of Division blocks, such  that each block performs one scalar division, instead of a single Division block that is configured for vector division. A second possible workaround is use an Embedded MATLAB block that performs the desired vector division instead of a  Division block.

Summary: STRNCMP and STRNCMPI return FALSE when comparing cell arrays and considering 0 characters (i.e., with the third input set to zero).
Description: The following statements currently return FALSE, but should return TRUE:   STRNCMP(cellArray1, cellArray2, 0)   STRNCMP(charArray, cellArray, 0)   STRNCMPI(cellArray1, cellArray2, 0)   STRNCMPI(charArray, cellArray, 0)

Summary: Cannot use tab completion with function syntax on a file name containing a space.
Description: For a file named "some image.png ", trying to tab complete the file name using function syntax returns the wrong result. For example, if you type: ls('so  then press Tab , MATLAB completes the string as: ls('some' image.png'  If you close the parenthesis, the resulting command is not legal MATLAB syntax.
Workaround: Use command syntax instead of function syntax. For example, instead of typing:ls('so Type:ls so Since the filename starts with the characters so, tab completion properly handles the space in the file name by putting single quotes around that part of the string resulting in the completion. In this example, when you press Tab, MATLAB displays:ls some' image.png' Do not use tab completion after typing:ls 'so In this case MATLAB looks for a file that starts with a single quote. When you press Tab, MATLAB displays:No Completions

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Real Time Workshop might compute wrong answers for Embedded MATLAB colon expressions that use 32-bit floating point numbers.
Description: Real Time Workshop produces wrong answers for Embedded MATLAB colon expressions of the form: single(-2^127): single(2^124): single(2^127) This bug occurs under the following conditions: When you use the single  datatype  When the range of the colon expression (2^128 in this example) exceeds realmax('single')
Workaround: Use the double datatype rather than single when the range of the colon expression exceeds realmax('single').

Summary: Incorrect filling of 2D tables using extrapolation of unsorted points on a line
Description: Table filling of 2D tables from points on a line is not performed correctly when the points are unsorted. The simplest case of this is when you try to fill a 2D table using data from a single row or column. If these points are unsorted, then the table is filled with incorrect values. If the points are sorted then the table is filled by replicating the row or column over the whole table.Filling 2D tables using extrapolation of points on a line works by projecting points on to the line and using linear extrapolation. The linear extrapolation is not performed correctly when the projected points are unsorted.
Workaround: Replace the file pExtrapolateSpecialCases.m, in the following location of your MATLAB installation folder with the attached file:/toolbox/mbc/mbcdata/@cgmathsobject/private/pExtrapolateSpecialCases.mThen, restart MATLAB.

Summary: Reading WMV files on Windows systems with mmreader crashes MATLAB
Description: Opening some Windows Media Video (WMV) files on Windows systems causes MATLAB to crash.For example, given a file named myfile.wmv , this code crashes MATLAB on some systems:obj = mmreader('myfile.wmv'); % read in the last frameread(readerObj, inf);

Summary: Error running some compiled Curve Fitting Toolbox code: undefined functions lscftsh or alslnsh
Description: If you attempt to run compiled Curve Fitting Toolbox code, you may get an error that some functions are missing. After using MATLAB Compiler to compile an application that uses functions in Curve Fitting Toolbox, when running the compiled application, you see one of the following errors:  ??? Undefined function or method 'lscftsh' for input arguments of type 'fittype'.  ??? Undefined function or method 'alslnsh' for input arguments of type 'fittype'. This bug only affects you if you have Curve Fitting Toolbox and MATLAB Compiler and do not  have Optimization Toolbox. If you have Optimization Toolbox, this bug should not affect you. Do not use the workaround. If you see similar behaviour, then please contact Technical Support.
Workaround: Create a new directory called MATLABROOT\toolbox\compiler\patch. You can do this in MATLAB with the following commands>> patchDir = fullfile(matlabroot,'toolbox','compiler', 'patch');>> mkdir(patchDir);Do not add this directory to the MATLAB path.Download the attached file, attachment_628656_20100614.zip.Unzip the contents of the zip file, cflscftsh.p and cfalslnsh.p, to MATLABROOT\toolbox\compiler\patch.Restart MATLAB.Recompile your application.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: target function library might erroneously replace saturate-on-overflow fixed-point division operation with wrap-on-overflow implementation
Description: Code generation for a model might erroneously replace a fixed-point division operation if all of the following conditions exist: The target function library (TFL) selected for the model conatins a table entry designed to replace wrap-on-overflow fixed-point division operations. Specifically, The entry key is 'RTW_OP_DIV '. The entry saturation mode is 'RTW_WRAP_ON_OVERFLOW '. All of the conceptual argument types for the entry are built-in integers (int8 , uint8 , int16 , uint16 , int32 , or uint32 ), not fixed-point integers.   The model contains at least one saturate-on-overflow fixed-point division operation with non-unary slope inputs or output.  Under these conditions, the TFL might incorrectly match the table entry and replace the saturate-on-overflow fixed-point division operation with the wrap-on-overflow fixed-point division implementation function. This results in loss of the saturation logic in the generated code and can lead to incorrect numerical results for cases where the inputs to the division operation cause an overflow to occur. Note:  Default TFLs supplied by The MathWorks do not trigger this bug.
Workaround: If your model contains fixed-point division operations with non-unary slope inputs or output, avoid registering TFL entries with the properties listed in the Description (division, wrap on overflow, and built-in integer types for the conceptual arguments). This prevents the erroneous replacement from occurring during code generation.

Summary: The SQP algorithm of fmincon can stop prematurely on problems with bounds but no inequality constraints.
Description: A bug in the stopping test in the SQP algorithm of fmincon  can cause the solver to stop prematurely, meaning before the algorithm has converged. This can occur when the problem: Has finite bounds on at least one variable (lb, ub) Does not have inequality constraints (linear or nonlinear)  ?
Workaround: Add a redundant linear inequality constraint, weaker than an existing bound.For example, suppose you have a finite upper bound on variable i:x(i) <= ub(i)Create a weaker linear inequality constraint:A = zeros(1,n); A(i) = 1;b = ub(i) + offset;Here n is the number of variables and offset is a positive value. offset weakens the inequality so the linear constraint is never active.

Summary: Error closing the model selection figure for local models
Description: There is an error when you close the model selection figure at the local model node and the figure does not close. This error only occurs when a best model is selected.
Workaround: Replace the file mv_ValidationTool.m, in the following location of your MATLAB installation folder with the attached file:/toolbox/mbc/mbcmodels/mv_ValidationTool.mThen, restart MATLAB.

Summary: Exporting function-call subsystem can generate extra function if outputs of two function-call subsystems merged
Description: Exporting a function-call subsystem can generate an unnecessary extra function if both of the following conditions exist: The model contains two or more function-call subsystems. The output of the function-call subsystem you are exporting is merged with the output of another function-call subsystem using a Merge block.  Under these conditions, the generated code might contain an extra function named subsystem  _ASYNCn  , where subsystem   is the name of the function-call subsystem that you are exporting and n   is a positive integer. The extra function either is empty or contains a redundant copy of some code from the exported function.

Summary: Missing function definition in exported function-call subsystem code if output signal drives both another function-call subsystem and output port
Description: Exported code for a function-call subsystem might omit the function definition for another function-call subsystem if both of the following conditions exist: The model contains two or more function-call subsystems. The output signal for the function-call subsystem that you are exporting branches. One branch drives a second exported function-call subsystem and another branch drives an output port at the same level as the two exported function-call subsystems.  Under these conditions, the function definition of the second exported function-call subsystem might be missing in the generated code for the first exported function-call subsystem.
Workaround: To work around this bug, avoid branching the function-call subsystem output signal to drive the output port at the level containing the two exported function-call subsystems.

Summary: Custom storage class definitions fail to load
Description: The Custom Storage Class Designer allowed you to do the following without reporting an error: Open the custom storage class (CSC) registration file (csc_registration.m ) for editing. Move the default CSC definition from the first position of the list of CSC definitions, possibly as a result of adding a new user-defined CSC defnition to the registration file. Save the changes made to the registration file.  When you try to reload the registration file, the operation fails.
Workaround: Open csc_registration.m for editing.Move the default CSC definition to the first position of the list of CSC definitions.Save the change when closing the file.

Summary: When used on a 64-bit Windows systems, a 32-bit compiler may terminate with an access violataion.
Description: When used on a 64-bit Windows systems, a 32-bit compiler may terminate with an access violataion.
Workaround: Backup the existing compiler.dll in install_root\bin\win32, and then download the attached compiler.dll file to install_root\bin\win32.

Summary: Cosine block does not accept frame-based signals in versions R2009a to R2010a
Description: The Cosine block in the Lookup Tables block library does not accept frame-based signals in versions R2009a to R2010a, but it did in past releases.

Summary: Verification fails with the following error in the C to IL phase or at end of compilation phase: Unable to open '<absolute path to a temporary folder>' or to dev_null
Description: For unknown reasons, verification can stop in the C to IL phase or at end of compilation phase. The error messages will look like the following: unable to open <results_folder>\C-ALL/dev_null  unable to open C:\<absolute path to a temporary folder>\Temp\PolySpace\<any_name>/<another name>
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your Polyspace installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your Polyspace installation. By default this is in c:\PolySpace or in /usr/local/PolySpace on Linux systems. This folder is referred to as <POLYSPACE> in subsequent steps. PolySpace_Common and PolySpaceFor<lang>_R20xx are the two principal sub-folders of <POLYSPACE>.Exit any Polyspace Graphical Intferface.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <POLYSPACE> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:unzip -l <POLYSPACE>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <POLYSPACE> folder.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell: cd <POLYSPACE>unzip <.zip file>Check that binaries have appropriate ˇ®read & executeˇŻ permissions.Delete the .zip file in the <POLYSPACE> folder.

Summary: In rare cases, the mupad command fails to start MuPAD from MATLAB.
Description: When you start the MuPAD notebook using the mupad  command, the command hangs. Interrupting the command with CTLR+C  produces the following message: >> mupadStarting MuPAD...???
