Operation terminated by user during ==> mupad.mupad>waitForFile at 275In ==> mupad.mupad>mupad.mupad at 74                waitForFile(nb.tmpfile); The following approach also can help recognize this bug. Try to start a notebook with the command system([matlabroot, '/toolbox/symbolic/mupad/share/bin/mupad']) If you have this bug, you will get the following error. Here glnxa64  denotes the platform that you use: /usr/local/matlab/toolbox/symbolic/mupad/glnxa64/bin/mupad: /usr/lib64/libstdc++.so.6: version `GLIBCXX_3.4.9' not found (required by /usr/local/matlab/toolbox/symbolic/mupad/glnxa64/bin/mupad)
Workaround: This workaround assumes thatMATLAB version 7.10 (R2010a) is installed in /usr/local/matlabYou have copied the zip file attachment_627361_20100330.zip to the folder /tmpTo work around the issue on glnx86 and glnxa64, replace the files/usr/local/matlab/toolbox/symbolic/mupad/share/bin/mupad/usr/local/matlab/toolbox/symbolic/mupad/share/bin/mupkernby the files in the zip file attachment_627361_20100330.zip. The recommended way to replace the files on a Linux shell is to unpack the zip archive inside the folder /usr/local/matlab/toolbox/symbolic/mupad/share/bin with the following commands:cd /usr/local/matlab/toolbox/symbolic/mupad/share/bincp mupad mupad_orgcp mupkern mupkern_orgunzip -x /tmp/attachment_627361_20100330.zipThe first line changes the directory. The next two lines make a backup of the original files. The last line replaces the old files with the new files. If unzip asks you to confirm that you want to replace the original file, press y to confirm.

Summary: Polyspace verification fails during compilation phase when invoking the MISRA-C++ checker
Description: When launching Polyspace verification on C++ code without using the MISRA-C++ checker, compilation completes and the verification continues.  However, when you invoke the MISRA-C++ checker on the same code, the softtware reports a compilation error in some C++ headers. For example, this can happen in some Microsoft Visual Studio .NET 2003 includes like afxcoll.inl : Verifying MyFile.cpp C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio .NET 2003\Vc7\atlmfc\include\afxcoll.inl, line 345: error: a reference of type "const void *&" (not const-qualified) cannot be initialized with a value of type "void *" | return m_pNodeHead->data; } | ^

Summary: MATLAB throws a spurious error for certain types of expressions.
Description: The following statement fails when it is used in a script. In this statement, A  is a variable, f  and g  are functions, and op  is a binary operator:   A([f(end) op g(end)]) This is also true for statements like either of the following:   A([f(end),g(end)])   A({f(end),g(end)})
Workaround: Use the named form of the binary operator, e.g., "plus" instead of the + sign. Similarly, use horzcat(...) for [...] and horzcat({...},{...}) for {...,...}.

Summary: Link to Context-Sensitive help for uitable warning "Table data is not editable at this location" is broken
Description: If you receive the following warning when editing a uitable: Warning: Table data is not editable at this location. Please click for more information When you click the link to obtain more information, you receive an error similar to this: An error was occured:??? Error using ==> helpview at 200 Specified topic id (NaN_column_error) does not exist in map file C:\MATLAB\R2010a\help\techdoc\ref\ref.map. and the context-sensitive help does not display.
Workaround: If you receive this warning, use the following command to view the context-sensitive help in the Help browser:web([docroot '/techdoc/ref/uitableproperties.html#NaN_column_error'])

Summary: SIL simulation with exported AUTOSAR runnables may not match Normal mode simulation
Description: If you export AUTOSAR runnables from function-call subsystems and an outport of one of the function-call subsystems has a data type that is an enumerated type, then a software-in-the-loop (SIL) simulation using the exported runnables may not match the Normal mode simulation if both of the following are true: The outport is associated with a function-call subsystem that is not executed during the first time step The default enumeration (first enumeration) of the enumerated type does not correspond to 0

Summary: hdfinfo causes segmentation fault on HDF4 file.
Description: Using hdfinfo  to read certain HDF4 files written with the SD interface can cause a segmentation fault.
Workaround: Installation instructions?????? Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.?????? Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.?????? Exit MATLAB.?????? Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.?????? Extract the files from the .zip file. ????????????? ???????????????????? Windows Systems:?????????????????????????? ????????????????????????????????? Summary: Inconsistent behavior passing empty cells from Microsoft Excel to MATLAB Builder EX components.
Description: When using MATLAB Builder EX components in Microsoft Excel, empty cells are handled differently, depending on whether they are a single cell or a range of cells. When a single cell is passed, the default behavior is to pass a empty matrix (i.e. []) to the called MATLAB code. When a range of empty cells is passed into a MATLAB function, the default behavior is to pass a matrix of zeros. This results in inconsistent behavior in how empty cells are passed into MATLAB functions.
Workaround: There is no way to workaround for this issue without writing custom Visual Basic code.

Summary: The cat function returns wrong results with symbolic inputs.
Description: The cat  function returns wrong results with symbolic inputs. For example: >> cat(1,sym('[0, 0]'),sym('[1, 0]')) ans =                 0 matrix([[1, 0]])
Workaround: When concatenating matrices, use horzcat or vertcat.

Summary: Build process failure with error Undefined identifier sigIdxIndex for model containing blocks with tunable parameter expression
Description: The code generation build process fails for a model that contains a block that has a tunable parameter expression, where the expression includes an array selection. For example, an expression P(I) , where P  and I  are parameters of data type double and non-Auto storage class. The failure occurs during TLC processing, and the error message includes the text: Undefined identifier sigIdxIndex   The following is an example of the error message in R2008a: Error: File: matlabroot /rtw/c/tlc/lib/paramlib.tlc Line: 244 Column: 91 Undefined identifier sigIdxIndex Error: File:  matlabroot  /rtw/c/tlc/lib/paramlib.tlc Line: 280 Column: 92 Undefined identifier sigIdx Error: File: matlabroot /rtw/c/tlc/lib/paramlib.tlc Line: 364 Column: 70 Undefined identifier sigIdx Error: File: matlabroot /rtw/c/tlc/lib/paramlib.tlc Line: 535 Column: 39 Undefined identifier sigIdx
Workaround: Replace sigIdxIndex as follows:Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB command prompt.Open matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/lib/paramlib.tlc.In paramlib.tlc, search for and replace two occurrences of sigIdxIndex with sigIdx.Save paramlib.tlc.

Summary: Persistent "Remapping selected signal" warning while building subsystems
Description: Building a subsystem in a model might throw the following warning if the input to the subsytem is an unnamed bus signal and the signal displays the propagated signal label:  Warning: Remapping selected signal 'Bus.SubBus.signal' to 'signal1.Subbus.signal' in 'my_model/Bus Selector' to match the bus hierarchy. Please save your model to make this change permanent.  However, saving the model, as suggested by the warning, does not make the change permanent and consecutive builds continue to throw the same warning.
Workaround: To work around this issue:Right-click the input signal to the subsystem that you want to build.Choose the Signal Properties menu item.In the upper right corner of the Signal Properties dialog box, set Show propagated signals option to off.Save the model.?

Summary: Increasing the value of -path-sensitivity-delta can decrease the precision of a verification
Description: The option -path-sensitivity-delta  allows you to increase the precision of interprocedural verification withina particular level.  However, increasing this value can increase the number of orange checks in your results, and can also greatly increase computation time. MathWorks recommends that you use the default value for this option.
Workaround: The following workarounds are for R2010a only. To workaround this issue on a Windows system:Browse to the folder %POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\binCreate backup copy of the following files: .\x86-mingw32\iabc012.cx86-mingw32.exe .\x86_64-mingw32\iabc012.cx86_64-mingw32.exeDownload the attached file 625732_Windows_R2010a.zip.Extract *.exe files from the zip archive and copy them into previous sub-foldersTo workaround this issue on a Linux system:Browse to the folder $POLYSPACE_C/Verifier/binCreate backup copy of the following files ./x86_64-linux/iabc012.cx86_64-linux ./x86-linux/iabc012.cx86-linuxDownload the attached file 625732_Linux_R2010a.zipExtract *-linux files from the zip archive and copy them into previous sub-folders

Summary: The results generated on a multi-core machine can be slightly different than with a single-core machine.
Description: Due to incorrect management of precision, verification results generated on a multi-core machine can be slightly different than the same verification performed on a single-core machine.  The difference is generally one or two orange checks.

Summary: PolySpace Queue Manager display the status "running" even when the verification has finished.
Description: The PolySpace Queue Manager Interface can display the status "running," even after a verification has finished. All other verifications are queued and "Follow Progress..." or "View Log File..." show that the verification has ended. Trying to stop the verification causes an error message like 'Error: the verification #id has completed before it could be killed '. This problem occurs when the Queue Manager handles more than one CPU.
Workaround: When this problem occurs, there is no way to remove the verification from the queue except by stopping the Queue Manager service and removing the verification manually on the server.To workaround this issue on a Windows system using R2010a Polyspace server:Stop the Queue Manager service on the CPU using the following command: %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\RemoteLauncher\PSTRLManager.exeNavigate to the following folder: %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\RemoteLauncher\bin\x86-mingw32Create a backup copy of the file polyspaced-RL-R2010a-V12.cx86-mingw32.exeDownload the attached file: "625512_Windows.zip."Extract the file to the folder specified in step 2, then restart the Queue Manager service using the command specified in step 1.Repeat all steps for each CPU, including the Queue Manager server. Note: You must update the Queue Manager server last.?To workaround this issue on a Linux system using R2010a Polyspace server:Stop Queue Manager service on the CPU using the following command: /etc/init.d/polsypaced stopNavigate to the followiong folder: $POLYSPACE_COMMON/RemoteLauncher/bin/x86-linuxCreate a backup copy of the file polyspaced-RL-R2010a-V12.cx86-linuxDownload the attached file: "625512_Linux.zip."Extract the file to the folder specified in step 2, then restart the Queue Manager service using the following command /etc/init.d/polyspaced start.Repeat all steps for each CPU, including the Queue Manager server. Note: You must update the Queue Manager server last.

Summary: Output Switch block with storage capability might cause SimEvents solver error
Description: If your model includes an Output Switch block with the following settings, you might see an error message. Switching criterion  = 'From signal port p' Store entity before switching  selected  An example of the error message is as follows: Error occurred in 'mymodel/___DES_SL_IF___'. Error in SimEvents solver :Time of next hit, 0, returned by S-function 'vs_sl2des' in 'mymodel/___DES_SL_IF___/___vs_sfun___' during flag=4 call must be greater than previous time hit value of 80975.99999999904.  ?
Workaround: In your model, for each Output Switch block with the settings as in the Description section, follow these steps:In the block dialog box, clear the Store entity before switching option.Insert a Single Server block into the model.Connect the Single Server block so it precedes the Output Switch block: In the block that connects to the IN port of the Output Switch block, remove that connection and reconnect the port to the IN port of the Single Server block.Connect the OUT port of the Single Server block to the IN port of the Output Switch block.In the block dialog box of the Single Server block: Set Service time to 0.Set Service completion event priority to 1.Run the simulation and determine whether the switch behavior has not changed. The service completion priority in the server is 1 (from step 4b), whereas the original storage completion event in the Output Switch block had higher priority (SYS2). If this difference affects the behavior of your simulation, you might need to adjust the event priority values of blocks in your model that have events simultaneous with the service completion in the new Single Server block. You can use the SimEvents debugger to determine which events are simultaneous in your simulation.

Summary: Failure during model reference code generation with Mux block connected to Data Store Write block
Description: Code generation for the accelerated simulation model reference target of a model might fail if the model contains a Mux block whose output signal connects to the inport of a Data Store Write block. Real-Time Workshop software throws the following error and assertion: ??? Error using ==> tlc_c at 160 Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: .\base_core\expr\ExprTools.cpp line 644: false ". Please report this to The MathWorks if you can cause it to recur.CGIR assertion in .\base_core\expr\ExprTools.cpp(644): 'false' [0] libmwsimulink.dll:void __cdecl assertion_func(void *,char const * const,int,char const * const)() + 46 bytes[1] cg_ir.dll:void __cdecl client_assertion_failed(char const *,int,char const *)() + 27 bytes[2] cg_ir.dll:public: static class CG::Expr * __cdecl CG::ExprTools::takeAddress(class CG::NodeFactory *,class CG::TypeFactory *,class CG::Expr *,bool)() + 294 bytes[3] cg_ir.dll:public: static class CG::Expr * __cdecl CG::ExprTools::takeAddressForFcnArg(class CG::NodeFactory *,class CG::TypeFactory *,class CG::Expr *)() + 22 bytes[4] rtwcg.dll:public: virtual void __thiscall RTWCG::DataStoreConnector::replace(class CG::VarExpr *,class RTWCG::RTWCGFactory &)() + 664 bytes
Workaround: Place a Signal Conversion block between the Mux block and the Data Store Write block. Right-click the Signal Conversion block and select SignalConversion Parameters. In the Block Parameters dialog box, select the Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization check box.

Summary: External mode Disconnect controls not properly enabled after connecting to xPC, Freescale MPC5xx, Infineon C166, or Texas Instruments C2000 target?
Description: When running a model in external mode with the Arm when connecting to target  option selected, if you connect to an xPC, Freescale MPC5xx, Infineon C166, or Texas Instruments C2000 target, the Disconnect  controls on the model toolbar and the External Mode Control Panel are not enabled. This makes it impossible to graphically disconnect from the target, unless you start and stop the target executable.
Workaround: To work around this bug, clear the Arm when connecting to target option in the External Signal & Triggering dialog box. Manually arm the trigger instead, using the Arm Trigger button on the External Mode Control Panel.

Summary: Documented reference to SimulinkMode property for configuration sets is incorrect
Description: The SimulationMode  property has been removed from the configuration set. However, the property was not removed from the Simulink.ConfigSet  documentation. If you try to set the property for a configuration set that is not attached to a model, the Simulink software displays a warning. If you set the property for a configuration set that is attached to a model, the Simulink software sets the simulation mode for the model.
Workaround: To set the simulation mode for a model, use set_param(modelName,'SimulationMode',value), where modelName is the name of the model and value is 'normal', 'accelerator', or 'external'.

Summary: The option Ignore objectives satisfied by existing test cases is ignored.
Description: When extending existing test cases, the option Ignore objectives satisfied by existing test cases  is ignored. Objectives satisfied by existing test cases are always included in the test suite generated by Simulink Design Verifier.
Workaround: Replace the MATLAB file matlab/toolbox/sldv/private/mdl_get_boolean_option.m with the attached file.

Summary: Error on MATLAB startup when certain Java JAR files are on the static javaclasspath.
Description: When certain JAR files (e.g., some versions of the MySQL JDBC driver) are added to the MATLAB? static javaclasspath via modifying classpath.txt , the following error is thrown at MATLAB startup. ERROR: Error starting reference:file:/C:/mysql-connector-java-5.1.12-bin.jar (org.osgi.framework.BundleException: Unresolved package in bundle 6: package; (package=javax.naming))org.osgi.framework.BundleException: Unresolved package in bundle 4: package; (package=javax.naming)
Workaround: To workaround this issue, put the JAR on the dynamic rather than static classpath. Do the following:Close MATLAB and remove the JAR from the static classpath by deleting its entry from classpath.txt.Start MATLAB, and add the JAR to MATLAB's dynamic classpath using the javaaddpath function. For example:>> javaaddpath('C:\mysql-connector-java-5.1.12-bin.jar')You can add this to a startup.m script to avoid having to do it manually when MATLAB starts.

Summary: Code execution is slow when run in cell mode or when publishing MATLAB? files
Description: The MATLAB JIT (Just in Time Accelerator) is disabled when executing code cells from within the MATLAB Editor or when publishing MATLAB files. As a result, the same code may not execute as fast as if it were run using the Play button in the Editor or run directly from within the Command Window. The performance difference is often most noticeable when running many iterations of a for  loop.
Workaround: Note, the workaround/patch described below only applies to MATLAB version 7.10 (R2010a)Instructions for installing the patch:Log into your computer using an account with Administrator privileges.Download the patch files associated with this Bug Report.Locate the download - this directory is referred to as $DOWNLOAD in subsequent steps.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt - this directory is referred to as $MATLAB in subsequent steps.Follow additional steps, depending on your operating system.On Windows:Exit MATLAB.Open the patch_624940_R2010a_win#.exe file attached to this Bug Report, where # is 32 or 64, depending on your particular installation of MATLAB.Extract the contents of the file to the directory:$MATLAB\bin\win#where # is 32 or 64.On Linux and Macintosh:From the $DOWNLOAD directory, type the following in a shell (on Linux) or a Terminal (on Macintosh) window: cd $DOWNLOADtar -xvf patch_624940_R2010a.tarcp -f $ARCH/*iqm* $MATLAB/bin/$ARCH/where $ARCH is:glnx86 for 32-bit Linuxglnxa64 for 64-bit Linuxmaci for 32-bit Macintoshmaci64 for 64-bit Macintosh

Summary: Adding a java class with name 'i' to MATLAB causes a Java exception or other problems
Description: Adding a java class with name 'i' hides the use of the MATLAB built-in function i, and is likely to break many aspects of MATLAB.  However, MATLAB should respond with clean errors, and a Java exception or segmentation fault.
Workaround: Do not hide the names of MATLAB primitives with any function, whether written in MATLAB, via MEX files, or from Java.

Summary: Generating code for a model that contains a dynamic object mode Softing CAN Send block for PCI or PC/104 might cause compile errors
Description: Generating code for a model that contains a dynamic object mode Softing CAN Send block for PCI or PC/104 might cause compile errors. In particular, this occurs if the Show status output ports  check box is not selected for this block.
Workaround: As a workaround, ensure that the Show status output ports check box is always selected before generating code for the model.

Summary: Extended characters typed or pasted into a nodesktop instance of MATLAB running in a xterm causes infinite loop of prompts to be displayed.
Description: This issue was originally found on the Mac but the problem is also reproducible on Linux too.  When running MATLAB with the -nodesktop option within an xterm MATLAB is incorrectly handling extended characters when they are typed directly or pasted into the xterm.  As MATLAB tries to process the extended characters it ends up going into an infinite loop and keeps displaying the command prompt.  The only way break out of the loop is to type ctrl-z and then kill the MATLAB process.
Workaround: There currently is not a work around for the problem.

Summary: xlsread performance regresses for some files
Description: For some configurations of data, xlsread  reads files more slowly than in previous releases. An optimization added for one case caused a regression for some others.
Workaround: In xlsread.m, replace existing nested helper function TrimTrailingNaNs with the code below:%Helper function to trim trailing rows function TrimTrailingNaNs(stride)  while lastrow>=firstrow + stride - 1      dataSection = data(lastrow - (stride-1):lastrow,:);     if ~all(cellfun(@isnumeric,dataSection(:)))          break;     elseif ~all(isnan([dataSection{:}]))         break;     end      lastrow = lastrow-stride;  end  end

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier fails to translate models when the Real-Time Workshop system target file of the model is missing from the MATLAB path.
Description: Simulink Design Verifier fails to translate models when the Real-Time Workshop system target file of the model is missing from the MATLAB path. This failure generally happens for models using custom system target files for Real-Time Workshop.
Workaround: Copy the Real-Time Workshop system target file for the model to a folder in the MATLAB path. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Gear Constraint block sometimes fails to issue error if gear pitch circles are not in contact
Description: The Gear Constraint block requires that the pitch circles of its two gears be in contact, within the linear assembly tolerance of the model. If this criterion is not met, the model should issue an error. However, in some cases when this criterion is not met, and an error should be issued, the error is not issued. This failure can cause a model containing a Gear Constraint to produce incorrect simulation results.
Workaround: In the Machine Environment block of the machine containing the Gear Constraint, reset the Linear assembly tolerance parameter to a smaller value.The preferred workaround, for a linear assembly tolerance of linearTolerance, is to reset the linear assembly tolerance to linearTolerance^2 instead.

Summary: Function lcolorbar errors when validating 'ColorAlignment' and 'Location' property values
Description: Function lcolorbar  errors if you include property name-value pairs for the 'ColorAlignment'  or 'Location'  properties.
Workaround: To work around this problem, replace the lcolorbar.m function file in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapdisp/lcolorbar.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: lcolorbar.m.oldCopy the version of lcolorbar.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Requirements stored in DOORS can produce errors or false reports of missing files in model manifest
Description: Generating a manifest for a model that contains requirements stored in DOORS can produce errors (in versions prior to R2010a) or false reports of missing files (in R2010a).
Workaround: To avoid these problems, in the "Generate Manifest" dialog, clear the option to "Find requirements documents".

Summary: Using the pragma "preelaborate" can cause verification to stop
Description: When using the pragma "preelaborate " in an Ada verification, verification can stop with the following error message: ================================================= rte-gnat1 fatal error (Assert_Failure)== Detected at <source_file.ada>:15:4=============================================== N_Object_Declaration (Node_Id=1482) (source)Sloc = 928 src.ada:15:4Defining_Identifier = N_Defining_Identifier "<identifier>" (Entity_Id=<Id>)Aliased_Present = TrueObject_Definition = N_Expanded_Name "<Name>" (Node_Id=1486) compilation abandoned  ?
Workaround: Comment out the pragma "preelaborate" in the source file, where the problem occurs.The source file is given in the header of the error message:================================================= rte-gnat1 fatal error (Assert_Failure)== Detected at <source_file.ada>:15:4===============================================Please note also that a patch is available for the 2008b release. Please contact the support that will provide you a link to download it.

Summary: CSH for TLM Generation does not work in Model Explorer.
Description: CSH for TLM Generation does not work in Model Explorer.
Workaround: If you want to use CSH with TLM generation, set the TLM generation options using Simulink Configuration Parameters.

Summary: CSH for FPGA Project Generation does not work in the Simulink Configuration Parameters dialog.
Description: CSH for FPGA Project Generation does not work in the Simulink Configuration Parameters dialog because it was not supposed to be activated for 10a, but it was. Users will see a "What's this?" icon when they right-click on an option. However, users will not get a CSH window and they will see an error in the MATLAB command window.

Summary: Error compiling using Deployment Tool
Description: Compiling using Deployment Tool generates the following error message:  Internal error: Zero length license information.  The error is only seen when MATLAB is started with -c to point to a license server or a license file.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, use the mcccommand for compilation.

Summary: Get Attribute and Set Attribute blocks cause connection lines to become disconnected
Description: When you perform any of the following dialog box operations in the Get Attribute or Set Attribute block, connection lines from the block become disconnected. Get Attribute Block Dialog Box Operations  Add an attribute to the table Remove an attribute from the table Change the When Attribute Is Missing  or Default Value  parameter value  Set Attribute Block Dialog Box Operations  Add an attribute to the table Remove an attribute from the table Change the Value From  or Value  parameter value
Workaround: Rename the following file by appending .old to the following file names: $MATLAB\toolbox\des\desblks\@DES\@GetAttribute\maskCallback.p$MATLAB\toolbox\des\desblks\@DES\@SetAttribute\maskCallback.p$MATLAB is the MATLAB root folder on your machine, as returned by entering this command in the MATLAB Command Window: matlabrootDownload the attached file, ga_maskCallback.p, and save it as maskCallback.p in this folder: $MATLAB\toolbox\des\desblks\@DES\@GetAttributeDownload the attached file, sa_maskCallback.p, and save it as maskCallback.p in this folder: $MATLAB\toolbox\des\desblks\@DES\@SetAttributeIn the MATLAB Command Window, enter: clear classesrehash toolboxcache

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier inconsistently generates incompatibility message for proof or test objectives associated with signals that have more than 100 elements.
Description: Simulink Design Verifier inconsistently generates an incompatibility message for proof or test objectives associated with signals that have more than 100 elements. If Simulink Design Verifier succeeds in detecting signals that have more than 100 elements, it generates the following message: "This model contains loops with more than 100 iterations where each iteration has a test or proof objectives. These loops are not supported". However, the analysis cannot always detect when signals have more than 100 elements. ?

Summary: Examining Ada checks in PolySpace Viewer produces message 'Internal error: unable to find check'
Description: The software may produce an error when you investigate Ada checks using the PolySpace Viewer. This error, which occurs most frequently when you investigate checks in nested procedures, produces the following message: 'Internal error: unable to find check <name of the check>' ? ?
Workaround: The following workaround is for R2010a only:Navigate to the folder %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\Viewer\bin ($POLYSPACE_COMMON/Viewer/bin on a UNIX system).Create a backup for the files rte-displayer.jar, rte-ihme-if.jar, rte-writer.jar, rte-ihme-verifier.jar, and rte-reader.jar.Download the attachment '624188_R2010a.zip'.Extract all files to the folder specified in step 1.?

Summary: A running instance of the IBM Rational DOORS application is not detected in 64-bit Microsoft Windows hosts.
Description: When you try to navigiate a requirement link to a DOORS object or you try to synchronize a Simulink model with a surrogate module in DOORS, you get the following warning: "DOORS is not running. Please start DOORS and retry", when the DOORS client is running and operational. Restarting the client does not help.
Workaround: Back up the file [MATLAB INSTALL DIR]/toolbox/slvnv/reqmgt/reqmgt.mexw64 and replace it with the attached file. Then restart MATLAB.

Summary: Signal object initial value of '[]' in a Normal mode referenced model leads to memory allocation error
Description: Running model([],[],[],'compile')  produces memory allocation error when all of the following conditions apply: The model contains a function-call referenced model. The referenced model is set to run in Normal mode. The referenced model uses a signal object with an initial value of '[]' .
Workaround: Set the initial value of the signal object to empty ('') instead of '[]'.

Summary: Slow performance or hang when dragging mouse to select several blocks/states in Simulink or Stateflow
Description: Trying to select several blocks or states in Simulink and Stateflow may cause very slow performance or even software hangs. This is usually accompanied by flickering in the window. This has only been observed on Windows Vista or Windows 7.
Workaround: Turn off the Aero theme in Windows Vista or Windows 7.Users have reported that this helps address the problem.

Summary: Adding a natural frequency design requirement in the SISO Design Tool produces an error
Description: In the SISO Design Tool, adding a natural frequency design requirement to a root locus editor design plot or a pole/zero map analysis plot that is displaying a discrete-time system produces an error: ??? Error using ==> subsasgn No public field Frequency exists for class editconstr.NaturalFrequency.  ?
Workaround:
Follow these steps to apply the patch.Exit MATLAB?.Rename $MATLABROOT	oolbox\control\ctrlguis\@sisogui\@rleditor
ewconstr.m to $MATLABROOT	oolbox\control\ctrlguis\@sisogui\@rleditor
ewconstr.m.old. To locate $MATLABROOT, enter matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.Rename $MATLABROOT	oolbox\shared\controllib\graphics\@resppack\@pzplot
ewconstr.m to $MATLABROOT	oolbox\shared\controllib\graphics\@resppack\@pzplot
ewconstr.m.old.Save the attached file EBRg623843.zip to $MATLABROOT.Run the following commands at the MATLAB prompt:cd(matlabroot) unzip('EBRg623843.zip')Restart MATLAB.Run the following command at the MATLAB prompt:  rehash toolboxcache?

Summary: packNGo issues "File does not exist" error for a model containing referenced models that generate shared utilities
Description: The packNGo  function might incorrectly generate an error for a model if the following conditions are true: The model contains model references. The referenced models are configured to generate utility functions in a shared location.  Under these conditions, if you invoke packNGo  to package the generated code for the top model, packNGo  reports an error similar to the following: ??? Error using ==> RTW.makertw.PCGHook at 39Error encountered while executing PostCodeGenCommand: File "rt_look.h" does not exist.Error in ==> rtw_c at 1029    PCGHook(h);Error in ==> RTW.makertw.make_rtw at 275              rtw_c(h,...Error in ==> make_rtw at 22buildResult = h.make_rtw(varargin{:});Error in ==> build_target>RunBuildCmd at 86      buildResult = feval(buildCmdFcn, iBuildArgs, buildCmdArgs);Error in ==> build_target at 6  [varargout{1:nargout}] = feval(iSubFcn, varargin{1:end});Error in ==> build_model_reference_target at 48      build_target('RunBuildCmd', iMdl, iBuildArgs);Error in ==> update_model_reference_targets>locUpdateMdlRefTarget at 870      [rtwCodeWasUptodate] = build_model_reference_target(mdl, iBuildArgs);Error in ==> update_model_reference_targets>locUpdateAllMdlRefTargets at 595                  [lvlStatus(i),lvlReason{i}] =...Error in ==> update_model_reference_targets>loc_update_model_reference_targets at 341    [status,reason] = locUpdateAllMdlRefTargets(iMdl,...Error in ==> update_model_reference_targets>do_update_model_reference_targets at 109  [oTopStatus] = ...Error in ==> update_model_reference_targets at 47    topStatus = do_update_model_reference_targets(iMdl, iBuildArgsMdlRef);
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Performing an indexed assignment into an array that came from a cell element may result in an incorrect answer.
Description: When using the result of an index into cell or struct, MATLAB may incorrectly assume that the result is not shared with the cell (or struct) contents.  This can lead to incorrect behavior where two separate arrays are modified by a single assignment.The following program should return 5 instead of 10:   function r=bug    x{1} = 1;    x{1}(1)=5;    tmp = x{1};    tmp(end)=10;    r = x{1}(1);
Workaround: Use the following command in the function causing the problem to disable the JIT for that function:   feature scopedAccelEnablement offHere is the function shown above, but with the work-around in place:    function r=bug    feature scopedAccelEnablement off    x{1} = 1;    x{1}(1)=5;    tmp = x{1};    tmp(end)=10;    r = x{1}(1);

Summary: C6747EVM/C6748EVM ADC/DAC blocks create run-time assertion error when sampling rate is set to 8KHz
Description: When the sampling frequency parameter of C6747EVM/C6748EVM ADC or DAC blocks is set to 8KHz, an assertion error is reported at run-time.
Workaround: Download the attached patch installer, patch_installer.m, and the ZIP archive, patch_623024.zip.Start MATLAB and enter the following command at the prompt to install the patch:patch_installer('patch_623024.zip', 'install')Follow instructions printed out on the MATLAB command line to complete the installation.The attached patch modifies the following files in your MATLAB installation:<matlabroot>\resources\TIC6000\en\blocks.xml<matlabroot>\toolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic6000\blks\masks\c6747evm_aic31_sampleRate.m<matlabroot>\toolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic6000\blks\masks\c6748evm_aic31_sampleRate.m<matlabroot>\toolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic6000\blks\lct\src\c6747evm_aic31.c<matlabroot>\toolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic6000\blks\lct\src\c6748evm_aic31.c

Summary: Opening a Simulink model containing a function-call Model block may cause a segmentation violation
Description: Opening a Simulink model that meets both  these conditions causes a segmentation violation: Contains a Model block that references a function-call model Has the Model block version mismatch  or Port and parameter mismatch  diagnostic set to Error   These two diagnostics are in the Configuration Parameters  > Diagnostics  > Model Referencing  pane.
Workaround: Use load_system instead of opening the model.Once you have loaded the model into memory, perform one of these steps:Save the model in the current release. Note that you must save the model file which actually contains the Model block. If the Model block is in a library, load that library and resave it in the current release.Turn the Model block version mismatch and Port and parameter mismatch diagnostics to either None or Warning.

Summary: S-Function Builder fails to build the S-Function if there is a sub-bus on 10th element of the bus being used
Description: S-Function Builder does not build the S-Function if there is a sub-bus on the 10th element of the bus that is being used. In this case, the S-Function Builder does not generate any code and no error message is displayed.

Summary: SIL block might not work correctly with variable-size signal
Description: Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder software might give an incorrect value for the valueDimension  field of a variable-size signal when all of the following conditions are met: You generated a SIL block for a subsystem The Create Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder SIL block  parameter is selected on the Generate S-function dialog box. The outport of the subsystem has a variable-size signal. The subsystem outport is a multi-word fixed-point data type and it has more than 1 dimension.  ?

Summary: Documentation example file for Simulink Fixed Point did not get installed in R2010a
Description: The following Simulink Fixed Point model is referenced from the 'Using the Fixed-Point Tool to Autoscale Results from Multiple Simulations' example in the documentation, but it did not get installed for R2010a: fpt_merge_demo.mdl  The associated documentation reference is web([docroot '/toolbox/fixpoint/ug/bsehkm2.html']) .
Workaround: Download the attached ZIP archive and extract fpt_merge_demo.mdl.

Description: The following Embedded MATLAB .m, .mdl, and .mat files are referenced from the documentation, but they are not installed with R2010a: Embedded MATLAB Getting Started Tutorial: Generating MEX Functions  Euclidean  example files - web([docroot '/toolbox/eml/gs/bsc7o2q-9.html']): build01.m build02.m build03.m build04.m euclidean01.m euclidean02.m euclidean03.m euclidean04.m test01.m test02.m test03.m test04.m test05.m euclidean.mat  ? Summary: DM6437EVM Audio ADC/DAC blocks stop working after a while when used with Ethernet blocks
Description: The DM6437EVM Audio ADC/DAC audio driver blocks stop working when you use them in a model with the following Ethernet blocks: TCP/IP Send TCP/IP Receive UDP Send UDP Receive

Summary: Toggling Manual Switch block during simulation sometimes issues an error
Description: When a Manual Switch block is used in a model, toggling the manual switch issues an error when the following conditions are true: Simulation is running or is paused.'Block reduction' in the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box is selected.The Switch block in the Manual Switch block's masked subsystem is reduced.

Summary: The GETGEODATA, GEOSERIESREAD, and GEOSOFTREAD functions fail to read GEO data into MATLAB due to a limited buffer size in the TEXTSCAN function.
Description: The Bioinformatics Toolbox functions GETGEODATA , GEOSERIESREAD , and GEOSOFTREAD  rely on the TEXTSCAN  function to import the data into the MATLAB Workspace. Some data files from the GEO database contain rows that can not be held in the default buffer size of the TEXTSCAN  function (for example, data files with more than 1000 genes (or features) per row).
Workaround: Download the attached files.Quit MATLAB.Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\geosoftread.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\geosoftread.m.old and rename the file$MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\geoseriesread.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\geoseriesread.m.old, where $MATLAB$ is the directory where MATLAB is installed on your machine.Copy the downloaded files to the directory $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray .Restart MATLAB.?

Summary: The Amplifier block in the Mathematical library inaccurately models noise level
Description: When performing noise analysis, the Amplifier block in the Mathematical library mishandles the value specified by the 'Linear gain (dB)'  parameter, producing the wrong answer.
Workaround: Open MATLAB, and run the following script once in the MATLAB Command Window:sub_lib = 'rfmathmodels1';block = 'rfmathmodels1/Amplifier/Gain1';open_system(sub_lib);set_param(sub_lib, 'Lock', 'off');set_param(block, 'Gain', 'kT*c1^2');get_param(block, 'Gain')save_system(sub_lib)set_param(sub_lib, 'Lock', 'on')close_system(sub_lib);rehash toolboxcacheThe script permanently repairs the problem with noise analysis in the mathematical amplifier.

Summary: Third-party OpenMP programs may run on a single core if they are invoked using the exclamation point (!) operator.
Description: Certain third-party programs that can normally utilize multiple cores may run on a single core when invoked in MATLAB? using the exclamation point (!) operator. These programs will run in parallel on multiple cores when they are invoked directly from the operating system.
Workaround: Before invoking the third-party program, use the setenv command to set the OMP_NUM_THREADS environment variable to the number of cores you wish the program to use. For example, the following code sets my_third_party_program to use 4 cores.setenv('OMP_NUM_THREADS','4');!my_third_party_program

Summary: Labels for the columnames and rownames properties for the figure created by the clustergram or HeatMap functions are not visible, even after zooming in.
Description: There is a limit of 200 horizontal or vertical labels that can appear in a clustergram  or HeatMap  figure. Beyond this number, the labels are automatically removed to prevent overcrowding. However, zooming sufficiently on the figure should display the labels, but currently does not.
Workaround: Download the attached file.Quit MATLAB.Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\@HeatMap\plot.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\@HeatMap\plot.m.old, where $MATLAB$ is the directory where MATLAB is installed on your machine.Copy the downloaded file to the directory $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\@HeatMapRestart MATLAB.?

Summary: Verification fails with a "signal 11" error when analyzing 'for' loops containing both a declaration for the index and a break
Description: Verification stops with a "signal 11" error when the code being verified contains a 'for' loop with both a declaration for the index and a break. For example: int main(void) {   for(int a1 = 1; a1 < 2; a1++)   {   break;   }  return 0; }

Summary: In the aitoff, hammer, and vgrint1 projections, map frame longitude limits are no longer restricted
Description: It does not make sense for the frame limits of the following projections to become asymmetric: Aitoff Modified Azimuthal Projection (aitoff ), Hammer Modified Azimuthal Projection (hammer ), and Van Der Grinten I Polyconic Projection (vgrint1 ). These projections are intended to show the entire Earth with the origin at the center of the map. Through R2008a, any changes that would reset the frame limits outside the interval [-180 180] were quietly ignored. In R2008b, this limitation was unintentionally removed. Without it, it is possible to create maps that fold back on themselves such that pairs of distinct points on the Earth map to single points in the map plane.
Workaround: It should not be difficult to work around this problem simply by taking care when setting the MapLonLimit and FLonLimit properties of map axes that use these projections. But for a more complete workaround, replace the aitoff.m function file (for the Aitoff Modified Azimuthal Projection) in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapproj/aitoff.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: aitoff.m.oldCopy the version of aitoff.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Repeat the previous two steps for the Hammer Modified Azimuthal Projection (hammer.m) and Van Der Grinten I Polyconic Projection (vgrint1.m).Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Using linmod or dlinmod with a Simulink model that contains Integrator block with initial conditions port driven by root level Inport block produces incorrect results
Description: Using the [A,B,C,D] = linmod(model,[],INLEVEL)  syntax of the linmod  or dlinmod  commands with a Simulink model results in an initial condition value of zero for the integrator, instead of the level INLEVEL . This happens when the model: Has an integrator with external initial conditions (IC) port. The IC port is driven by a root level input port.

Summary: When called repeatedly, a number of Optimization Toolbox solvers take longer to finish than in earlier releases.
Description: A number of solvers take longer to finish than in earlier releases, due to overhead in forming enhanced exit messages. This overhead is noticeable in repeated calls to the optimization solver (e.g., when the solver is called inside a loop) when each optimization run takes very little time. Specifically, the fmincon , fminunc , fminimax , fseminf , and fgoalattain  solvers slowed in release R2009a, and the lsqnonlin , lsqcurvefit , and fsolve  solvers slowed in R2009b. The overhead occurs because of an extra call to the optimset  function when forming the enhanced exit messages.

Summary: The Find Files tool displays incorrect count of matching files
Description: When using the Find Files tool to perform a search that includes subdirectories, the displayed count of matching files might be incorrect.
Workaround: Use this workaround with MATLAB? Version 7.10 (R2010a) only. Do not use it with any other version of MATLAB.Download the attached zip file to your matlabroot/java folder (where matlabroot is the location on your file system returned by running the matlabroot function in MATLAB).Unzip the file using the MATLAB unzip function.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Performance regression in bwdist when two output arguments requested
Description: If you specify two output arguments to bwdist , i.e., [D, L] = bwdist(bw) , there is a performance regression relative to previous releases. Note that calling bwdist  with a single output argument, i.e., D = bwdist(bw) , is still significantly faster in R2009b than in previous releases.
Workaround: To achieve the same level of performance shown prior to R2009b, use [D, L] = bwdist_old(bw). Be sure to note that the case with a single output argument, D = bwdist(bw) is still significantly faster than D = bwdist_old(bw).

Summary: TLC %createrecord directive might create record array incorrectly
Description: The %createrecord  directive might create a record array (array of records) incorrectly if all of the following conditions are met: %createrecord  directive is called within a TLC multiple inclusion directive such as %for , %foreach , and %roll  %createrecord  creates a record array, where there is more than one element of the array
Workaround: Move the %createrecord statement outside of the enclosing multiple inclusion directive. If the array of records is updated and used only within each iteration, make a copy of the record array at the beginning of each iteration using %copyrecord.

Summary: Using File > Load in a Signal Constraint block does not load a saved response optimization project correctly
Description: When you use File > Load  in a Signal Constraint block to load a saved response optimization project, the underlying data gets corrupted and the saved project is not loaded correctly. The following steps illustrate one scenario when this issue occurs: Create and save a new response optimization project. Change the design requirements. Save the project by overwriting the existing project. Change the design requirements. Load the optimization project you saved in step 3 using File > Load . Change the design requirements. Save the project by overwriting the existing project. Load the project you saved in the previous step using File > Load .  The Signal Constraint block does not show the updated design requirements you specified in step 6 but continues to show the design requirements you specified in step 4.
Workaround: There are two ways to work around this issue:Option 1: Close and reopen the Simulink model to correctly load the saved optimization project.Option 2: Download the updated files:1. Exit MATLAB.2. Make a backup copy of the following files:$MATLABROOT\toolbox\sldo\sloptim\sloptguis\@srogui\@SignalConstraintDialog\targetEditor.m$MATLABROOT\toolbox\sldo\sloptim\sloptguis\@srogui\@SignalConstraintEditor\initialize.m3. Download the attached files.4. Copy the files into the folders specified in step 2.5. Restart MATLAB.6. Run the following command at the MATLAB prompt:rehash toolboxcache?

Summary: Using hdf5write for compound HDF5 types containing strings can cause segmentation violation.
Description: Using hdf5write  to write a hdf5.h5compound  data type containing a string can cause a segmentation violation.  To illustrate, the following example uses the hdf5.h5compound  function to create an HDF5 compound data type, timestamp . One of the members of this compound data type is a string. The example assigns values to the string component, using the setMember  function, and then tries to write the compound data type to an HDF5 file. %% Initialize timestampscurTime = now;timestamp = hdf5.h5compound;addMember(timestamp,'num');addMember(timestamp,'string');setMember(timestamp, 'num', uint32(floor(curTime*86400)));setMember(timestamp, 'string', hdf5.h5string(datestr(curTime)));%% Write Timestampshdf5write('./MyFile.h5', '/timestamp', timestamp);
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the appropriate file and replace the version of the file in your existing MATLAB installation folder, following this procedure:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file(s) $MATLABROOT/toolbox/matlab/imagesci/private/hdf5writec.mex$MEXEXTwhere $MATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB installation folder, as returned by the matlabroot function, and where $MEXEXT represents the file extension for your platform, as returned by the mexext function. For example, on a 64-bit Linux machine, copy hdf5writec.mexa64 to hdf5writec.mexa64.old.Download and unzip the attached zip archive file corresponding to your MATLAB release version.Copy the file that is specific to your platform (also identified by mexext) into the folders specified in the step 2.Restart MATLAB.Run the     rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Unusual map axes properties can result in corrupted map frames
Description: With pseudocylindrical projections, such as sinusoidal, for example, it is customary to have the origin at the center of the map. But is is possible to shift the origin well off center. The most direct way to do this would be to set the FLonLimit  property of the map axes to asymmetric values ([0 360]  for example, instead of [-180 180] ). Another way is to specify both the Origin  property and the MapLonLimit  property when constructing the map axes, with values such that the origin longitude is not centered in the longitude limits. A third way is to reset the MapLonLimit  with setm  after constructing the map axes. In all these cases, the asymmetric frame that results may be incorrect and this will be very obvious when the map axes Frame  property is 'on ' . Another problem with map frames and unusual property settings arises in the case of azimuthal projections such as the orthographic (ortho ) and equidistant azimuthal (eqdazim ) projections. These projections use a convention in which the first element of the FLatLimit  property is set to -Inf  and the second element equals the radius of the circular frame (as a spherical distance in degrees or radians). If the first element is set to some finite value, it should be ignored, but this isn't the case when constructing a map frame, and a quadrangular frame can result instead of a circular frame. (In addition, the FLonLimit  property, which should be left at its default value of [-180 180]  is then used to set the longitude extent of the quadrangle.)
Workaround: When using a cylindrical, conic, or other non-azimuthal projection, you can avoid this problem most of the time by ensuring that your origin falls on the central meridian of your map, so that your frame limits extend an equal distance to each side. When using an azimuthal projection, you should ensure that the first element of the FLatLimit property is set to -Inf and that the FLonLimit property has the value [-180 180].

Summary: Using single colon indexing on the left hand side of an assignment may lead to an incorrect answer.
Description: Using single colon indexing on both the right and left hand sides of an assignment may lead to an incorrect answer, e.g.a(:) = b(:);
Workaround: Do not use single colon indexing unless it is necessary. For example, use   a = b;instead of either of the following:   a(:) = b(:);   a(:) = b;

Summary: When calling notify on an array of objects, the source argument passed to the event callback can be the wrong object if you are using custom EventData.
Description: For handle  classes that use events, the event callback is passed two arguments: the object that is the source of the event and event data in the form of an EventData  class instance. When notify  is invoked on an array of objects (i.e., a source array), the callback is invoked once for each object in the source array, and each object is passed in turn to the callback along with an EventData  instance. When using custom event data (i.e. a subclass of the event.EventData  class), the callback is invoked once for each of the objects in the source array, but every call to notify  is passed only the first object, not each object in turn. Likewise, the Source  property of the custom event.EventData  object is always set to the first object of the source array.
Workaround: When working with custom event data, rather than call notify on an array, iterate over the source object array to call notify separately on each individual element.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: A model with more than one level of Model Reference hierarchy in Normal mode may not correctly identify identifier clashes
Description: When generating Real-Time Workshop code for a model that has more than one level of Model Reference hierarchy, identifier clashes between the topmost model and the lowest level model may not be correctly identified if all the models are referenced in Normal mode As a result of this, one or both of the following may happen: The generated code may fail to compile. Global variables from a Simulink.Signal  or Simulink.Parameter  object may be defined multiple times which can cause incorrect results.
Workaround: To detect whether this bug affects your model, download the two attached files and run the detection script at the MATLAB command prompt:detectG619966(model)where model is the name of your model.To workaround this bug, set the Simulation Mode of all of the Model blocks in your top model to Accelerator.

Summary: TreeBagger fails if called with 'nprint' and with 'UseParallel' set to 'always', but with no MATLAB pool
Description: To run the TreeBagger  function in parallel mode, you first use the matlabpool  command to set up a MATLAB pool of parallel workers, then you run TreeBagger  with the 'UseParallel'  parameter set to 'always' . If you don't set up a pool beforehand, TreeBagger  runs each of its iterations on the main (client) MATLAB rather than on a parallel worker. However, if you also specify the 'nprint'  parameter to request progress reports on how many trees have been generated, the TreeBagger  function errors.
Workaround: When no matlabpool is running, do not use the 'UseParallel' parameter and the 'nprint' parameter in the same command.

Summary: NNTool buttons dissapear after minimization in Win Vista
Description: If after creating new data in NNTool the user minimizes the window, then maximizes it again (by clicking its minimized tab in the toolbar or typeing "nntool" at the MATLAB command line) the window reappears but without its buttons.
Workaround: The only workaround, while a fix is being implemented, is to not minimize NNTool in this situation.

Summary: Segmentation violation during code generation using AUTOSAR target and Stateflow machine parented data
Description: Real-Time Workshop code generation might produce a segmentation violation under the following conditions: On the Real-Time Workshop  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the System target file  parameter is set to autosar.tlc  The model contains Stateflow charts that use machine parented data
Workaround: Before generating code, type the following at the MATLAB prompt:>> sf('Feature', 'CoderUnificationSupportMachineData', 0)

Summary: MZXMLREAD returns a Java Exception when reading an MZXML formatted file containing empty scans with no data.
Description: MZXMLREAD  returns an error when reading an MZXML formatted file containing empty scans. Empty scans are XML elements with the tag SCAN  and with a child with the tag PEAKS . Usually the PEAKS  element contains the mass spectrometer trace data. However, there are edge cases in which the spectrometer creates a trace (a PEAKS  element), but does not capture any data. In this case, MZXMLREAD returns a Java Exception of type java.lang.NullPointerException .
Workaround: Download the attached file.Quit MATLAB.Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\mass_spec\mzxmlread.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\mass_spec\mzxmlread.m.old, where $MATLAB$ is the directory where MATLAB is installed on your machine.Copy the downloaded file to the directory $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\mass_specRestart MATLAB.?

Summary: mmreader returns the same video frames when reading some WMV files in a loop on Windows systems
Description: On Windows systems, mmreader  returns the same video frames when reading some WMV files in a loop.For example, given a file myfile.wmv , this code sometimes generates an exception:obj = mmreader('myfile.wmv');for ii = 1:2  vidFrame{ii} = read(obj,ii);endassert( ~isequal(vidFrame{1}, vidFrame{2}) );

Summary: Code generation fails for model with virtual buses entering or leaving variant Model blocks
Description: Code generation can fail with a segmentation fault for a model with virtual buses that enter or leave a variant Model block.
Workaround: Place bus conversion blocks at any ports of a variant Model block that reads from, or writes to, a virtual bus:At a Model block inport that reads from a virtual bus, place a Signal Conversion block and set its Output parameter to Nonvirtual bus. At a Model block outport that writes to a virtual bus, place a Signal Conversion block and set its Output parameter to Virtual bus.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when generating code for a model specifying preprocessor conditionals for variant Model blocks
Description: MATLAB crashes when generating code for a model implementing model reference variants if you specify a variant object as follows: On the Model Variants block parameter dialog, the Simulink.Variant  object Condition  is defined using a Simulink.Parameter  object. The Simulink.Parameter  object is specified with a Storage class  that is a custom storage class reference.  ?
Workaround: Do not set the storage class of a Simulink.Parameter object, which is referenced by the Condition of a Simulink.Variant object, to a custom storage class that is a custom storage class reference. Use the shipping ImportedDefine(custom) or CompilerFlag(custom) storage classes. If you need to create your own custom storage class, do not define it to be a reference to another custom storage class.

Summary: SimPowerSystems models with Powergui block placed in a subsystem may produce error or warnings messages, or deliver wrong simulation results
Description: When the Powergui block, with the Simulation type  parameter set to Discrete  or to Phasor , is placed into a subsystem, the model incorrectly simulates as continuous. This will cause the model to produce incorrect simulation results, or produce unexpected warnings or error messages. The Powergui block must be placed at the top level of block diagram to ensure correct simulation.
Workaround: To work around this issue, move the Powergui block to the top-level diagram.

Summary: Bioinformatics Toolbox function PDBSUPERPOSE incorrectly calculates the RMSD value.
Description: The Bioinformatics Toolbox function PDBSUPERPOSE incorrectly calculates the RMSD value when the PDB input file does not have a one-to-one correspondence between the atom coordinate information (ATOM fields) and the sequence residue information (SEQRES fields).
Workaround: ?1. Download the attached file.2. Quit MATLAB.3. Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\proteins\pdbsuperpose.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\proteins\pdbsuperpose.m.old, where $MATLAB$ is the directory where MATLAB is installed on your machine.4. Copy the downloaded file to the directory $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\proteins.5. Restart MATLAB.6. Type the following command at the MATLAB prompt:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Model Advisor check Check subsystem names always passes for masked subsystems
Description: The following Model Advisor check does not review names of masked subsystems, and therefore passes for any name of the masked subsystem: By Product > Simulink Verification and Validation > MathWorks Automotive Advisor Board Checks > Check subsystem names By Task > Modeling Standards for MAAB > Naming Conventions > Check subsystem names

Summary: Bloomberg Connection Server fails to start
Description: The Bloomberg Communications Server, bbcomm , fails to start automatically. Try entering the following command: b = blp;  You get this error message: ??? Error using ==> blp at 38Failed to start session.
Workaround: From a DOS prompt, type bbcomm or use the attached version of $MATLAB/toolbox/datafeed/datafeed/@blp/blp.p. Users on 64 bit Windows machines will have to start bbcomm manually from a DOS prompt.

Summary: MCRInstaller does not register mwcommgr.dll and mwcomutil.dll on Windows 7 and other platforms
Description: In R2009b, the 32- and 64-bit MCRInstaller does not run vcredist.exe  on Windows 7, as part of its pre-installation steps.In R2009b and R2010a, the 32-bit MCRInstaller fails to register mwcommgr.dll  and mwcomutil.dll  properly on Windows XP with later service packs, Vista, Windows 7 and Windows Server 2008.
Workaround: On Windows 7:Run the appropriate 32- or 64-bit VCREDIST.exeRun the MCRInstaller.On all 32-bit platforms, run regsvr32.exe to register mwcommgr.dll and mwcomutil.dll.On other 32-bit Windows platforms:Run the MCRInstallerRun regsvr32.exe to register mwcommgr.dll and mwcomutil.dll.

Summary: Errors in Command Window when typing in custom equation in SFTOOL
Description: Typing some characters into the Custom Equation edit control of the Surface Fitting Tool can incorrectly produce errors in the MATLAB Command Window. For example, typing the 'b ', in the Custom Equation edit control produces errors in the MATLAB command window such as:  No public field TolX exists for class curvefit.interpoptions. Error in ==> Fitdev>iTranslateCustomInputs at 790  fopts.(varargin{i}) = varargin{i+1}; The error occurs when your typing uniquely matches the start of a surface fitting model name from the list shown by cflibhelp . For example, 'b ' uniquely matches the start of the biharmonicinterp , which is a model type for surfaces. ?
Workaround: You can ignore these errors because they have no affect on the fits that you make. You can continue to use SFTOOL.

Summary: If a CTF archive, generated for a MATLAB Builder NE assembly, contains dependent libraries (DLLs), they are not available to an application consuming the assembly.
Description: Libraries packaged in the CTF archive need to be on the run-time path in order to be loaded by an application. MATLAB Builder NE ignores the library path during MCR initialization, causing library load failures. For example, if a MATLAB function is using certain Image Processing Toolbox functionality, the following error might be thrown: ??? Invalid MEX-file... imfiter_mex.mexw32The specified module could not be found.  In this case, the ippl.dll  is the missing module that gets packaged in the CTF archive.
Workaround: For R2010a, use the following workaround:Download MWArray_R2010a_32.zip for the 32 bit MWArray.dll and download MWArray_R2010a_64.zip for the 64 bit MWArray.dll. The ZIP file contains MWArray.dll and ManagedCPPAPI.netmodule.Backup MWArray.dll and ManagedCPPAPI.netmodule in MATLABROOT\toolbox\dotnetbuilder\bin\arch\v2.0 where arch is win32 or win64.Unzip the file to MATLABROOT\toolbox\dotnetbuilder\bin\arch\v2.0.Rebuild the assembly. This is required because the version of the patched MWArray.dll is 2.10.0.1 compared to 2.10.0.0, which is the version of the shipped MWArray.dll in R2010a.Replace the reference to MWArray.dll with this new MWarray.dll in any project that uses a MATLAB Builder NE assembly, built in R2010a.If your application depends upon MWArray.dll being in the GAC (Global Assembly Cache), use gacutil to install it there.For releases prior to R2010a ,copy the dependent DLL(s) to MCRROOT/bin/arch (for example, ippl.dll).

Summary: In the Model Advisor report, clicking the Run Advisor button report produces an error.
Description: In the Model Advisor report, clicking the Run Advisor  button produces an error with message Undefined function or method 'isempty'.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Using the Simulink Design Verifier Embedded MATLAB functions sldv.prove() and sldv.test() inside a Stateflow Truth Table block results in an error.
Description: Running property proving for a model that uses the Simulink Design Verifier Embedded MATLAB functions sldv.prove()  or sldv.test()  inside a Stateflow Truth Table block generates the following error: ??? Undefined function or variable "codeBlock".  Error in ==> get_eml_line_info at 21   lineNum = cv('CodeBloc','objLines',codeBlock, objCvId);  Error in ==> sldvprivate at 4   [varargout{1:nargout}] = feval(function_name, varargin{1:end});  Error in ==> DataUtils.save_data>storeLink at 236   lineInfo = sldvprivate('get_eml_line_info',sfId, cvId);  Error in ==> DataUtils.save_data at 42   linkStorage = storeLink(currentGoal, length(Objectives), linkStorage);  Error in ==> sldvRunAnalysis>generateData at 353   sldvData = Sldv.DataUtils.save_data(testcomp.analysisInfo.analyzedModelH);  Error in ==> sldvRunAnalysis at 147   [sldvData, fileNames] = generateData(testcomp, as, fileNames);
Workaround: To work around this problem, update the save_data.m file in your MATLAB installation:Locate the file in your MATLAB installation <MATLAB install>\toolbox\shared\sldv\+Sldv\@DataUtils\save_data.mRename the file (e.g., change its extension to be .old instead of .m).Copy the attachment that corresponds to your MATLAB version (R2009b or R2010a) into the folder that contains the renamed fileRename the copied file save_data.m.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Cannot generate code from CAN Unpack blocks when you use C compilers
Description: You cannot generate code from a CAN Unpack block when you use C compilers and you select additional output ports from the Function Block Parameters dialog box.
Workaround: Install the attached zip file to work around this issue.Log in to an account with permissions to overwrite files in the MATLAB installation directory.Locate your MATLAB installation directory by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB Command window.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file and store the file in the installation directory.Open the zip file.Create a backup for each of the files listed in the zip file.Extract the file to following path under your installation directory: toolbox\shared\can\canmex\tlc_c\ .Delete the downloaded .zip file.In case you encounter a Windows System Access denied error when you attempt to extract the zip file:Start WinZip in administrator mode.From WinZip, find and open the downloaded zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your installation directory.
