Summary: Embedded MATLAB generates C++ code that fails to compile due to a missing const qualifier.
Description: Embedded MATLAB does not propagate the const  qualifier for variable-sized matrices, producing C++ code that fails to compile. The error message looks like this: example.cpp(158) : error C2664: 'f' : cannot convert parameter 1 from 'const real_T []' to 'real_T []'Conversion loses qualifiers  Here is a simplified example of the C++ code that causes this compiler error: void f(real_T eml_v_data[10], int32 eml_v_sizes[1]);void g(const real_T x[10]){ int32_T sz[1]; sz[0] = ... f(x, sz); ...}  The error occurs because the const  qualifier is missing on the first argument of function f .
Workaround: There are two workarounds:Generate C code rather than C++. C compilers emit a warning message, rather than an error message.Manually add a const qualifier to the generated code.

Summary: PolySpace verification fails during compilation phase with the error message: "constant source operand of branch not 1 or 0"
Description: This behavior occurs most frequently on "&" bitwise operation in a conditional if expression. For example: void foo(void) { if (2 & 2) {} }  Verification stops at compilation phase with a message like the following: Verifying foo.c In function `foo': C:\foo.c, line <x>: Error: constant source operand of branch is 73006488, not 1 or 0 Warning: Failed compilation of foo.c  ? ? ?
Workaround: To avoid this problem, you must explicitly change the 'if' condition with a boolean expression like the following:void foo(void) { if ((2 & 2) != 0){} }

Summary: UDP interfaces do not allow sending or receiving of complete jumbo packets.
Description: Instrument Control Toolbox would not allow UDP datagram packets to contain more than 8192 bytes. To work around this limitation two new datagram packet size properties have been introduced that allow you to specify the size of the datagram packet. If the size is not specified, the behavior is backward compatible. echoudp('on', 5454);u=udp('localhost',5454);u.InputDatagramPacketSize=65000;u.OutputDatagramPacketSize=65000;fopen(u);fwrite(u,uint8(1:65000));
Workaround: For R2008a-R2010a, download G618091.zip and perform the following steps in MATLAB.cd(matlabroot);unzip('C:\TEMP\G618091.zip');Restart MATLAB to ensure the changes are enabled.?

Summary: The Draw Shapes block and the ShapeInserter System object draw incorrect polygon shapes when applying a fill operation to a polygon with co-linear vertices.
Description: The Draw Shapes  block and the ShapeInserter  System object draw incorrect polygon shapes. This occurs when you apply a fill operation to a polygon with co-linear vertices.
Workaround: For R2010a, follow these steps to correct this issue:1. Quit MATLAB?.2. Unzip the attached file, svipdrawshapes.zip, to the following location: $MATLABROOT/toolbox/vipblks/vipmex/3. Unzip the attached file, mvipdrawshapes.zip, to the following location: $MATLABROOT/toolbox/vipblks/viplibmex/3. Restart MATLAB?.If you are using a different version of MATLAB?, please contact MathWorks to obtain the replacement files.?

Summary: User-created Contents.m file not included in deployed applications, causes the MATLAB ver command to fail.
Description: Each MATLAB toolbox stores its official version number in a file called Contents.m . MATLAB Compiler includes Contents.m  files in deployed applications to support deploying MATLAB's ver  command. However, MATLAB Compiler only searches for Contents.m  files in directories located under the MATLAB root directory. Contents.m  files created in directories outside the MATLAB root directory are not be included in the deployed application. This can cause the ver  command to fail with the error message: No properly formatted Contents.m file was found for '[toolbox name here]'.

Summary: The jacobian function errors for numeric input
Description: The jacobian  function errors when passed a numeric input. For example, the function errors for the following input: jacobian(0, sym('x'))
Workaround: Convert the numeric input to a sym object explicitly:jacobian(sym(0),sym('x'))

Summary: hypergeom(1, 200, 1.0) returns an incorrect result
Description: The following function call produces the wrong result. In MATLAB: hypergeom(1, 200, 1.0) returns ans =-3.9299e+300 In MuPAD: hypergeom([1], [200], 1.0) returns 1.894311436e325  The correct result is 1.005024999 .
Workaround: In MuPAD, perturb the input parameterhypergeom([1 + 10^(-DIGITS)], [200], 1.0)or boost DIGITS:DIGITS:= 350:hypergeom([1], [200], 1.0)In MATLAB, there is no workaround.

Summary: Link to the PolySpace Installation Guide from the main PolySpace Products for C help page is not functioning
Description: When trying to open the PolySpace Installation Guide  from the main "PolySpace Products for C" help page (PolySpace C Documentation.pdf'),nothing happens. The link is not functionning.
Workaround: To work around this issue, rename the following file:"%POLYSPACE_COMMON%\Docs\PolySpace_Installation_Guide.pdf"to:"%POLYSPACE_COMMON%\Docs\PolySpace_Install.pdf"

Summary: sbionlinfit, sbionlmefit, and sbionlmefitsa incorrectly report errors when a PKData object is missing RateUnits and the model uses UnitConversion.
Description: SimBiology version 3.2 may incorrectly report an error that "OBSERVEDDATA.RateUnits must be specified for each rate column when simulating a model with UnitConversion on", even when the PKData object contains doses that do not specify a rate. Specifically, when using sbionlinfit , sbionlmefit , or sbionlmefitsa , the error will occur when all of the following conditions apply: The model is being used with UnitConversion; The PKData object contains multiple doses; and The PKData object specifies an empty string for one or more elements of the RateUnits property.
Workaround: NOTE: This fix applies only to customers running SimBiology R2010a version 3.2.Option 1: Specify valid rate units in every element of PKData.RateUnits, even for doses that do not specify a rate.Option 2: Download an updated version of the file pkdatacompile.p:Find the $MATLABROOT directory where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB? root directory on your machine, as returned by typing:     matlabrootat the MATLAB Command Prompt.Quit MATLAB.Rename the file pkdatacompile.p to pkdatacompile.p.old.This file is located in    $MATLABROOT/toolbox/simbio/simbio/privateDownload and unzip the attached file for your system.Place the new file, pkdatacompile.p, in the same directory mentioned in step 3.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Subystem analysis fails for subsystems that contain Data Store Read or Data Store Write blocks associated with Data Store Memory blocks within masked subsystems
Description: Simulink Design Verifier analysis can be invoked on Atomic Subsystems. If an Atomic Subsystem contains Data Store Read or Data Sore Write blocks associated with a Data Store Memory block under a masked subsystem inside that Atomic Subsystem hierarchy, the analysis fails.
Workaround: Back up matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv\private\mdl_get_dsrw_nodsm.m to a directory outside the MATLAB path.Save the attached file as matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv\private\mdl_get_dsrw_nodsm.m.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Box-Cox transformation plots for radial basis function models not visible
Description: If a radial basis function model fails to fit when generating a Box-Cox transformation plot, the entire plot fails to display.
Workaround: Replace the file sse_transform.m in the following location of your MATLAB installation folder with the attached version of the file:/toolbox/mbc/mbcmodels/@xregrbf/sse_transform.mThen, restart MATLAB.

Summary: Verification stops with the following error message:"Crash in C to IL : @FFT: Lack of cast between incompatible result type and first operand type (case 7)"
Description: During verification of an application, the process stops during the "C to Intermediate language" phase with the following message: **** C to intermediate language translation - 2 (P_RB) took 0real, 0u + 0s**** C to intermediate language translation - 3 (P_SIA)----------------------------------------------------------------------@FFT: Lack of cast between incompatible result type and first operand type (case 7) in PROC P.<procedure> (<procedure>  is the name of a procedure in the application) Then a few lines later: |An internal consistency problem has been detected.|In order to avoid generating incomplete verification information,|the computation has been halted.|Please complete a Bug Report at The MathWorks Support site|https://www.mathworks.com/support.|Please remember to attach the log file from this analysis to the report.  This kind of error is specific to the code, so it is not possible to give a small code example that reproduces the problem. ?
Workaround: If you see this message, use the option -c-extra-flags -no-suif-checker. This should resolve the problem, and has no impact on your results.To enable this option in a launching command, you can add it as any other option, but please note that it is a single option in two parts.To enable this option using the Launcher, go to PolySpace inner settings > Other options, then enter "-c-extra-flags -no-suif-checker"

Summary: The doc_timescope_tut model throws a run-time error.
Description: When you try to run the doc_timescope_tut  model from the Displaying Time-Domain Data s ection of the Signal Processing Blockset? User's Guide , you get the following error message: The variable Input sample time is not defined in the MATLAB workspace.  Using the old value of 0.
Workaround: To workaround this issue in R2010a, replace your existing doc_timescope_tut model with the version attached to this bug report. To do so, use the following procedure:Close the doc_timescope_tut model.Download the attached file and save it with the same name in the following folder:   MATLABROOT\help\toolbox\dspblks\examples Note: MATLABROOT is the path to the folder where MATLAB? is installed. To identify your MATLAB root, type matlabroot at the command line.You can now open and run the doc_timescope_tut model.

Summary: PolySpace verification incorrectly reports a red NTL check on a left shift instruction
Description: This behavior occurs most frequently on "for " loops that contain a left shift. For example: void foo(void)  {   unsigned char var;   for (var = 64; var >= 32; var = var >> 1) {}  }   ?
Workaround: Note that following workaround is only available for R2009b release.To workaround this issue on a Windows system:Browse to the folder: %POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\binCreate backup copies of the following files:.\x86_64-mingw32\iabc012.cx86_64-mingw32.exe .\x86-mingw32\iabc012.cx86-mingw32.exeDownload the attached file: 616986_Windows.zip.Extract the .exe files from the zip archive and copy them to the following sub-folders: .\x86-mingw32 .\x86_64-mingw32To workaround this issue on a Linux system:Browse to the folder: $POLYSPACE_C/Verifier/binCreate backup copies of the following files:./x86_64-linux/iabc012.cx86_64-linux ./x86-linux/iabc012.cx86-linuxDownload the attached file: 616986_Linux.zipExtract the -linux files from the zip archive and copy them to the following sub-folders: ./x86-linux ./x86_64-linuxTo workaround this issue on a Solaris system:Browse to the folder $POLYSPACE_C/Verifier/binCreate a backup copy of the following file: ./sparc-solaris/iabc012.csparc-solarisDownload the attached file: 616986_Solaris.zipExtract the -solaris file from the zip archive and copy it to the following sub-folder: ./sparc-solaris?

Summary: Incorrect mappings between latitude and y in the braun and bsam projections
Description: In both the braun (Braun Perspective Cylindrical) and bsam (Bolshoi Sovietskii Atlas Mira Cylindrical) projections, the forward and inverse mappings between latitude and the y-coordinate value in the map plane are incorrect, except when braun is used with its 'MapParallels' property set to the default value of 0. Otherwise, due to an implementation flaw in braun.m, points project to y-coordinate values that are too large in absolute value by a factor of 2/(1 + cos(lat1)), where lat1 is the latitude of the standard parallels in the case of the braun projection and 30 degrees in the case of bsam. (bsam is a special case of braun and encounters the same flaw in its codepath.) Remark: gstereo (Gall Stereographic Cylindrical Projection), a special case of braun, does not suffer from this problem because it is implemented independently of braun and follows a different codepath.
Workaround: To work around this problem, replace the braun.m function file in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapproj/braun.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: braun.m.oldCopy the version of braun.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Warning: Out of memory. Unable to allocate x,xxx,xxx,xxx bytes appears at start of level 0 and verification does not progress
Description: Sometimes, if you run a verification with a small C file, the analysis does not progress beyond the beginning of level 0: ...*** Beginning Software Safety Analysis Level 0 *** ********************************************************** **** Software Safety Analysis Level 0 - 1 (P_NS)  Occasionally, you see a warning: **** Software Safety Analysis Level 0 - 1 (P_NS) Warning: Out of memory. Unable to allocate x,xxx,xxx,xxx bytes.

Summary: Some channels of the MPL PATI board might not work correctly when accessed with the xPC Target PATI Digital Output block
Description: Some channels of the MPL PATI board might not work correctly when accessed with the xPC Target PATI Digital Output block. In this case, digital output channels will continue to output 0 volts, even when driven to output the high state. The set of digital output channels that fail depends on the specific MPL PATI board you have.
Workaround: Download the attached file and put it into the xpcblocks subdirectory. Then rebuild your model.

Summary: Separating multiple left hand side variables with a semicolon might terminate MATLAB.
Description: This following code can cause MATLAB to terminate on 64 bit platforms. The cause is the use of the semicolon to separate the multiple left hand side variables.    s = struct('f',{5 17}); [a(1); b(1)] = s.f
Workaround: Using a semicolon to separate multiple left hand side variables is incorrect MATLAB syntax, and is expected to cause a MATLAB error. Multiple left hand side variables should instead be separated by commas:   s = struct('f',{5 17}); [a(1), b(1)] = s.f

Summary: With Generate multicycle path information option selected, multicycle path analysis phase of code generation can take an exceptionally long time to complete if a large timing controller is generated.
Description: If you select Generate multicycle path information  to generate a multicycle path constraint file, HDL code generation may take a very long time to execute and may appear to hang MATLAB. This situation can arise under either or both the following circumstances: The coder creates a timing controller that has hundreds or thousands of states. (Note that a large timing controller can be created when the greatest common divisor of all system rates is a large number.) The model is a multirate design that has many levels of hierarchy.  The multicycle path analysis for such designs is complex and takes a long time to complete.
Workaround: To work around this issue you must replace the file MulticyclePathConstraintGenerator.p in your MATLAB installation with a new version as follows:1. Quit MATLAB.2. Delete MulticyclePathConstraintGenerator.p from your current MATLAB installation. This file is located in your MATLAB installation in the directory:MATLABROOT\toolbox\shared\hdlshared\+hdlconnectivity\@MulticyclePathConstraintGenerator3. Download the attached file, attachment_616541_20100309.zip.Unzip it to produce the file MulticyclePathConstraintGenerator.pMove the above file to the directory in step 2.4. Restart MATLAB.5. Type the following command at the MATLAB prompt:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Statistics on UNR checks are different in the Viewer and in the log file.
Description: The statistics on UNR checks reported in the log file are not same as those reported in the Viewer. This happens because the log file counts checks in standard stubs and in unreachable procedures. Right count is given by Viewer.

Summary: Verification stops in level 0 phase, with the message: "@FFT: * CSE: convergence failure on main loop"?
Description: Verification stops in level 0 phase, with the following error message: **** Software Safety Analysis Level 0 - 23 (P_IIP) took 0.1real, 0.1u + 0s (0gc)**** Software Safety Analysis Level 0 - 24 (P_CSE)----------------------------------------------------------------------@FFT: * CSE: convergence failure on main loop|An internal consistency problem has been detected.|In order to avoid generating incomplete verification information,|the computation has been halted.|Please complete a Bug Report at The MathWorks Support site|https://www.mathworks.com/support.|Please remember to attach the log file from this analysis to the report.  The reason for this error is specific to the code. If you see this message, please contact technical support, and send your code if possible, as we may provide a workaround.
Workaround: Note that following workaround is only available for R2010a release. Note that you only have to install the patch on the server side except if you are doing desktop verifications.To workaround this issue on a Windows system: Browse to the folder: %POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\bin Create backup copies of the following files: .\x86_64-mingw32\iabc-il.cx86_64-mingw32.exe and .\x86-mingw32\iabc-il.cx86-mingw32.exe Download the attached file: 2010-08-20-attachment-616495-r10a-server-win.zip. Extract the .exe files from the zip archive and copy them to the following sub-folders .\x86-mingw32 and .\x86_64-mingw32To workaround this issue on a Linux system: Browse to the folder: $POLYSPACE_C/Verifier/bin Create backup copies of the following files: ./x86_64-linux/iabc-il.cx86_64-linux and ./x86-linux/iabc-il.cx86-linux Download the attached file: 2010-08-20-attachment-616495-r10a-server-linux.zip Extract the -linux files from the zip archive and copy them to the following sub-folders: ./x86-linux and ./x86_64-linux

Summary: mmreader uses excess memory when reading large AVI files on Windows systems
Description: On Windows systems, reading many frames from a large AVI file with mmreader  requires significantly more memory in R2010a than in R2009b.  In some cases, mmreader  hangs MATLAB.For example, given longfile.avi  with more than 600 frames, this code uses more memory in R2010a than in R2009b:    obj = mmreader('longfile.avi')    % read in the first 600 frames    images = read(obj, [1 600]);
Workaround: Read video frames in a loop rather than in a single call to read. For example:    obj = mmreader('longfile.avi')     numFrames = 600;      % Pre-allocate an array of 600 video frames    images = zeros( obj.Height, obj.Width, 3, numFrames);     % Read in the frames one at a time    for ii=1:numFrames         images(:,:,:,ii) = read(obj,ii);     end

Summary: The MuPAD fucntion call transform::fourier(cos(w_0*t)*heaviside(t), t, w) returns the wrong result.
Description: The following code produces the wrong result in which the dirac terms are missing: transform::fourier(cos(w_0*t)*heaviside(t), t, w) assuming w_0 in R_ (w*I)/(w^2 - w_0^2) The correct result is (PI*dirac(w + w_0))/2 + (PI*dirac(w - w_0))/2 + (w*I)/(w^2 - w_0^2)

Summary: Working with MATLAB Builder NE MWArrays in a tight loop causes memory leak
Description: The following C# code presents an example of this leak:   int l = 80000;  MWNumericArray mwIn;  int n = 0;            while (!Console.KeyAvailable)  {      mwIn = new MWNumericArray(MWArrayComplexity.Complex,MWNumericType.Double, l);      for(int i = 1; i <= l; i++)      {          mwIn[i] = 2.0;          mwIn[MWArrayComponent.Imaginary, i] = 4.0;      }  } In general, MWArray  resources are not being freed causing the memory leak. Specifically in this example, the leak is caused by using the MWNumericArray  indexer (i.e.: mwIn[i] ) in a loop.
Workaround: There is no workaround for R2009b.For R2010a, use the following workaround:Download MWArray_R2010a_32.zip for the 32 bit MWArray.dll and download MWArray_R2010a_64.zip for the 64 bit MWArray.dll. The ZIP file contains MWArray.dll and ManagedCPPAPI.netmodule.Backup MWArray.dll and ManagedCPPAPI.netmodule in MATLABROOT\toolbox\dotnetbuilder\bin\arch\v2.0 where arch is win32 or win64.Unzip the file to MATLABROOT\toolbox\dotnetbuilder\bin\arch\v2.0.Rebuild the assembly. This is required because the version of the patched MWArray.dll is 2.10.0.1 compared to 2.10.0.0, which is the version of the shipped MWArray.dll in R2010a.Replace the reference to MWArray.dll with this new MWarray.dll in any project that uses a MATLAB Builder NE assembly, built in R2010a.If your application depends upon MWArray.dll being in the GAC (Global Assembly Cache), use gacutil to install it there.

Summary: Memory leak caused by creating and disposing Java MWArrays in a tight loop.
Description: As of R2009b, MWArrays are automatically cleaned up on shutdown. A memory leak has been found in the code that manages this clean-up. The leak eventually results in an inoperable system.
Workaround: There is no workaround for this issue. However, the attached patches will resolve the issue for releases R2009b and R2010a, respectively.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Local block output buffers might be overwritten
Description: In the generated code, a local block output might be overwritten prior to being read if all of the following conditions are true: The block is a Stateflow, Embedded MATLAB, or S-function block Code generation for the block is a TLC implementation, for example, inline S-function. The TLC implementation uses the BlockOutputSignal  function and supports expression folding. Configuration parameter Eliminate superfluous local (Expression folding)  is enabled on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.  ? ?
Workaround: To workaround this situation, perform the following steps:Download and unzip the attached file to a new folder.Add the new folder to the MATLAB path.To determine whether your model contains a modeling pattern that exposes the bug, run the detection script at the MATLAB command prompt:modelIsOK = findPossibleInvalidBufferReuse(model)where model is the name of your model and modelIsOK is false if an instance of the modeling pattern is found, otherwise it is true.If the detection script identifies the pattern in your model, run the following workaround script to fix the issue:status = Workaround_g616071(setting)To enable the bug fix, specify setting as true(1). To disable the bug fix, specify setting as false(0). The return value, status, is true if the workaround script is successful, otherwise it is false. Note: Only use the workaround script if the detection script identifies the pattern in your modelChange Log:March 8, 2010: G616071-R2007b.zip - first version March 18, 2010: G616071.zip - improved the detection script so it reports less false positives, removed R2007b from all MATLAB scriptsApril 19, 2010: G616071.zip - improved initialization to make the detection script more robust

Summary: PolySpace verification incorrectly reports a red COR check on function pointer call using an extern variable
Description: On the following example, Polyspace reports an incorrect red COR: typedef uint (*FUNCPTR)(uint, uint);typedef struct _mstruct{  FUNCPTR ptfunc;}MyStruct;unsigned int myfunc(unsigned int a, unsigned int b){assert (a == b);return (a);} extern MyStruct *ext_pt_struct;extern unsigned int vara, varb; int main(void){  MyStruct def_struct;  ext_pt_struct->ptfunc = myfunc;  def_struct = *ext_pt_struct;   return (int)def_struct.myfunc(vara,varb); // COR that should not be red }  The problem comes from the extern pointer to variable.
Workaround: Replacing the extern by a defined variable works around the problem and propagates alias to def_struct variable.

Summary: Repeating SIL simulation of AUTOSAR atomic software component with multiple runnables may result in invalid results
Description: Suppose you create a SIL block for an AUTOSAR atomic software component with multiple runnables. If you place the SIL block in a test harness model and simulate the model multiple times in a single MATLAB session, the results for the second and subsequent simulations may be invalid because inter-runnable variable data is not reset after the completion of a simulation.
Workaround: After each simulation of the test harness model, at the MATLAB command prompt, type clear mex .

Summary: Performance plot shows incorrect best performance when validation is not used
Description: When training a neural network without any validation data, the performance plot (plotperform ) shows incorrect values for best performance. If test data is used, the test performance is displayed for best performance. Training performance is what the performance plot should have highlighted. If neither test or validation data are used, best performance is displayed as NaN . Again, training performance is what should have been highlighted by the plot in this case.
Workaround: Edit plotperform.m, and change the following line from:bestPerf = tperf(bestEpoch+1);to:bestPerf = perf(bestEpoch+1);

Summary: Unable to change the data type of a signal in the Test Case Editor.
Description: While in the SystemTest Test Case Editor, an error dialog may appear when trying to change the 'Data Type' of a signal. The title of the dialog is "Error - Invalid Value" The text of the dialog is "Insufficient number of outputs from right hand side of equal sign to satisfy assignment." The Data Type of the Signal is not changed. The MathWorks strongly recommends that you upgrade to a newer release.
Workaround: As a workaround, please perform the following steps:Quit MATLAB?.Navigate to MATLABROOT\toolbox\systemtest\systemtest\+systest\@TestCase. MATLABROOT is the MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the MATLABROOT function at the MATLAB command line.For backup purposes, rename setDataType.m to setDataType.m.oldDownload attachment_615655_20110103.zip, extract the file setDataType.p, and place it in the directory from step 2.Start MATLAB.On the MATLAB command line, execute "rehash toolboxcache".

Summary: Subscripted assignment on a symbolic scalar NaN returns a wrong answer.
Description: Executing the following commands s=sym(NaN); s(1)=sym(7)  does not change s  to 7.
Workaround: Use a nonscalar array or assign directly to the variable s.

Summary: Model update (Ctrl+D) fails when the model contains an Embedded MATLAB Function block which uses bit operations such as bitconcat.
Description: This bug occurs under the following conditions: When using the default C compiler, lcc, that comes with MATLAB for Windows platforms. When running on the win32 platform or in 32-bit mode on the win64 platform. The model contains an Embedded MATLAB Function block which uses bit manipulation functions such as bitconcat.  Model update (Ctrl +D) generates a large C expression with shifts and casts and fails with an error message such as: ... compiler error in getreg--No more registers available "c:\programfiles\matlab\r2010a\sys\lcc\bin\lcc.exe" ...
Workaround: Install a different C compiler. A list of supported compilers, some of which are free, see: https://www.mathworks.com/support/compilers/current_release/. After installing the new compiler, use the "mex -setup" command to select it as the default compiler.

Summary: r = [s.f{a}] yields the wrong answer in some situations
Description: The following function should return [2 3], but it returns simply 2:   function r = g615362   eval('s.f = { 1 2 3 4 5 };');   s;   a =  logical([ 0 1 1 0 0]);   r = [s.f{a}];
Workaround: The problem can be solved by assigning to the indexee:   function r = g615362   s = [];   eval('s.f = { 1 2 3 4 5 };');   s;   a =  logical([ 0 1 1 0 0]);   r = [s.f{a}];

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Fixed-point operations involving constants whose data type cannot represent 0 or 1 might generate code with wrong answers.
Description: This bug might affect code or models that contain: Fixed-point data types that cannot represent 0  or types that cannot represent 1 . Constant values that are the closest values to 0  or 1  for these types.  This bug occurs when a mathematical operation involving a constant of a fixed-point data type is incorrectly optimized. For an operation OP  , such as +,-,* , let id_OP  represent the identity value for this operation. For example, for + , id_+ = 0 . The optimization tries to find expressions of the form X OP id_OP  and replace these with X . The bug occurs because the compiler incorrectly identifies some other constant k  as being equal to id_OP . This bug can occur when: id_OP  is not representable in the fixed-point data type. The constant k  is the closest representable value to id_OP .  In practice, the only values of id_OP  that can occur are 0  or 1 . Because MATLAB toolbox functions and Simulink blocks use mathematical operations such as +,-,*, you might encounter this bug even if your code does not directly call these operations. This bug affects: Code generated for simulation for Accelerator, Embedded MATLAB Function blocks, Stateflow, and for emlmex when used with Fixed-Point Toolbox Real-Time Workshop Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder

Summary: When estimating state-space models, the resulting singular values plot shows values corresponding to all possible orders between 1 and maximum specified value
Description: When you specify a range of orders for estimating a state-space model, the resulting singular value plot for choosing the best model order does not correspond strictly to the specified orders. Instead, the plot shows singular values corresponding to all possible orders between 1 and maximum specified value.  For example, when you use the n4sid  command with the order vector [3 4 7], as in:  order = [3 4 7]model = n4sid(data, order)  you get a singular value plot containing 7 bars for orders [1 2 3 4 5 6 7] instead of only 3 bars corresponding to orders 3, 4 and 7.
Workaround: To workaround this issue:1. Exit MATLAB.2. Download one of the following patches:Use R2008a.zip for MATLAB release R2008a.Use R2008bOrLater.zip for MATLAB releases R2008b or later.The patch contains the following files:<matlabroot>/toolbox/ident/ident/n4sid.m<matlabroot>/toolbox/ident/ident/n4sid_f.m<matlabroot>/toolbox/ident/idutils/idordch.mwhere <matlabroot> refers to the root directory where MATLAB is installed. You can determine the root directory of MATLAB by executing the matlabroot command in the MATLAB Command Window.3. Copy the files to their respective folders.4. Restart MATLAB.5. Run the following command at the MATLAB prompt:rehash toolboxcacheThere is no workaround available for releases R2007b or earlier.

Summary: Changes to block dialog parameters of the Magnitude-Angle to Complex, Complex to Magnitude-Angle, and Complex to Real-Imag blocks result in incorrect icon updates
Description: When the dialog parameters of the Magnitude-Angle to Complex, Complex to Magnitude-Angle or Complex to Real-Imag blocks are changed interactively, the block icon and the number of ports on the block do not update to reflect the values of the updated parameters. For example:  If the "Input:" mode on the Magnitude-Angle to Complex block dialog is modified from 'Magnitude-Angle' to 'Magnitude', then the edit box 'Angle:' does not appear on the dialog. Also, the number of input ports on the block do not change from two to one.  If the "Output:" mode on the Complex to Magnitude-Angle block dialog is modified from 'Angle' to 'Magnitude', the block icon does not update to display the string '|u|'.   If the "Output:" mode on the Complex to Real-Imag block dialog is modified from 'Real' to 'Imag', the block icon does not update to display the string 'Imag(u)'.
Workaround: You can work around this problematic icon/port behavior by updating (Ctrl-D) the model after making a change to the dialog parameters of any of the above-mentioned blocks.

Summary: Publishing MATLAB? code to PDF does not include specified image
Description: MATLAB code publishing supports the following syntax for including an image in your published output, where image.bmp  is a relative path from the output folder to your image: %% % <<image.bmp>> However, when you publish to PDF, MATLAB cannot find image.bmp .
Workaround: When you specify an output format of PDF, do one of the following:Specify the full path to the file. For example:%%% <<C:\Work\html\image.bmp>>Use the syntax noted in the Description. However, place image.bmp in your tempdir, and ensure that image.bmp is a relative path from your tempdir to your image. Your tempdir is the folder that MATLAB returns when you type tempdir in the Command Window.

Summary: Failure when linking code generated for model reference variants
Description: When generating code for a model hierarchy that utilizes model reference variants with preprocessor conditionals, the link of the top-level application might fail if there is a submodel referenced from multiple variants. The link error states that functions corresponding to that submodel are missing.

Summary: System functions a referenced model are ambiguous when $N is missing in symbol format string for Subsystem methods.
Description: Names such as 'Start' and 'Term' and 'bbio2' are used to name top-level system functions for a referenced model when $N is missing fromt he symbol format string. For these functions, the root component, is special cased to be 'mr_MyModel' for a referenced model named 'MyModel'. With the default rule of $N$R$F$M, we would have names such as 'mrMyModel_Start', 'mr_MyModel_Term' and 'mr_MyModel' for the start, terminate and output functions respectively. In the case of the top-level system functions only, $N and $R together contribute the root component. $R is required however doesn't have the desired effect in the absence of $N. ? When two models using the symbol format rule above are referenced by the same parent model, name conflicts are guaranteed to occur.
Workaround: Include $N in the symbol format string for Subsystem methods in any model that is expected to be referenced.

Summary: Using the "-I" option to include a path containing Japanese characters causes a compilation problem during PolySpace verification.
Description: When you use the option "-I " to include paths containing Japanese characters, these paths are not handled correctly by the PolySpace launcher, leading to a compilation issue. Even if the path is correct, headers are not found, and at compilation phase PolySpace stops with a message: "no such file or directory ". To confirm this problem, open the file "options_command.txt " in the results directory and observe that the paths are not set correctly by PolySpace Launcher.

Summary: Java IllegalStateException returned when you pause after typing function name and opening parenthesis
Description: MATLAB returns a Java IllegalStateException , instead of function hints, when all of the following are true: Function hints are enabled in Keyboard preferences (Select File > Preferences > Keyboard  to view the preference setting.) The tilde (~ ) is an input argument to function, function  . You type function   and an opening parenthesis in the Command Window or Editor, and then pause.

Summary: Function-call Model blocks do not log signals in all cases
Description: If a Model block references a function-call model, and from the Model block right-click context menu you select Log Referenced Signals  -> Log signals as specified by the referenced model , then Simulink does not log signals that are marked for logging in the referenced model.
Workaround: Do not select the Log signals as specified by the referenced model option. Instead, in the Log Referenced Signals dialog, choose individual signals that you want to be logged.

Summary: The Draw Shapes block and the ShapeInserter System object draw lines of incorrect length when you apply antialiasing with an image where the height and width are not equal.
Description: The Draw Shapes  block and the ShapeInserter  System object draw lines of incorrect length. This occurs only when you apply the antialiasing smoothing algorithm with an image where the height and width are not equal.
Workaround: For R2010a, follow these steps to correct this issue:1. Quit MATLAB?.2. Unzip the attached file, svipdrawshapes.zip, to the following location: $MATLABROOT/toolbox/vipblks/vipmex/3. Unzip the attached file, mvipdrawshapes.zip, to the following location: $MATLABROOT/toolbox/vipblks/viplibmex/4. Restart MATLAB?.If you are using a different version of MATLAB?, please contact MathWorks to obtain the replacement files.?

Summary: Outdated PDF version of User's Guide
Description: The matlabroot /help/pdf_doc/qualkits/iec/certkitiec_ug.pdf  file is an outdated version of the User's Guide. The User's Guide in the MATLAB Help Browser and on the web are current for R2010a.
Workaround: To view a current PDF of the User's Guide, open matlabroot/toolbox/qualkits/iec/certkitiec_ug.pdf.If you have write permission, remove the matlabroot/help/pdf_doc/qualkits/iec folder and its contents.

Summary: The documentation page for the "Defense Communications: US MIL-STD-188-110a Receiver" demo does not appear in the demos Help browser for the Communications Blockset.
Description: When you try to access the documentation page for the Communications Blockset "Defense Communications: US MIL-STD-188-110a Receiver" demo, the page does not display in the Help browser. The Help browser shows a "Warning - Documentation File Not Found" message. You can access the actual documentation page in one of the following ways: 1) At the MATLAB command line, type commmilstd188110a  to open the demo model, then click the "Info" block to see the documentation page. 2) In the Help browser, go to the demo documentation page by following this path in the left hand panel of the browser: Communications Blockset -> Demos -> Applications-Specific Examples -> Defense Communications: US MIL-STD-188-110a Receiver.
Workaround: Place the attached files in the following directory under your MATLAB installation: MATLABROOT/toolbox/commblks/commblksdemos/html/ Note: MATLABROOT is your MATLAB installation directory. To identify your MATLAB root, type matlabroot at the command line. There are two ways to do this: 1) Download the attached zip file, commmilstd188110a_doc.zip, into a writable directory. Extract the zipped files into directory mentioned above. 2) Download the attached html and png files (a total of 13 files) into the directory mentioned above.

This inability to create datasets with compression is caused by a bug in version 2.16 of the HDF-EOS library, which is included with MATLAB R2010a. You can still read and write existing datasets with compression using this version of the library.

Summary: HTML tags containing spaces do not function properly when used as help for a masked block
Description: To preserve spaces in the mask help text, Simulink replaces the spaces with "&nbsp;". As a result of this conversion, any spaces you use within an HTML tag also get replaced. That converstion breaks the functionality of the HTML code.
Workaround: Create a separate .html file with the required HTML code and use the MATLAB web command in the Mask help section, specifying that separate .html file.For example, if the separate .html file for the Mask help is my_html_mask_help.html, then in the Mask help section of the Mask Editor, just include a command such as:web(which('my_html_mask_help.html'))

Summary: Closing Simulink editor windows while the Log History dialog is open during save may lead to a crash
Description: When you interactively save a Simulink model whose 'UpdateHistory' property is set to 'UpdateHistoryWhenSave', the software displays a dialog that allows you to add a new comment to the history.  It is possible to close model windows while the log history dialog is open, by clicking their close boxes.  However, closing these windows results in a crash when you close the history dialog.
Workaround: Do not close model windows while the log history dialog is open.

Summary: Functions may error incorrectly on referencing matrix input
Description: The following functions were upgraded to accept referencing matrices as input, in addition to referencing vectors: areamat , filterm , findm , getseeds , gradientm , imbedm , limitm , los2 , ltln2val , mapprofile , maptrims , meshgrat , meshlsrm , meshm , neworig , resizem , setpostn , setltln , usamap , vec2mtx , viewshed , worldmap . In rare cases where the last row of the referencing matrix is a floating point approximation to a rational value (and with otherwise valid input), these functions may incorrectly generate an error indicating that the implied limits extend outside the valid range. (The valid range is [-90 90] for latitudes and a total span of 360 for longitudes.)
Workaround: To work around this problem, if you are using R2010a, you should replace the convertToGeoRasterRef.m function file in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/map/+internal/+map/convertToGeoRasterRef.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: convertToGeoRasterRef.m.oldCopy the version of convertToGeoRasterRef.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.Note: As of June 4, 2010, the attachment provides a more robust version of convertToGeoRasterRef.m, which you should use even if you have previously applied this workaround.To work around this problem, if you are using R2009b, you'll need the workaround provided with Bug Report 581492. If you apply that workaround now (or you've applied it since June 4, 2010) you will fix this problem as well and no further action will be needed.However, if you applied the workaround for Bug Report 581492 prior to June 4, 2010, you do not need to re-apply it, but you should replace matlabroot/toolbox/map/map/+internal/+map/convertToGeoRasterRef.m with version provided with this bug report, just as described above for R1010a users.

Summary: MISRA C++ rule 12-1-1 is not reported by the coding rules checker is in some cases
Description: MISRA C++ rule 12-1-1 says "An object's dynamic type shall not be used from the body of its constructor or destructor." Violations of this rule are not detected on class destructors, and on structure constructors and destructors.Example with class: class C2  {   public:    ~C2 ( )    {      typeid ( C2 ); // 12-1-1 should be violated here     C2::foo ( );      foo ( );      dynamic_cast< C2* > ( this );   } virtual void foo ( );   C2 ( )    {      typeid ( C2 ); // 12-1-1 is violated and detected here     C2::foo ( );     foo ( );     dynamic_cast< C2* > ( this );   }  };  Example with structure:  struct S2 {     ~S2 ( )    {       typeid ( S2 ); // Violation should be raised here       S2::foo ( );       foo ( );       dynamic_cast< S2* > ( this );    }    virtual void foo ( );    S2 ( )   {     typeid ( S2 ); // Violation should be raised here    S2::foo ( );     foo ( );     dynamic_cast< S2* > ( this );  } };

Summary: Unexpected run-time error "attempt to reenable function call subsystem" when using a Simulink function-call subsystem in Stateflow under certain circumstances
Description: Assume the following circumstances: A Stateflow chart is placed inside a conditional subsystem (such as an enabled subsystem). The "States when enabling" property of the Enable port is set to "reset". The Stateflow chart contains a Simulink function-call subsystem placed inside a state. The outer conditional subsystem is disabled and then re-enabled at a later time.  Under these circumstances, the Simulink function-call subsystem is re-enabled (without previously being disabled) when the outer conditional subsystem is re-enabled. This behavior is exhibited by the attached model mg612834.mdl
Workaround: Place the Simulink function call directly at the chart level rather than inside the state. Note that this might result in a change in behavior. When a Simulink function is placed inside a state, it is disabled and enabled whenever the state is exited and re-entered. However, a Simulink function placed at the chart level gets disabled and enabled whenever the chart itself gets disabled and enabled.

Summary: Incorrect error when multiple sample times exist at the destinations of a Unit Delay or Zero-Order Hold block that serves as a rate transition block
Description: Simulink incorrectly reports an error when the following conditions hold: A Unit Delay block or a Zero-Order Hold block is serving as a rate transition block. This rate transition block has multiple destinations. Those destinations do not all have the same sample time. If the model is using a variable-step solver, then all of the sample times on the destinations are continuous or fixed in minor step. If the model is using a fixed step solver, all destination sample times are continuous or the fastest discrete rate of the model.

Summary: Undetected periodic function-call run-time error
Description: A periodic function-call subsystem must be executed exactly once at each execution step after it has been enabled by its function-call initiator. The subsystem is designed to produce an error under either of the following two conditions: (1) when the subsystem is executed without being enabled, or (2) when the subsystem is either not executed, or executed more than once in a given execution step after it has been enabled.  However, if a model contains a periodic function-call subsystem that is called by an initiator that is itself inside a triggered function-call subsystem, then the error pertaining to the second condition above may not be detected.   An illustrative example is shown in the attached model 'periodic_fcncall_runtimechk.mdl'. The periodic function-call subsystem 'BBB' should be executed only once per time step. However, since the initiator 'FCI1' calls the triggered function-call subsystem 'AAA' 3 times per time step, and the initiator 'FCI@AAA' of the periodic function-call subsystem 'BBB' is inside 'AAA', eventually 'BBB' is called 3 times per time step. This erroneous condition is not detected.
Workaround: There are no workarounds for this issue

Summary: When running Image Processing Toolbox functions in a deployed MCR context on Mac OS X, functions that depend on shared libraries in the MCR bundle are not found on the shared library path.
Description: When calling imfilter  in a deployed context on Mac OS X, the following error occurs: ??? Invalid MEX-file '/deployMacCase/mac_deploy_test_mcr/toolbox/images/images/private/imfilter_mex.mexmaci': dlopen(/deployMacCase/mac_deploy_test_mcr/toolbox/images/images/private/imfilter_mex.mexmaci, 1): Library not loaded: @loader_path/libmwippl.dylib   Referenced from: /deployMacCase/mac_deploy_test_mcr/toolbox/images/images/private/imfilter_mex.mexmaci Reason: no suitable image found. Did find: /System/Library/Frameworks/JavaVM.framework/JavaVM/libmwippl.dylib: stat() failed with errno=20. Error in ==> imfilter at 346 Error in ==> mac_deploy_test at 8 MATLAB:invalidMEXFile   This bug is caused by a problem in the way MEX files are built on the Mac. This bug is only reproducible when running certain Image Processing Toolbox functions in a deployed context. You may also see similar variations of this error with the libraries libmwneighborhood.dylib and libmwiptutil.dylib. If you call Image Processing Toolbox functions from MATLAB, or execute a deployed application from within a MATLAB session, you will not encounter this bug.

Summary: Failure to detect rate transition in multirate, multitasking model containing subsystem with nonvirtual Inport blocks
Description: Simulink software might fail to detect a missing rate transition in a multirate, multitasking model if all of the following conditions exist: The model contains an atomic or enabled subsystem. The atomic or enabled subsystem has an Inport block that is nonvirtual , for one of the following reasons: The Inport block or one of its elements connects directly to an Outport block of the subsystem. The Inport block drives a memory layout block, such as Merge or Mux, that connects directly to an Outport block of the subsystem.   At the boundary of the subsystem, a sample time difference exists relative to the rate of the signal driving the subsystem. A difference can be introduced in any of the following ways: The sample time on the atomic subsystem is set to a different rate (Release 14 or later). Inside the subsystem, the sample time of the nonvirtual Inport block is set to a different rate. The subsystem Inport block has multiple source blocks with different sample times, so the Inport block inherits the sample time that is the greatest common divisor of all of its source block sample times.    Failure to detect the missing rate transition has the following potential consequences: The Multitask rate transition  diagnostic parameter, when set to warning  or error , should flag the missing rate transition, but does not. The Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer  solver option, when selected, should handle the missing rate transition, but does not.
Workaround: To work around this bug, navigate to the boundary of the atomic or enabled subsystem, and manually insert a Rate Transition block for any subsystem input that has a different sample time than the signal driving that input.

Summary: blaxxun Contact is not supported on 64-bit Windows
Description: The Supported Computer Platforms table in System Requirements lists blaxxun Contact as being supported on Windows x64. This is not correct; the only platform that supports blaxxun Contact is 32-bit Windows.

Summary: Changes to referenced configuration set of a referenced model can cause code generation to fail
Description: If you change a parameter in a referenced model's referenced configuration set, then code generation for the parent model may fail. For example, if you change the System target file  parameter in the configuration set, then generating code for a parent model fails, with the following error: ??? Error using ==> get_target_dir_name at 1357 Configuration component 'RTWSystemTargetFile' of model 'parent' and configuration component 'RTWSystemTargetFile' of model 'child' are not compatible. The error message returned by the comparison function is: The parameter setting for 'RTWSystemTargetFile' must be the same for all models in the model reference hierarchy  ?
Workaround: You can force Simulink to rebuild the SIM target for the referenced model. Delete the following directory:slprj\sim\modelName\where modelName is the name of the referenced model.?

Summary: The fmincon Optimization Toolbox solver errors when using the DerivativeCheck option with multiple nonlinear constraints and only one variable.
Description: fmincon  throws an error under the following circumstance: * The DerivativeCheck  option is 'on' . * There are multiple nonlinear constraints. * The problem is one-dimensional. * The relative difference between the user-supplied nonlinear constraint derivative and its finite-difference approximation is more than 1e-6. The error message is similar to the following, with the indices changed: ??? Attempted to access analytic_deriv(47,1); index out of bounds because size(analytic_deriv)=[1,100].Error in ==> graderr at 34 fprintf(' User-supplied derivative, %s: %g\n',gradfcnstr,full(analytic_deriv(i,j)))... ?

Summary: Outdated PDF version of User's Guide
Description: The matlabroot /help/pdf_doc/qualkits/do/qualkitdo_ug.pdf  file is an outdated version of the User's Guide. The User's Guide in the MATLAB Help Browser and on the web are current for R2010a.
Workaround: To view a current PDF of the User's Guide, open matlabroot/toolbox/qualkits/do/qualkitdo_ug.pdf.If you have write permission, remove the matlabroot/help/pdf_doc/qualkits/do folder and its contents.

Summary: Specifying Truth Table block "Local" scope data as any type other than double causes simulation error
Description: When a Truth Table block has "Local" scope data of a type other than double , model simulation can fail with a "class mismatch" error.

Summary: Publishing: Code output sometimes truncated when publishing to Microsoft? Word
Description: MATLAB? truncates output when publishing to Microsoft? Word if the code being published produces a lot of output to the Command Window.

Summary: Tooltip on pointer dereferences can be difficult to understand
Description: On the following example: 1 int *ptr;2 ptr = malloc(1000);3 ptr[10] = 0;  At line 3, PolySpace put a green 'NIP' check and an orange 'IDP' check. Moreover, PolySpace tooltips on 'ptr' displays following message: variable?'ptr'?(pointer?to?int?32,?size:?32?bits)pointer?may be nullpoints?to?4?bytes?at?offset?0?in?allocated?buffer?of?1000?bytespoints?to?dynamically?allocated?memoryassignment of dereference of expression (int32): 0  The message related to 'NIP' green check can be confusing. Note that even if 'ptr' is initialized, it might not be well allocated, i.e. malloc can return 'null'. Only if sufficient memory is allocated, 'ptr' points to 4 bytes at offset 0 in a buffer of 1000 bytes. Note that the assignment mentioned in the last line of the tooltip occurs only if both checks are green.

Summary: Report Generator may lose unsaved Microsoft Word documents
Description: When using Report Generator to generate a Word document, open instances of Microsoft Word (including those launched outside of the MATLAB environment) may close silently and lose any unsaved work.
Workaround: To correct this issue, apply the attached patch:1. Close MATLAB.2. From the MATLAB installation directory, navigate to toolbox\rptgen\rptgen.3. Rename docview.m to docview.m.bak.4. Copy the attached docview.m into the directory indicated above.5. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Failure to report illegal rate transition for virtual subsystem containing asynchronous function-call subsystem
Description: Simulink software fails to report an illegal rate transition for a model containing a virtual subsystem when all of the following conditions exist: The virtual subsystem contains an asynchronous function-call subsystem. The asynchronous function-call subsystem has multiple source and destination blocks that reside outside  the virtual subsystem. A Rate Transition block is correctly inserted for at least one of the source or destination blocks. At least one of the remaining source or destination blocks is not connected through a Rate Transition block.  Under these conditions, the software should report the absence of a required rate transition but does not.
Workaround: To work around this bug, perform one of the following actions:Convert the virtual subsystem to nonvirtual subsystem.Reconfigure the model such that the asynchronous function-call subsystem and its destination and source blocks reside at the same graphical system level.

Summary: MATLAB? may crash while parsing a valid floating-point number from a string of length greater than 294000.
Description: MATLAB? may crash while parsing a string of length greater than 294000 that contains a single valid floating-point number on the command prompt. This bug may also affect the function str2num . For example, the following code may cause a crash: >>eval(['1.',repmat('1',1,300000)]);  ? ?

Summary: Slow performance in regexpi.
Description: If you use the case-insensitive regexpi  function with a pattern that begins with a letter and a target string that does not contain the lowercase of that letter, you are likely to see a degradation in performance. The following pattern string begins with the letter q . The string to be parsed also contains that letter, but not in lowercase:    >> pat = 'quick';   >> str = 'The Quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog '; To demonstrate the performance issue, repeat the string 10,000 times:     >> s2 = repmat(str, 1, 10000); Time regexpi  as it looks for the word 'quick':     >> tic; regexpi(s2, 'quick'); toc   Elapsed time is 1.828308 seconds. Note that the performance is much better for a word starting with a letter that is present in lowercase:     >> tic; regexpi(s2, 'fox'); toc   Elapsed time is 0.026431 seconds.
Workaround: There are at least two ways to work around the performance issue:Replace calls such as regexpi(str, pat) with regexp(upper(str), upper(pat)). This can change the results of some of the string outputs of regexp, but works well if indices are expected:   >> str = 'The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog ';   >> s2 = repmat(str, 1, 10000);   >> tic; regexp(upper(s2), upper(pat)); toc   Elapsed time is 0.041048 seconds.Change the first letter in the pattern to be the set of characters containing the letter in both upper and lower case:    >> tic; regexpi(s2, '[Qq]uick'); toc   Elapsed time is 0.107632 seconds.

Summary: The ga function errors when I use the fitscalingtop fitness scaling function.
Description: The fitscalingtop  fitness scaling function returns a row vector, whereas the ga  function expects a column vector. This mismatch causes ga  to error.

Summary: Incorrect files created using OBJ2MFILE for device objects with non-default driver names
Description: In versions prior to R2010b, when you use MAKEMID to create a MATLAB Instrument Driver, and assign a name to the driver: makemid('ag6000','mydriver.mdd')  The MATLAB file created for the resulting device object is incorrect: d = icdevice('mydriver.mdd','GPIB1::0::INSTR'); obj2mfile(d,'myfile.m') type myfile.m . . . % If the object has not been created, create it otherwise use % the object that was found. if isempty(obj1) obj1 = icdevice('ag6000', 'GPIB1::0::INSTR'); else disconnect(obj1); obj1 = obj1(1); end . .
Workaround: Edit the generated MATLAB file.Locate the script which creates an instrument object using icdevice().Change the driver name to mdd file name.

Summary: Under rare circumstances, continuous-time Stateflow charts can result in a segmentation violation
Description: Assume the following rare circumstances: The Stateflow chart has its "Update method" set to "continuous". The Stateflow chart has the option "Enable zero-crossing detection" enabled. The model exhibits a lot of "chatter" such that there are a very large number of zero crossings (on the order of 100,000) before the simulation terminates. The check box for "Enable debugging/animation" is selected in the Simulation Target  pane of configuration parameters.  Under these circumstances, Stateflow simulation might crash with a segmentation violation.
Workaround: One workaround is to disable debugging in the Simulation Target pane. This means that Stateflow will not report run-time errors such as array out of bounds, etc.

Summary: Memory Copy block does not set initialization and termination values correctly.
Description: The code generated for the Memory Copy block may not set the initialization and the termination values correctly when the destination data type is of the integer type. This problem may occur due to an incorrect data type cast of the large integer values specified in the block mask.
Workaround: The attached patch is for the R2009b and R2010a releases.To install the patch:Download the attached files, patch_installer.m and patch_610112.zip.Start MATLAB and enter the following command at the prompt: patch_installer('patch_610112.zip', 'install')Follow the instructions that are displayed on the MATLAB command line.Reboot MATLAB.This patch adds or modifies the following file:toolbox/idelink/foundation/pjtgenerator/blks/tlc_c/memorycopy.tlc

Summary: Function maptrims trims extra rows and columns
Description: When the requested limits for trimming a regular data grid fall exactly on a cell boundary, the maptrims  function trims away the row and column containing that cell, even if the cell falls entirely within the requested limits. For example, if the first element of the latlim  input (the lower limit in latitude) equals 0 and there is a row of cells that extend northward starting at the equator, then that row will be trimmed away. In fact, given a regular grid Z  and referencing vector refvec , after executing the following code: [latlim, lonlim] = limitm(Z,refvec)[Ztrimmed,Rtrimmed] = maptrims(Z,refvec,latlim,lonlim); Ztrimmed  will have two fewer rows and two fewer columns than Z . The first and last rows and first and last columns of Z  will have been trimmed away.
Workaround: If you have R2009b or R2010a, you can work around this problem by replacing the maptrims.m function file in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/map/maptrims.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: maptrims.m.oldCopy the version of maptrims.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.As of July 16, 2010, the attached version of maptrims.m has been updated to address Bug 656307 as well as this one.A more minor problem with the maptrims function, Bug 586774, was fixed in R2010a, but if you are using R2009b, note that this version of maptrims.m fixes that bug also.

Summary: Using a variable as both a struct (or class) and a cell may cause MATLAB to crash.
Description: Using a variable as both a struct (or class) and a cell may cause MATLAB to crash. The use of the variable can appear on the left hand side or right hand side of an assignment. This particular example assigns a struct to y if x is zero, or a cell array to y otherwise:     function y = bug(x)   if x == 0      for k=1:10         y.var{k} = [];      end   else      y = y{x};   end
Workaround: Disable the JIT using "feature scopedAccelEnablement off" in the function that is crashing.

Summary: Function polyxpoly fails to detect some intersections
Description: For certain geometries involving nearly-vertical lines, function polyxpoly  fails to detect some intersections. In addition, it may report the same intersection more than once if the intersection falls on a vertex of one (or both) of the input curves.

Summary: LOADLIBRARY does not properly interpret enum definitions with a name starting in underscore.
Description: The following code generates a warning in MATLAB: /* File example.h */typedef enum _myEnum {    val1, val2 } myEnum;int myFun(myEnum var); MATLAB code: >>[nf,warns]=loadlibrary('example')Type 'myEnum' was not found.  Defaulting to type error.Found on line 6 of input from line 2 of file example.h >>libfunctions example -fullFunctions in library example:[int32, voidPtr] myFun(voidPtr) Because of the voidPointer  used in myFun , the function is not useable.
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds to this problem:Edit a copy of the header file changing the name of the enum.Generate a loader MATLAB file using the MFILENAME option to LOADLIBRARY and modify the input arguments to the affected function changing 'error' to the enum name.

Summary: The inputname function can incorrectly return the name of a structure field reference instead of an empty string.
Description: In the following program, the result of inputname(1) should be the empty string.  The inputname builtin only returns a non-empty string for plain variables.  The results of temporary expressions, e.g. struct field accesses are not valid inputs to inputname and should return an empty string.  See the help for inputname for more details. function x = viewBug  s.y = 1;  x = getInputName(s.y);function x = getInputName(n)  x = inputname(1);>> viewBug        % This should return the empty stringans =   s.y
Workaround: Add the following commmand to the function calling the function that uses inputname:     feature scopedAccelEnablement offHere is the function shown above, but with the work-around in place: function x = viewBug  feature scopedAccelEnablement off;  s.y = 1;  x = getInputName(s.y);function x = getInputName(n)  x = inputname(1);

Summary: Simulink might incorrectly derive input signal dimensions to Merge block, possibly causing data corruption or a Segv
Description: If your model contains a Merge block with the Allow unequal port widths  parameter enabled, then your model may be vulnerable to this bug. This rare bug typically occurs when the input to a Merge block is not connected, is driven by a ground block, or is involved in a loop where the signal dimensions could not be derived in a straight-forward manner. If one of these conditions applies to your model, perform the following steps to further assess if your model is vulnerable: Turn on the signal dimensions display by selecting Format --> Port/Signal Displays --> Signal Dimensions . Update the block diagram.  For each Merge block in question, perform the following analysis on each input port: Identify the signal width  for the output port of the Merge block. The signal width should be a single number and should already be displayed on the signal line for the output port. If the width is not displayed, then the width is 1. Identify the signal width  for each input port of the Merge block. The signal width should be a single number and should already be displayed on the signal line for the input port. If the width is not displayed, then the width is 1. Identify the offset  for each input port of the Merge block. The offset for the i th input port is the i th element of the parameter Input port offsets  on the block dialog. For each input port, check if input width  + offset  > output width . If this inequality is true for any one input port, then your model is subject to this bug.
Workaround: As a best practice, however, always disable the Allow unequal port widths parameter for the Merge block. (Consult the documentation for the Merge block on how to use a combination of Merge and Concatenate blocks and thereby avoid enabling the Allow unequal port widths parameter).If you must allow unequal port widths, then for each input port of the Merge block:Derive the correct signal width manually.Insert and configure a Signal Specification block at the input port of the Merge block to ensure the correct signal dimensions.

Summary: Model build fails with "Unable to build..." error when using Microsoft Visual Studio 2008 compiler without Microsoft testing tools installed
Description: Performing a model build for code generation or simulation might fail with an "Unable to build ..." error message if both of the following conditions exist: You are using the Microsoft Visual Studio 2008 compiler. The compiler was installed without installing the Microsoft testing tools.  The build script incorrectly relies on the presence of file MSTest.exe  to determine whether the compiler is correctly installed. Here is a sample instance of the complete error message: Unable to build a standalone executable to simulate the model 'test2' in rapid accelerator mode.This could be because the model has blocks that either do not support code generation or generate codeonly for specific hardware targets. Models with such blocks cannot be simulated in rapid accelerator mode. The rtwbuild error (message and identifier shown below) may identify these blocks. message: Real-Time Workshop builds with Microsoft Visual C on 64 bit windows require that the compiler be installed. At least one of these environment variables is expected to be set: VS90COMNTOOLS VS80COMNTOOLS VS90COMNTOOLS Please check your Microsoft Visual C installation and ensure the appropriate environment variable is set. identifier: 'RTW:buildProcess:missingMSVCEnvironment'
Workaround: To work around this bug, perform one of the following actions:Install the Microsoft testing tools when installing the Microsoft Visual Studio 2008 compiler.Create a dummy file called MSTest.exe and place it at relative location \Common7\IDE\ in your Visual Studio compiler installation tree. For example, if the compiler is installed at C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio 9.0\, place the file at C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio 9.0\Common7\IDE\.

Summary: Spectrum Scope and Vector Scope blocks display incomplete signals when grid is on
Description: If you are using a Spectrum Scope or Vector Scope block with the grid turned on, your signals may appear to be partially or completely missing from the scope window. The signals appear to be missing because they are partially or completely hidden behind the grid lines.
Workaround: To workaround this problem, turn off the grid. You can do so in one of the following ways:By opening the block dialog box and clearing the Show grid check box on the Display Properties tab.By opening the scope window and deselecting the Show Grid option on the Axes menu. By right-clicking inside the scope window and deselecting the Show Grid option on the context menu.?

Summary: Segmentation violation caused by automatic rate transition insertion for Zero-Order Hold or Unit Delay block with null destination or source
Description: Model simulation might halt with a segmentation violation when all of the following conditions exist: The model contains a Zero-Order Hold or Unit Delay block that has multiple destination blocks or multiple source blocks, with multiple rates present among the destination or source blocks. At least one of the destination or source ports for the Zero-Order Hold or Unit Delay block is unconnected or connected to a Terminator block. The model option Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer  is selected on the Solver  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.  Under these conditions, the software erroneously attempts to insert a Rate Transition block for the block with the null destination or source.

Summary: Embedded IDE Link build process errors out with Undefined function or variable...
Description: If you set the System target file  parameter manually to idelink_ert.tlc  or idelink_grt.tlc  in models that contain a Target Preferences block, and then generate code from the model, the software generates an error message similar to the following example: Undefined function or variable 'VDSPLinkTgtPkg.C2000' For example, the following steps produce this error message: Open a model that contains a Target Preferences block configured for an IDE and processor. In the model, select the Simulation > Configuration Parameters  menu. Select Real-Time Workshop  in the tree view on the left side. Set System target file  to ert.tlc , and click Apply . Without closing the Configuration Parameters dialog, set the System target file  to idelink_ert.tlc , and click Apply . Build the model to observe the error.
Workaround: To work around this bug:Cut the Target Preferences block from your model.Paste the Target Preferences block back into your model.Click Yes in the Initialize Configuration Parameters dialog box.You can now generate code from your model without producing an error.

Summary: Stateflow Snapshot images are upside down when image file format is Windows metafile and paper orientation is landscape
Description: Stateflow Snapshot images are upside down when you set Image file format  to Windows metafile  and Paper orientation  to Landscape .
Workaround: Use a different image file format or paper orientation, or apply one of the attached patch files (depending on what release you are using).For R2007b through R2010a:Stop MATLAB.Download attached takeSnapshot.p.Copy takeSnapshot.p to $matlabroot/toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/@rptgen_sf/@csf_obj_snap.p.Restart MATLAB.For R2007a:Stop MATLAB.Download attached takeSnapshot_7a.p.Rename takeSnapshot_7a.p to takeSnapshot.p.Copy takeSnapshot.p to $matlabroot/toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/@rptgen_sf/@csf_obj_snap.p.Restart MATLAB.?

Summary: Polyspace installation for Linux hangs with 100% CPU usage
Description: When installing or uninstalling Polyspace software on a Linux system, the installer stops responding, and creates a process that uses 100% of CPU capacity.  This process never terminates. The problem occurs when there is no display (the DISPLAY environment variable is empty). It also occurs when you abort an uninstallation. The problem does not occur on Windows systems.

Summary: ssc_build fails when path contains Japanese character with trailing backslash
Description: When you run ssc_build on a package that is in a directory whose name contains a Japanese character with a trailing backslash, the build fails.
Workaround: Rename the directory where the package resides, so that none of the characters in the directory name have a trailing backslash.

Summary: findpeaks fails to detect peaks with repeated values
Description: findpeaks  fails to detect peaks when the peak value is repeated in adjacent elements of the vector. For example: x = [1 2 3 3 2 1];  findpeaks(x)  returns an empty array, [], because of the repeated peak value 3. ? ?

Summary: Stateflow charts containing certain Data Store Memory matrix data accesses cause an assertion during simulation
Description: When a Stateflow chart contains an expression such as "x[i]", under the following conditions, Stateflow simulation fails with an assertion: "i" is a Data Store Memory variable. It is a scalar of type int32. "x" is a Data Store Memory variable. It is a vector of any type. Debugging/animation is turned off in the Configuration Parameters dialog box.  The assertion stack trace looks like below: failed assertion in .\classic\cg_node.cpp at line 687:0Stack Trace:[0] stateflow.dll:0x3023c61a(0x6c898a6c, 0x6c8a39dc ".\classic\cg_node.cpp", 687, 0)[1] stateflow.dll:0x30075de6(0, 0x6c8a39dc ".\classic\cg_node.cpp", 687, 0x6c898a6c)[2] cg_ir.dll:0x6c87471b(0x6c8a39dc ".\classic\cg_node.cpp", 687, 0x6c898a6c, 1)[3] cg_ir.dll:0x6c65ab48(0x9fd98e08, 0x9fd9f378, 1, 0x9fda0848)[4] cg_ir.dll:0x6c65463a(0x9fd9f378, 0x9fd9f348, 0x9fd05058, 0x00c2e764 "x??")
Workaround: Selecting the Enable debugging/animation check box on the Simulation Target pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box prevents this bug.

Summary: Incorrect coverage coloring for logic blocks
Description: When you collect and display condition coverage for a model that contains logic blocks, you might discover that some logic blocks are colored red despite achieving 100% coverage. Similarly, subsystems that contain logic blocks might be colored red despite achieving 100% coverage.
Workaround: Back up the following file in your MATLAB installation: <matlabroot>\toolbox\slvnv\simcoverage\private\install_informer_text.mReplace the install_informer_text.m file with the attached file.Restart MATLAB.Enter the following command at the MATLAB command prompt: rehash toolboxreset

Summary: Report Generator does not display title page correctly in .DOC reports without chapters or sections
Description: When using MATLAB Report Generator or Simulink Report Generator to create a Microsoft Word (.DOC) document that contains no chapters or sections, the title and other title page information may not appear correctly or at all.
Workaround: Include the entire report, except for the title page, in a Chapter/Subsection component, which is in the Formatting category in the Options pane of the Report Explorer.

Summary: The setInterpMethod method for Simulink.Timeseries objects generates an error
Description: Calling the Simulink.Timeseries  object method setInterpMethod  produces the following error: ??? Undefined function or method 'setInterpMethod' for input arguments of type 'SimTimeseries'.Error in ==> tsdata.timeseries.setInterpMethod at 5 this.TsValue = setInterpMethod(this.TsValue,varargin{:});Error in ==> C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009a\toolbox\simulink\simulink\@Simulink\@Timeseries\subsref.p>subsref at 27 ? ?
Workaround: Download the attached file, setInterpMethod.m.Replace the file setInterpMethod.m in the directory matlabroot/matlab/toolbox/matlab/timeseries/@tsdata/@timeseries with the downloaded file.You might need to restart MATLAB to use the updated file.

Summary: MATLAB Compiler may fail to include toolbox version information in a compiled application or library, causing the VER command to fail.
Description: VER  extracts the version information for a given toolbox from that toolbox's Contents.m  file. MATLAB Compiler assumes that an application is only interested in version information for toolboxes used by the application. MATLAB Compiler adds the Contents.m  file for each toolbox used by the application to the list of files shipped with the application. In some cases, MATLAB Compiler cannot detect that an application uses functions from a given toolbox and does not include that toolbox's Contents.m  in the application. If that application invokes VER , the query may fail because of the missing Contents.m  file. The symptoms of the error will vary, depending on the code that called VER . For example, creating a generalized Brownian motion object, by calling the GBM  constructor, produces an error because MATLAB Compiler cannot detect that the GBM  class originates in the Financial Toolbox.GBM = gbm(-0.0028837, 0.029568, 'Correlation', 1, 'StartState', 2.9304e+008) As part of its initialization, the GBM  object creates a DRIFT  object. The error occurs when the DRIFT  object attempts to determine the current version of the Financial Toolbox. ??? Error using ==> sscanfNot enough input arguments.Error in ==> drift.drift>drift.getCurrentVersion at 328Error in ==> drift.drift>drift.drift at 205Error in ==> sdeld.sdeld>sdeld.sdeld at 202Error in ==> cev.cev>cev.cev at 180Error in ==> gbm.gbm>gbm.gbm at 143
Workaround: Work around this problem using one of the following techniques:Use %#functionAdd a reference to a Financial Toolbox function to your application, using %#function. For example, add the following line to any MATLAB file included in your application:%#function annurateThe annurate function is a good candidate because its code is relatively simply, primarily calling functions in the MATLAB toolbox. Using annurate is unlikely to increase the size of your application or increase the time required by DEPFUN.Manually add Contents.m to ApplicationUse -a to explicitly add the Financial Toolbox's Contents.m file to the application:mcc -mv tgbm -a MATLABROOT/matlab/toolbox/finance/finance/Contents.m

Summary: MuPAD user interface fails to start and produces the error "Cannot start engine."
Description: The MuPAD notebook user interface displays an error dialog box "Cannot start engine", and MATLAB remains busy. Only hitting CTRL-C to interrupt MATLAB returns to the prompt.
