Workaround: To fix this issue on win32 in R2009a, R2009b, and R2010a:Start MATLAB that requires patching.Run the attached MATLAB function.When the script prompts for confirmation, press Y.Restart MATLAB.Alternatively, avoid the Kaspersky Anti Virus Tool version 6.

Summary: PolySpace verification stops during "C++ source normalization" with the message: "@FFT: Negative padding computed"
Description: While verifying C++ code, the verification stops with the following message in the log file: Number of dispatched calls: 0   ...   Max number of dispatched calls in virtual calls: none   Max number of dispatched calls on types: none ---------------------------------------------------------------------- @FFT: Negative padding computed : | padding = -4 | offset = 4 | after_field = 8 | offset_prec = 0 | size_prec = 8 |An internal consistency problem has been detected. |In order to avoid generating incomplete verification information, |the computation has been halted. |Please complete a Bug Report at The MathWorks Support site |https://www.mathworks.com/support. |Please remember to attach the log file from this analysis to the report. ----------------------------------------------------------------------  This problem can occur when alignment is forced inside C++ headers with "__attribute " or "__declspec ," depending on the value of the -dialect  option.

Summary: sys(end) returns incorrect result for multi-input, single-output LTI object sys
Description: The expression sys(end)  does not return the correct result for multi-input, single-output LTI models (tf , zpk , ss , or frd  models). For example, sys = ss([1 2 3])  sys(end)  returns d =   u1  y1 1Static gain.  instead of d =   u1  y1 3Static gain.
Workaround: Use sys(1,end) or sys(:,end) instead.

Summary: PIL does not correctly check error status of target connectivity API rtiostream
Description: During a PIL simulation, the software does not correctly check the error status of the target connectivity API rtiostream .The file pil_rtio_data_stream. c located in matlabroot \toolbox\rtw\targets\pil\c\  has, under the sub-function pilReadData , the following code at line 75 in R2008b, R2009a, R2009b and line 170 in  R2010a: if (sizeRecvd == RTIOSTREAM_ERROR) { This code is incorrect, and errors reported by rtiostream  are not propagated correctly to the PIL application.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the following line (either line 75 in R2008b, R2009a, R2009b or line 170 in R2010a),  if (sizeRecvd == RTIOSTREAM_ERROR) {with:  if (errorStatus == RTIOSTREAM_ERROR) {

Summary: The LCC and WATCOM compilers cannot compile code generated by the video.ImageFilter, video.Crosscorrelator2D, or video.Convolver2D System objects using the Embedded MATLAB? Function block in Simulink?.
Description: The following types of errors are produced when a model is simulated with code generated by one of these three System objects using the Embedded MATLAB? Function block in Simulink? and the LCC or WATCOM compilers: Error using the LCC compiler: lcc preprocessor error: .\mw_ipp.h:11 Could not find include file  Error using the WATCOM compiler: Open Watcom Linker Version 1.8 Portions Copyright (c) 1985-2002 Sybase, Inc. All Rights Reserved. Source code is available under the Sybase Open Watcom Public License. See http://www.openwatcom.org/ for details. loading object files searching libraries Error! E2028: _ippiFilter_32f_C1R@44 is an undefined reference Error! E2028: _ippiFilterGetBufSize_64f_C1R@16 is an undefined reference Error! E2028: _ippiFilter_64f_C1R@48 is an undefined reference .
Workaround: In order to work around this issue, please use a different compiler, such as the Microsoft? Visual Studio? compiler.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB functions may incorrectly create shared utilities, resulting in uncompilable code.
Description: If you enable shared utilities and use parameters in Embedded MATLAB code in Simulink or Stateflow, your model might generate uncompilable code. When this bug occurs, the C compiler reports an error about undeclared identifiers.

Summary: MATLAB? error message, The desktop configuration was not saved successfully, appears in Command Window
Description: The desktop configuration was not saved successfully  message appears repeatedly in the Command Window.
Workaround: This can be caused by a problem, such as lack of permission, when MATLAB attempts to write a file in the preferences directory. However, it can also be due to an internal error that occurs when MATLAB attempts to save certain desktop layouts. In this latter case, to prevent the problem from recurring, delete the desktop layout file:1. Issue the prefdir command to locate the preferences directory.2. Exit MATLAB.3. Delete the file named MATLABDesktop.xml from the preferences directory.4. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Model does not compile when array calibration parameter either feeds reusable subsystem or is LUT block parameter
Description: If a model contains a calibration parameter that is an array, and this array either feeds a reusable subsystem or is a parameter of a Look-Up Table (LUT) block, then the model will not compile. A typical compilation error message is: CalPrmArray.c: In function 'Runnable_untitled_Step':CalPrmArray.c:38: warning: passing argument 1 of 'CalPrmArray_Subsystem1' from incompatible pointer typeCalPrmArray.c:59: error: lvalue required as unary '&' operand
Workaround: If the the calibration parameter is an array that feeds a reusable subsystem, then a workaround is to make the signal between the calibration parameter and the reusable subsystem a test point signal.There is no known workaround if the calibration parameter is a parameter of a LUT block.

Summary: For very small files, isdicom will error
Description: When used on files of less than 4 bytes, the isdicom  function will error with this message: Error in ==> isdicom at 29 group = typecast(header(1:2), 'uint16');

Summary: Error when opening certain Excel files on Mac OS 10.6 (64-bit)
Description: In Mac OS 10.6 (64-bit), the Import Wizard throws an error when attempting to import .xlsx or certain .xls Excel spreadsheet files.
Workaround: If possible, save the file as an Excel 97-2003 .xls workbook before importing into MATLAB.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Real-Time Workshop might generate incorrect code for Width block
Description: When a Width block's output signal is of a non-Auto storage class, Real-Time Workshop generated code may not compute correct value for this signal.
Workaround: For the Width block output signal, specify the storage class as Auto.

Summary: Creating a figure in a UIControl callback sometimes causes segmentation violation on Linux
Description: A MATLAB segmentation violation can occur when a user-defined UIControl callback creates a figure. When this happens, a stack trace is generated with libmwhg.dylib:hoGetHandle near the top of the stack. MATLAB may continue to run or it may terminate. To date, this issue has only been reported on Linux platforms.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: For Math Function, Trigonometric Function, and Shift Arithmetic blocks with matrix inputs of different shapes
Description: Real-Time Workshop might generate incorrect code for a model which contains at least one of the following blocks: Math Function block with the Function  parameter set to hypot , mod , or rem  Trigonometric Function block, with the Function  parameter set to atan2  Shift Arithmetic block  The block inputs are also defined as follows: The first input to the block is a 1-by-N  matrix (a row vector). The second input to the block is a 1-D array of N  elements, or, in the case of the Shift Arithmetic, the Number of bits to shift right  parameter is a 1-D array of N  elements.  In addition, the block must have at least one input or output signal whose elements are not stored contiguously in memory. The most common ways to get noncontiguous signal elements would be to use the Selector/Assignment blocks or the For Each Subsystem to select an individual row of a matrix. Real-Time Workshop software uses column-major format to store matrix data, therefore, the elements of a row are not contiguous. With all of the above conditions, the generated code incorrectly indexes the block inputs, which causes the output to be numerically incorrect. There are two ways to detect the problem: Compare simulation results to code generation results. See Solution 1-3RX3AI  for detailed instructions. Inspect the generated code and the model to compare them.
Workaround: Specify both block inputs with the same shape. For example, if one input is a row vector, the other input should also be a row vector (and not a 1-D array). You can insert a Reshape block on one of the inputs to adjust it to the desired shape.

Summary: Calling objbounds on an empty image causes an error.
Description: Calling objbounds  on an empty image: h = image([],[],[])objbounds(h) results in the following error: There is no 'zdata' property in the 'image' class.Error in ==> objbounds ?

Summary: Printing Simulink Models to EPS format results in incorrect placement of the graphic on the page
Description: When printing a Simulink Model to EPS format, the EPS file bounds are not tight. This causes incorrect placement of the the EPS graphic when it is imported into other documents, such as Microsoft Word.
Workaround: A patch for R2009a is attached to this EBR.To install this patch:Place the attached file (render.p) in the folder: MWE_INSTALL/toolbox/matlab/graphics/private.Exit the MATLAB session.Ensure that the new file gets picked up during re-start.

Summary: int on trigonometric terms can be slow
Description: In some cases, computing simple integrals of trigonometric terms takes more time than expected. For example, try to execute the following commands: syms x y t;K1 = diff(sin((3*pi)/2 + (pi*x)/2)*sin((3*pi)/4 + (pi*x)/4),x);K2 = [sin((3*pi)/4 + (pi*y)/4)^2, sin((3*pi)/2 + (pi*y)/2)*sin((3*pi)/4 + (pi*y)/4),... sin((3*pi)/4 + (pi*y)/4)^2, sin((3*pi)/2 + (pi*y)/2)*sin((3*pi)/4 + (pi*y)/4)];K3 = sin((3*pi)/4 + (pi*y)/4)^2;K4 = diff(K1,x);tic;int(K1*K2*K3*K4,y);toc;
Workaround: Suppose, you integrate with respect to x, and n is an integer. Integration is faster if trigonometric terms appear only in the form sin(n*x). For an example, use a linear substitution to shorten the computing time for the above example:tic;f = subs(K1*K2*K3*K4, y,  4/pi * (t - 3/4 * pi));F = int(f, y);res = 4/sym(pi)*subs(F, t, 3/4*pi + pi/4*y);toc;

Summary: In R2010a, a script or function which contains a call to exit MATLAB on the same line as another command might not display the text emitted by that other command.
Description: If a script contains a call to exit MATLAB, and the exit  command is preceded by a command which displays text, and both commands are on the same line of the script file, the text might not appear when the script executes.  For example, a script contains the line:  disp('My Text'); exit  When run in R2010a, this script will exit MATLAB without displaying the text.
Workaround: Call pause(0) after the command which displays text. In the example above, change the script to read: disp('My Text'); pause(0); exit

Summary: MuPAD can return spurious solutions with one excessive degree of freedom.
Description: In rare cases, MuPAD returns spurious solutions with one excessive degree of freedom. For example, for the following system of equations solve([(2*x)/exp(x^2 + y^2) - (2*x*(x^2 + y^2))/exp(x^2 + y^2), (2*y)/exp(x^2 + y^2) - (2*y*(x^2 + y^2))/exp(x^2 + y^2)]) the solver returns a union of sets. Since one of the returned sets is an infinite set of the form Dom::ImageSet(matrix([[z], [z1]]), [z, z1], [C_, Dom::ImageSet(z^(1/2), z, C_)]) , this result incorrectly implies that you can choose any arbitrary x  and y  (independent of x ) from the right half-plane.
Workaround: Solve for only one variable. For example, the solutions of the following equations are correct:solve([(2*x)/exp(x^2 + y^2) - (2*x*(x^2 + y^2))/exp(x^2 + y^2), (2*y)/exp(x^2 + y^2) - (2*y*(x^2 + y^2))/exp(x^2 + y^2)], x)solve([(2*x)/exp(x^2 + y^2) - (2*x*(x^2 + y^2))/exp(x^2 + y^2), (2*y)/exp(x^2 + y^2) - (2*y*(x^2 + y^2))/exp(x^2 + y^2)], y)The solutions of these equations show the dependencies between x and y. Therefore, they do not introduce an excessive degree of freedom.

Summary: Data cursor cannot be repositioned when an axis has semiautomatic limits.
Description: Axes can have "semiautomatic" limits, in which one axis limit is set to inf  or -inf  and the other is finite. If you set an axes to use semiautomatic limits, as shown in the following example: plot(1:10);axis([0 inf 0 inf]);datacursormode on When you click on the line when data cursor mode is active, the data cursor will snap to a point you might not have selected. You cannot reposition the data tip, and if you add a new one, it snaps to the same location.

Summary: The Selection Linking tab of the Requirements > Settings dialog box is disabled on non Windows systems, but the path type check in Model Advisor instructs the user to change the setting.
Description: You might get the following message after running the Identify requirement links with path type inconsistent with preferences  check in Model Advisor: For the following requirements, the paths to the requirements use a different path type than the preferredtype. The preferred type is set to: Relative to the model directory. To resolve the inconsistency, do one ofthe following: To change the preferred type, in the model window select Tools > Requirements > Selection-based Link Settings and select Document file reference.  You cannot change this setting on a Linux machine because the Selection Linking  tab is not available.
Workaround: Use command line to adjust the setting. Firstly, get your current settings:>> linkSettings = vnv_settings_mgr('get', 'linkSettings')linkSettings =modelPathStorage: 'none'  docPathStorage: 'modelRelative'  twoWayLink: 0Then configure the link settings according to your needs. You may wish to match the model state so that theconsistency check will pass. The available options are:'absolute''modelRelative''pwdRelative''none'For example, if the requirements links in your Simulink model use absolute paths to documents, and this is how you prefer it, modify the setting:>> linkSettings.docPathStorage = 'absolute'linkSettings =modelPathStorage: 'none'  docPathStorage: 'absolute'  twoWayLink: 0Save your changes:>> vnv_settings_mgr('set', 'linkSettings', linkSettings)Confirm that the change went through: >> newSettings = vnv_settings_mgr('get', 'linkSettings') newSettings =modelPathStorage: 'none'  docPathStorage: 'absolute'  twoWayLink: 0 Rerun the Identify requirement links with path type inconsistent with preferences check in Model Advisor.

Summary: When using the GETHMMPROF or PFAMHMMREAD function to retrieve biological Hidden Markov Models from PFAM databases, for example the Sanger Institute or Janelia Farm (HHMI), they return an error indicating that the input file is not a valid PFAM.
Description: PFAM database upgraded to HMMER3/b formatted files in late 2009. The Bioinformatics Toolbox functions read only HMMER2.0 PFAM formatted files.
Workaround: Download the attached file.Quit MATLAB.Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\pfamhmmread.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\pfamhmmread.m.old, where $MATLAB$ is the directory where MATLAB is installed on your machine.Copy the downloaded file to the directory $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfoRestart MATLAB.Note: This workaround is only functional in versions R2009b (3.3) and later.?

Summary: Printing or generating reports on Simulink models can run out of memory
Description: Printing or generating a report on a Simulink model can run out of memory, depending on the amount of system memory available. This causes the print or report generation operation to fail.

Summary: mlint cannot open file when file specification has Japanese characters in the path
Description: If you pass a full file specification to the mlint  function and the path portion of that file specification contains Japanese characters, then mlint  returns the following error message: File cannot be opened for reading.  (This is the English version of the message.)
Workaround: Use the fileread function to read the contents of the file that you want to pass to mlint, and then pass the results of the fileread function to mlint using the '-text' option. For example:mlint(ˇ®-textˇŻ, fileread(ˇ®myFileNameˇŻ), ˇ®myFileNameˇŻ)

Summary: Serial devices do not communicate even when object's DTR status is on
Description: This issue occurs if the Serial DataTerminalReady (DTR) status is on by default but, is not asserting the pin when you open the serial port. To assert the pin when fopen  in called and DTR is on, type: s=serial('com1');s.DataTerminalReady='on';fopen(s);  To avoid asserting DTR, set DataTerminalReady value to 'off' before you open the port.
Workaround: To fix this issue:Download the attached patch file named G592438.zip. Unzip and save it to the MATLAB root directory.cd(matlabroot);unzip c:\tmp\G592438.zipRestart MATLABTo remove this file, delete it form the following location in the MATLAB root directory:java/patch/com/mathworks/toolbox/instrument/Serial.class

Summary: Embedded MATLAB Function blocks appear as Stateflow blocks in Simulink Report Generator reports
Description: The Simulink Report Generator reports Embedded MATLAB Function blocks as empty Stateflow blocks in generated reports.

Summary: Error when estimating parameters of a Simulink model which contains bus signals that are inputs to a block with internal states
Description: If your Simulink model contains bus signals that are inputs to a block with internal states, such as Unit Delay and Zero-Order Hold blocks, the following error occurs when estimating parameters: Unable to locate the block named "...".
Workaround: Modify your Simulink model to include a Bus to Vector block.

Summary: H5G.open does not work with three input arguments
Description: In R2009a, H5G.open  was extended to take an optional group access property as a third input argument. The presence of this argument triggers use of the 1.8.x HDF5 library version of H5Gopen  function as opposed to the 1.6.x version. In R2009b, this third input argument was unintentionally removed.
Workaround: Installation instructions?????? Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.?????? Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.?????? Exit MATLAB.?????? Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.?????? Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: ????????????? ???????????????????? Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.???????????????????? Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>????????????? ?????? ?????? Extract the files from the .zip file. ????????????? ???????????????????? Windows Systems:?????????????????????????? ????????????????????????????????? Open the .zip file.????????????????????????????????? Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.?????????????????????????? ???????????????????? ???????????????????? Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): ?????????????????????????? cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>???????????????????? ????????????? ?????? ?????? Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Change LogJuly 12, 2011:  attachment_587132_10a_2010-02-22.zip:  repackaged originalJuly 12, 2011:  attachment_587132_9b_2010-02-22.zip:  repackaged original

Summary: Axes tick positions do not set correctly through Edit Axes Ticks dialog for very small tick step values.
Description: When you open the Plot Tools for a figure and use the 'Edit Axes Ticks' dialog to set the 'Step by' value to 0.1e-9, the ticks do not occupy the entire range of the axis. They only go up to 3.9e-9 and then there is a gap between 3.9e-9 and 5e-9. The following code and steps show this issue: plot(10e-10*(1:5), 1:5); propedit % Click on the axes % Select the 'Ticks' button in the Property Editor % Select the 'Step by' option% Change the 'Step by' value to 0.1e-9 % Press 'Apply'  This issue occurs for all axes.

Summary: REMOVECONSTANTROWS neural network processing block fails in Simulink.
Description: If a neural network has the input or output processing function REMOVECONSTANTROWS, and it has been configured to remove constant data, then neural networks Simulink diagrams generated from the network will fail to simulate.
Workaround: The work around is to use an alternate function GENSIM2 (attached) instead of GENSIM. And to manually fix the resulting REMOVECONSTANTROWS Simulink block. The steps are: 1) Use the attached GENSIM2 instead of GENSIM to generate the network. 2) Fix the "removeconstantrows_reverse" block in the new network diagram as follows: - Double click the "Neural Network" block to open it. - Double click the "Process Output 1" block to open it. - Right click the "removeconstantrows_reverse" block and select "Look under mask". - Double click the "Selector" block to open its dialog. - Replace the bottom text field "Input port size" with the string "length(rearrange)+1". (Don't include the quotes.) - Click Ok. This should allow the network to simulate properly.

Summary: PolySpace installation fails on a UNIX OS with the error message: "Could not initialize class sun.awt.X11GraphicsEnvironment"
Description: This problem occurs on a UNIX OS when the DISPLAY variable is not set correctly. Specifically, when the the DISLAY variable is set, but the value does not point a valid display. The error message is: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: Could not initialize class sun.awt.X11GraphicsEnvironment   at java.lang.Class.forName0(Native Method)   at java.lang.Class.forName(Class.java:169)   at java.awt.GraphicsEnvironment.getLocalGraphicsEnvironment(GraphicsEnvironment.java:68)   at sun.font.FontManager.initSGEnv(FontManager.java:1113)   at sun.font.FontManager.findFont2D(FontManager.java:1784)   at java.awt.Font.getFont2D(Font.java:455)   at java.awt.Font.getFamily(Font.java:1144)   at java.awt.Font.getFamily_NoClientCode(Font.java:1118)   at java.awt.Font.getFamily(Font.java:1110)   at ZeroGqw.a(DashoA10*..)   at ZeroGqw.a(DashoA10*..)   at ZeroGqw.a(DashoA10*..) ...
Workaround: Unset the DISPLAY variable.

Summary: Repeated save and load of containers in V7.3 format exhibits a memory leak
Description: When you repeatedly save and load container arrays (structs, cell arrays, or objects) in the V7.3 MAT-file format, memory usage increases.

Summary: MATLAB will not start on Windows Vista or Windows 7 systems utilizing VPN or mobile broadband connections
Description: MATLAB will not start when there are certain PPP adaptors configured on Windows systems. Such adaptors include connections made via VPN (Virtual Private Network) or a mobile broadband device (Internet connection made using a 3G/GSM/CDMA/EDGE etc. network). MATLAB works fine when not using this type of connection.The problem occurs due to a bug in FlexNet version 11.6.1 which is used in MATLAB.  When MATLAB is started, FlexNet checks all possible system Host ID's, including the physical and virtual addresses for all Ethernet adaptors. In the case of certain virtual PPP adapters, the MAC address is returned as an empty string and the FlexNet software incorrectly handles this situation by looping indefinitely.  This loop prevents MATLAB from starting properly and manifests itself as a hung matlab.exe process.  When the bug occurs, the MATLAB splash screen may occur for a brief moment and then disappear promptly.
Workaround: The only workaround we have at this time is to close the VPN client connection or disconnect from the mobile broadband connection during MATLAB startup.  It may also be necessary to disconnect the mobile broadband device from the computer during MATLAB startup.

Summary: When using PolySpace Model-Link TL (for TargetLink), automatic stubbing of LUT (Lookup Tables) causes a compilation problem during the verification.
Description: When using PolySpace Model-Link TL, lookup tables are stubbed by PolySpace, meaning that they are replaced by a function modeling the LUT. However, generation of this stub function can fail, causing the prototype to be unknown to PolySpace. As a consequence, PolySpace verification stops during the compilation phase. Symptom: Verification stops with the following message: Stubbing standard library functions ...Stubbing unknown functions ...Fatal error: function 'Auto_Generated_Function_Name' has unknown prototype ?
Workaround: Note that the following workaround is only available for a R2009b release:Browse to the folder %POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\bin\x86-mingw32Create a backup copy of rte-kernel.cx86-mingw32.exeDownload the attached 581203_Windows_R2009b_1.zip fileExtract the exe file from zip archive and copy it to %POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\bin\x86-mingw32Browse to the folder %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\PolySpaceModelLinkCreate a backup copy of PolySpaceForTargetLink.mDownload the attached 581203_Windows_R2009b_2.zip fileExtract the MATLAB file file from zip archive and copy it to %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\PolySpaceModelLinkFor a previous release, manually write a stub for the LUT.?

Summary: Disabled links not correctly marked as edited/dirty
Description: When you change block dialog parameters of child blocks in disabled links, the links are not correctly marked as edited. As a result, the edited disabled links do not show up correctly in the Links Tool.

Summary: get_param of MaskValueString can return incorrect values with Japanese characters
Description: For a masked block, getting the MaskValueString  property can result in an incorrect value being returned if the values contain Japanese characters that contain the vertical-bar (|) character. This is a result of an ambiguity between the character used in a value versus it being used as a separator between mask values.
Workaround: Use get_param(block, 'MaskValues') instead of get_param(block, 'MaskValueString'). MaskValues returns a cell array, which does not need to use the vertical-bar (|) separator and hence avoids the ambiguity and returns a correct result

Summary: Incorrectly generating reusable functions for subsystems with wide function-call outputs leads to failure of code generation
Description: This document pertains to models that contain two or more instances of a single reusable subsystem that has non-scalar (wide) output function-call signals. For such instances to be reusable, the scalar elements of their function-call signal must be connected to identical networks of function-call subsystems. If instead these networks are slightly different in terms of the connectivity structure of their function-call subsystems, then the Real-Time Workshop code generated by the model may cause a compile-time error. An illustrative example is attached in model mbug_fcncall_reusable.mdl . In this model, subsystems 'Subsystem1' and 'Subsystem2' have identical contents consisting of the function-call initiators 'FCI1' and 'FCI2'. Furthermore, these subsystems are connected to very similar networks of function-call subsystems; in this case, all function-call subsystems ('A', 'B', 'C', 'D', 'E' and 'F') are identical to each other. Note that 'Subsystem1' and 'Subsystem2' should not generate the same reusable function because the network of function calls connected to them do not have the exact same structure. However, in Simulink 7.5 or earlier, the subsystems generate a single reusable function which results in a compile error when you generate Real-Time Workshop code for this model.
Workaround: The workaround for this issue is to set the affected subsystems such that they do not generate reusable code. Choose either 'Inline' or 'Function' for the 'Real-Time Workshop system code' parameter of these subsystems.

Summary: nnmf use of the TolFun tolerance is nonstandard.
Description: The nnmf  function takes a matrix A and tries to find two factors W and H so that the product W*H is approximately equal to A. It chooses the factors to minimize the function F defined as the root mean square of the elements of the difference A-W*H.  The TolFun  tolerance usually relates to the change in objective function value. (A tolerance is a threshold which, if crossed, stops the iterations of a solver.) For the nnmf  function, the objective function is F. However, unlike the usual criterion, nnmf  stops iterations when F decreases by a factor TolFun  from one iteration to the next. This can cause premature stopping.

Summary: Fixed-Point Toolbox? demos missing from the MATLAB? Help browser
Description: Some Fixed-Point Toolbox? demos failed to get published in R2010a, and cannot be viewed from the MATLAB? Help browser. When you try to view one of the affected demos, the Help browser displays an error message similar to the following:Warning - Documentation File Not FoundMATLAB cannot link to this file:.../matlab/toolbox/fixedpoint/fidemos/html/fi_datatype_override_demo.html   If the link is to a documentation file for a product that is not installed, you cannot view it in the Help browser. You can view documentation for that product at Documentation on the MathWorks Web site. Otherwise, the link might be invalid. Please report the problem at https://www.mathworks.com/support/service_requests/contact_support.do  This bug affects the following demos: Fixed-Point Data Type Override, Min/Max Logging, and Scaling Fixed-Point Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) Fixed-Point Lowpass Filtering Using Embedded MATLAB MEX Fixed-Point Arctangent Calculation Fixed-Point Sine and Cosine Calculation
Workaround: You can publish the demo file yourself. To do so, open the demo file in the MATLAB Editor and select File > Publish ... .?The following files in the \matlab\toolbox\fixedpoint\fidemos folder correspond to the demos that did not get published in R2010a:fi_datatype_override_demo.mfi_radix2fft_demo.memlmexbasicsdemo.mfixpt_atan2_demo.mfi_sin_cos_demo.m

Summary: Clicking annotations in Simulink can make them go blank on Windows XP 64bit.
Description: This is a sporadic Simulink interface problem, and it may not be reproduced easily. The problem may occur under the following conditions: working in 64-bit MATLAB under 64-bit Windows XP operating systems. performing changes to a large model with about 200,000 blocks or having a large number of variables in the base workspace (in the order of tens of thousands)   Under the above conditions, performing a series of steps/actions using the Simulink interface involving editing an annotation, signal, or block label in a Simulink model by clicking on it may lead the annotations to go blank.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, use the 32-bit MATLAB to perform the aforementioned activities.

Summary: Calling patch with a struct containing empty faces data when the renderer is OpenGL can cause a segmentation violation.
Description: Given the following faces/vertices struct  c : R = rand(10,10,10); c = isosurface (1:10,1:10,1:10,R,min(R(:))); c =     vertices: [1 9 7]       faces: [0x3 double] These statements: set(gcf,'renderer','opengl'); patch(c); generate this stack trace: [0] glren.dll:void __cdecl drawPatch(class glren::GLRenderEngine *,bool,struct GObject_tag *)(0x02c6ba80, 0, 0x1d7b2550, 0x1d7b2718) + 525 bytes [1] glren.dll:private: void __thiscall glren::GLRenderEngine::processObjectsRecursive(bool,struct GObject_tag *)(0, 0x1d7b2718, 0x5ed02e48, 0x1d7b2718) + 286 bytes [2] glren.dll:private: void __thiscall glren::GLRenderEngine::processObjects(struct GObject_tag *)(0x1d7b2701, 0, 1, 0x1d63db10) + 134 bytes [3] glren.dll:private: void __thiscall glren::GLRenderEngine::RenderSceneRecursive(struct GObject_tag *)(0x1d805a78, 0x1d5e6c60, 0x1d63db10, 0x1d63db10) + 283 bytes [4] glren.dll:private: void __thiscall glren::GLRenderEngine::RenderScene(struct GObject_tag *)(0x1d63db10, 0x1d5e6c60, 0x00c3b350 "h??", 0x78d95f78) + 306 bytes [5] glren.dll:public: virtual void __thiscall glren::GLRenderEngine::draw(struct GObject_tag *)(0x1d63db10, 0x00c3b368, 0x78ce42e3, 0x02c6ba84) + 13 bytes [6] hg.dll:void __cdecl RenderEngineDraw(struct RenderEngine_tag *,struct GObject_tag *)(0x02c6ba84, 0x1d63db10, 0x1d63db10, 0x1d5e6c60) + 24 bytes This is an issue only when the renderer is OpenGL. ?
Workaround: To work around this issue, check for an empty faces field before creating the patch. For example:R = rand(10,10,10); c = isosurface (1:10,1:10,1:10,R,min(R(:))); if( ~isempty(c.faces)) set(gcf,'renderer','opengl'); patch(c);end

Summary: Opening Plot Browser results in a Java exception
Description: This bug occurs as a result of threading issues. Consequently, it does not reproduce consistently. figure; plot(1:10); text(5.5,5,'$\frac{1}{2}$','Interpreter','latex','FontSize', 14)  Then click menu: View->Plot Browser .  When the bug occurs, the Command line outputs a Java exception message. For example: ??? Java exception occurred: java.lang.IllegalStateException: Attempting to replace component for Plot Browser at com.mathworks.mwswing.desk.Desktop.addClient(Desktop.java:481) at com.mathworks.mde.desk.MLDesktop.addClient(MLDesktop.java:833) at com.mathworks.mwswing.desk.Desktop.addClient(Desktop.java:445)   Error in ==> getplottool>createOrGetPlotBrowser at 81  dt.addClient (browser, 'Plot Browser', false, loc, false);   Error in ==> getplottool at 46  comp = createOrGetPlotBrowser (groupName, dt);   Error in ==> showplottool at 133  comp = getplottool (fig, compName);   Error in ==> plotbrowser at 16 compTmp = showplottool (varargin{:}, 'plotbrowser');   Error in ==> viewmenufcn at 41  plotbrowser (hfig, 'toggle')   ??? Error while evaluating uimenu Callback   ?

Summary: Opening a MATLAB or Simulink file outside of MATLAB does not invoke the MATLAB Editor.
Description: If you have multiple versions of MATLAB installed, and you've associated a file with a MATLAB version that is not the most recently installed, when you try to open the file in Windows Explorer (e.g. by double-clicking the file name) the version of MATLAB associated with the file will be invoked, but the MATLAB Editor will not. This can happen if the association is persistent (i.e. created through changing the file's properties), or temporary (i.e. opened using "Open With"). If this file was associated with the most recently installed version of MATLAB on the computer (which may not be the most recently released version), this same action will invoke the MATLAB Editor with the file loaded and ready to edit.
Workaround: Since the correct behavior occurs only when a file is associated with the most recently installed version of MATLAB, uninstall and then reinstall the MATLAB version that's manifesting this problem.

Summary: Certain objects with Dependent properties can display incorrectly in the Variable Editor
Description: If an object is declared with a "Dependent" properly, but no get  method is declared for that property, the Variable Editor does not display the object properly. Instead, all of the rows appear empty.
Workaround: If appropriate, define a get method for the Dependent property.

Summary: Code generation of Lookup Table blocks does not support tunable structure parameters.
Description: In R2010a, you can refer to the fields of a structure in Simulink blocks and the structure is preserved as a tunable parameter in the generated code. However, the generated code does not preserve the tunability of a structure parameter for Lookup Table blocks or any other blocks that take the address of a field of the structure.

Summary: Error starting 64-bit MATLAB for debugging MEX-files on Macintosh? OS X 10.5 Leopard or 10.6 Snow Leopard systems: "No Info.plist file in application bundle or no NSPrincipalClass in the Info.plist file, exiting"
Description: On Macintosh? OS X 10.5 Leopard or 10.6 Snow Leopard systems, typing matlab -Dgdb  from the command line or launching MATLAB with gdb from Xcode reports the following error:  "No Info.plist file in application bundle or no NSPrincipalClass in the Info.plist file, exiting" This issue affects 64-bit versions of MATLAB only.
Workaround: Start MATLAB first, then connect the debugger to the running process.If you debug using Xcode, select MATLAB from the Attach to Process item in the Run menu. If you debug using gdb from the command line, type gdb attach pid where pid is the process ID of the MATLAB executable. The process ID can be determined using the Activity Monitor application (found in Applications -> Utilities) or using the top command.Alternatively, specify the following, substituting your MATLAB release version:matlab -Dgdb(gdb) set start-with-shell 0 (gdb) set exec-argv0 /Applications/MATLAB_R2009b.app/bin/maci64/MATLAB.app/Contents/MacOS/On OS X 10.6 Snow Leopard, the following additional gdb command must be specified:(gdb) set exec-pathname /Applications/MATLAB_R2009b.app/bin/maci64/MATLAB.app/Contents/MacOS/MATLAB Then, start MATLAB:(gdb) run

Summary: Some out-of-memory condition might cause Real-Time Windows Target kernel and PC to stop responding and display a blue screen
Description: Under some out-of-memory conditions, the Real-Time Windows Target real-time kernel and PC might stop responding and display a blue screen. To recover from this issue, if the Real-Time Windows Target PC does not automatically reboot, reboot the Real-Time Windows Target PC.
Workaround: Suggested workaround:Most such out-of-memory conditions occur because the value of the Duration parameter in the Simulink window Tools > External Mode Control Panel is too large. Experimentally decrease the value of the Duration parameter and rebuild the model to find a more appropriate value.

Summary: Second output argument of the function mspeaks is a cell array when only one trace is passed in as the input.
Description: Second output argument of the function mspeaks  is a cell array (instead of a two-column matrix) when only one trace is passed in as the input. This behavior is inconsistent with the other output arguments.
Workaround: Download the attached file.Quit MATLAB.Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\mass_spec\mspeaks.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\mass_spec\mspeaks.m.old, where $MATLAB$ is the directory where MATLAB is installed on your machine.Copy the downloaded file to the directory $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\mass_specRestart MATLAB.

Summary: Adding setmcruserdata to a component compiled via deploytool generates incomplete function overloads.
Description: setmcruserdata  is a built-in function. When exporting this function via deploytool  the MATLAB Help needs to be added. This causes MATLAB Compiler to think the passed function is a script file and generates a (zero input, zero output) interface for the function instead of the documented signature for the built-in. This issue is applicable to all built-in commands that are exported via deploytool .
Workaround: Create an overloaded function in matlabroot/toolbox/compiler/patch directory with the same function name and the expected function signature.For example, in the case of setmcruserdata, create file setmcruserdata.m in the matlabroot/toolbox/compiler/patch directory:function setmcruserdata(key,value) % This overload is a pass through to the built-in function.% This primary purpose of this overload to define the% signature of the built-in function.builtin('setmcruserdata',key,value);Also, create the corresponding getmcruserdata.m file with the following contents in the same directory:function value = getmcruserdata(key) % This overload is a pass through to the built-in function.% This primary purpose of this overload to define the% signature of the built-in function.value = builtin('getmcruserdata',key);

Summary: Macintosh? platform: using MATLAB > Hide MATLAB disables some keys
Description: On Apple? Macintosh? platforms, after you select MATLAB > Hide MATLAB , or use the shortcut for it, Command+H , pressing certain keys has no effect.
Workaround: Click the Finder or in the Macintosh desktop, and then in a MATLAB? window, and the keys will work again.

Summary: Linux? platform: Printing text to Postscript? printer produces blank pages
Description: On Linux? platforms, printing text from the Editor or Command Window to a Postscript? printer may produce blank pages. This is due to a bug in how Linux software converts Postscript? data into PCL (Printer Control Language).
Workaround: There are two workarounds. One is to use a PostScript printer so that there is no translation of the Postscript data. If that is not possible, follow these steps to print the content:Select File > Print > Print to File, and then click Print to create a file.Open the newly created file in a text editor.Search for the string DeferredMediaSelection, and then delete the line that contains it. For example, delete the entire line that appears similar to: <</DeferredMediaSelection true /PageSize [612.0 792.0] /ImagingBBox null /ManualFeed false /NumCopies 1 >> setpagedevice.Save the file, and then print it using a Linux? printing program (for example, KGhostView if you're using KDE).

Summary: Macintosh? OS X 10.5 platforms: open dialog boxes can prevent access to other windows
Description: On Macintosh OS X 10.5 platforms, MATLAB? desktop windows and dialog boxes sometimes remain in front of other application windows, preventing access to the other windows.
Workaround: Restart MATLAB to resolve the problem.

Summary: Code generation might produce CGIR assertion caused by 'tidIdx < getNumTs()' under certain conditions
Description: Code generation for a model might fail if all of the following conditions exist: The model contains at least one subsystem that runs at an asynchronous rate and generates reusable code (subsystem A). Subsystem A contains at least one function-call subsystem that generates reusable code (subsystem B). There are multiple instances of subsystem B in the model, but not all instances reside in an instance of subsystem A. The first instance of subsystem B encountered during code generation does not reside in an instance of subsystem A, and has a different sample time than other instances of subsystem B.  Under these conditions, code generation fails and the following error is displayed in the MATLAB Command Window: CGIR assertion in .\rtwcg_utils\rtwcg_fcnconst.cpp(368): 'tidIdx < getNumTs()'
Workaround: To work around this bug, disallow subsystem reuse for function-call subsystems running at asynchronous rates. In the block parameters dialog box for each subsystem, set the Real-Time Workshop system code parameter to either Function or Inline.  Alternatively, download the detection scripts attached to this Bug Report.  The file fixForEBR417873.m should be used to initiate the detection script.  To see help on usage of the script, type help fixForEBR417873 at the Command Window. Note that both the p coded file and the MATLAB file are needed.

Summary: SimPowerSystems?does not?fully support the subsystem code reuse feature
Description: In your? application that needs to use multiple instances of the generated executable, you need to?check the Generate reusable code  option (ERT Target).?If? your model contains SimPowerSystems blocks, when you generate code for your model you might get the following error message:? The model can not generate reusable code because "<modelname>/powergui/EquivalentModel1/State-Space" does not support code reuse.

Summary: Incorrect results from function bufferm for polygons that cross the 180-degree meridian
Description: The bufferm  function computes polygonal buffer zones around vector polygon features. However, when an input polygon crosses the 180-degree meridian, bufferm  returns a buffer polygon that is invalid. Even very simple input geometries can yield output that has a clearly incorrect shape and that can contain self-intersections.
Workaround: If possible, apply a longitude shift or more general rotation to the original polygon to bring it within [-180 180] degrees in longitude, apply bufferm, then reverse the shift or rotation.

Summary: Trimming regular data grids for projection leaves null-data areas along edges
Description: When a data set that is regularly gridded in latitude-longitude is trimmed for display and projection by geoshow , data values along the edge of the grid are converted to NaN, which may result in one of the following conditions: The result looks fine when displayed in a projection in which the edges of the data set follow the edges of the map, but in a projection in which the edges wrap around to meet somewhere within the map, a gap may be visible. The final example of the demo "Creating Maps Using GEOSHOW" (mapexgeo ) shows this problem; depending on the figure size and position you may see a white line running along the Prime Meridian in the center of the map. The coordinates of an entire row or column of the data set, or of individual grid cells, may be close to the edge of the grid, but are incorrectly converted to NaN values. This results in a map display in which data within the map limits are not visible.

Summary: Low level file I/O can lose precison when reading u/int64 values larger than approximately 2^53
Description: The MATLAB fread  and fwrite  functions have supported reading and writing 64-bit integers beginning with Version 7.0.4. However, there have been instances in which the exact value of an imported integer is not accurately preserved. This only occurs when the integer being read is very large (approximately > 2^53). This occurs because the algorithms in certain cases rely on conversion to double precision. This precision cannot accurately represent integer values larger than a certain threshold.
Workaround: There are no known workarounds at this time.

Summary: MATLAB? Editor: Cannot perform redo operation after an automatic autowrap occurs
Description: In the MATLAB? Editor, when you type a long comment line, the comment automatically wraps when you reach the Comment formatting  Max. width  value (which is a preference setting). After an autowrap occurs, you can undo as usual, by pressing Ctrl+Z . However, if you then try to redo your changes by pressing Ctrl+Y , the Editor does not redo the changes you made after the point at which the autowrap occurred.
Workaround: Do one of the following:Disable autowrapping by selecting File > Preferences > Editor/Debugger > Language, clearing the Autowrap comments checkbox, and then clicking OK.Use the Redo and Undo toolbar buttons instead of the undo and redo keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+Z and Ctrl+Y).

Summary: Saving an unchanged model can cause changes in the MDL file
Description: Simply loading and saving a MDL file can cause the saved MDL file to contain many differences from the original, despite the fact that the user has made no changes. In particular: quotes can appear round the values of parameters in blocks to which no changes have been made DataRecord tags can change completely in models to which no changes have been made The "Position" parameter of visible systems and charts can change slightly Long strings may be split into multiple lines at different columns from before  This can make it difficult, when using a plain text comparison between versions of a MDL file, to see where changes were made in a model. ?
Workaround: To work around problem 1 (quotes appearing around parameter values), update the model by pressing Ctrl-D before saving it.Problem 2 (modified DataRecord tags) will occur in any model that contains a Signal Builder block. There is no workaround.There are no work-arounds for problems 3 & 4.

Summary: Problems with contours displayed by functions contourm, contourfm, contour3m, and geoshow
Description: There are multiple problems with the displays created by the contourm , contourfm , contour3m , and geoshow  functions, including the following: If you make a contour plot with any of these functions, tighten the map limits to trim some contours, and then restore the original map limits, the map may fail to display some of the areas covered by your data set. You may also receive an error from the clipdata  function. If you use function contourfm  with data grids that encircle the entire globe, some areas may remain unfilled. And, if you choose to display lines, some filled areas may be artificially divided along a meridian by spurious lines. Contour lines drawn by functions contourm  and contour3m  do not always extend to the edge of the area covered by the data grid, even when there are no null-data areas. All three contour functions and geoshow  (with 'DisplayType','contour' ) give invalid results when using the 'globe'  projection. Functions contourm  and contour3m  may tag contour lines with an incorrect level value. If you specify a list of levels in which the minimum value is larger than the minimum value of the data, the contourfm  function will not fill in areas falling below the lowest level specified. This behavior is asymmetric because even if the maximum level specified is less than the maximum value of the data, areas above that level are filled.
